PMC8777167.diagnosis: Phytophotodermatitis (plant-induced phototoxic dermatitis) of the right elbow with concurrent dicloxacillin morbilliform drug eruption
rationale_summary: Severe burning/stinging erythema began after gardening on a (cloudy) summer day, followed by edema and blistering with crusted erosions-typical of phototoxic plant exposure
Lesion is localized to a sun-exposed/contact area (right elbow) rather than spreading like cellulitis
Normal CRP and CBC argue against bacterial cellulitis despite marked erythema
New pruritic maculopapular trunk rash started after dicloxacillin, consistent with an antibiotic exanthem

No enamel/dentin (no hard-tissue) formation, supporting ameloblastic fibroma rather than fibro-odontoma/fibrodentinoma
No cytologic atypia or mitotic activity, arguing against ameloblastic fibrosarcoma

PMC7443985.diagnosis: Scleroderma renal crisis (SRC), likely precipitated by high-dose corticosteroids (with possible contribution from intravitreal bevacizumab)
rationale_summary: Progressive diffuse skin thickening with nailfold capillaroscopy suggestive of systemic sclerosis and biopsy concerning for morphea vs systemic sclerosis
Abrupt onset of severe hypertension with rising creatinine and new proteinuria after initiation of prednisone 60 mg daily
End-organ injury from hypertensive emergency (blurry vision with cotton-wool spots)
No renal artery stenosis on Doppler ultrasound, supporting an intrinsic SRC process rather than renovascular hypertension
Temporal association with intravitreal VEGF blockade raises alternative/overlapping consideration of VEGF-related TMA, but clinical context strongly supports SRC

PMC10999549.diagnosis: Gallbladder torsion (gallbladder volvulus) causing ischemic/hemorrhagic acute acalculous cholecystitis
rationale_summary: Previously healthy child with recurrent acute epigastric/RLQ pain, vomiting, positive Murphy sign, no jaundice, normal liver/biliary labs and no gallstones

Ultrasound: enlarged gallbladder with wall thickening, pericholecystic fluid/fat stranding; later absent wall vascular flow and cone-shaped structure at the neck with fundus displaced left and mobile with repositioning (suggesting a floating, twisted gallbladder)
CT: leftward deviation from gallbladder bed, poorly enhancing irregular wall (ischemia), pericholecystic fluid/fat stranding, and intraluminal hyperdensity compatible with hemorrhage/necrosis

PMC8378370.diagnosis: Bullous fixed drug eruption (vaccine-induced) due to recombinant zoster vaccine (Shingrix)
rationale_summary: Bullous eruption with central dusky areas appearing 2 days after the second Shingrix dose, without other new medications or topical exposures
Localized involvement of the vaccinated-side arm/axilla/lateral chest wall with mucosal sparing is typical for fixed drug eruption patterns
Infectious vesiculobullous causes were less likely given negative PCR for HSV-1/2 and VZV from a bulla

PMC11535749.diagnosis: SBP-101-associated toxic maculopathy (drug-induced retinal pigment epitheliopathy/outer retinopathy)
rationale_summary: Normal baseline eye exam/OCT before starting SBP-101, followed by bilateral vision loss beginning after the second monthly cycle

Progressive bilateral macular pigmentary change with punctate autofluorescence abnormalities, ellipsoid zone disruption, sub-RPE hyperreflective deposits, and subsequent RPE/outer retinal atrophy with choroidal thinning
Central visual field loss with preserved RNFL/optic nerve OCT, and ERG showing relatively preserved rod responses with cone dysfunction (reduced 30-Hz flicker)
Paraneoplastic autoimmune retinopathy less supported by normal anti-recoverin/anti-enolase testing and the macula-predominant toxic-appearing structural changes

PMC11821356.diagnosis: Adult-onset PFAPA syndrome (Periodic Fever, Aphthous stomatitis, Pharyngitis, and cervical Adenitis)
rationale_summary: Recurrent, predictable febrile attacks every 6-8 weeks lasting up to 7 days with complete wellness between episodes
Each episode includes pharyngitis with white exudate, sore throat/dysphagia, and tender cervical/submandibular lymphadenopathy
Marked inflammatory response during attacks (CRP 64 mg/L, serum amyloid A 140 mg/L) with negative infectious studies/cultures and no sustained response to antibiotics
Extensive malignancy/autoimmune evaluation unrevealing (reactive lymph node biopsy, normal bone marrow/cytogenetics, normal ANA, normal colonoscopy despite PET uptake)

PMC10622404.diagnosis: Severe rhabdomyolysis causing acute kidney injury with life-threatening hyperkalemia and metabolic acidosis
rationale_summary: Several days of progressive diffuse myalgia and inability to walk
Marked rhabdomyolysis markers: creatine phosphokinase >22,000 U/L and myoglobin >30,000 μg/L with very high AST and LDH
Acute renal failure on CKD: creatinine 6.58 mg/dL and BUN 304 mg/dL
Severe hyperkalemia (K 7.5 mmol/L) with metabolic acidosis (pH 7.26) and ECG changes (peaked T waves) with bradyarrhythmia requiring temporary pacing

PMC8544292.diagnosis: Oculomotor (cranial nerve III) palsy due to an oculomotor nerve schwannoma
rationale_summary: Progressive unilateral ptosis with ophthalmoplegia (limited adduction, elevation, and depression) localizes to CN III dysfunction
Dilated pupil on the affected side supports a structural/compressive third-nerve lesion rather than myasthenia gravis
Mild ocular pain and tearing with months-long progression fit a focal orbital/cavernous/nerve mass process despite fluctuating ptosis and a transiently positive ice-pack response

PMC5676292.diagnosis: Cerebral salt wasting syndrome (CSW) complicating encephalitis
rationale_summary: Acute severe hyponatremia (Na 112 mmol/L) with low serum osmolality (227 mOsm/kg) and inappropriately concentrated urine (urine osmolality 437 mOsm/kg) with very high urine sodium (129 mmol/L)
Marked polyuria with clinical hypovolemia/dehydration (weight loss, decreased skin turgor, tachycardia) favors renal salt loss over SIADH
Occurs in the setting of acute CNS disease (encephalopathy/seizures with abnormal EEG and suspected Mycoplasma-associated encephalitis)

PMC9675078.diagnosis: Epstein-Barr virus (infectious mononucleosis) presenting as acute dacryocystitis with lacrimal sac inflammatory abscess/periorbital cellulitis
rationale_summary: Unilateral epiphora with S-shaped medial eyelid swelling and tenderness over the lacrimal sac, progressing to a fluctuating nasolacrimal lump, supports acute dacryocystitis with sac collection on CT
Persistent fever and lack of improvement on topical and IV antibiotics with negative blood/eye cultures suggest a nonbacterial etiology

PMC3747369.diagnosis: Nevirapine-induced hypersensitivity reaction with severe drug-induced hepatitis (nevirapine hepatotoxicity)
rationale_summary: Symptoms began ~20 days after starting nevirapine (temporal link to new drug)
Diffuse maculopapular rash with fever, myalgias/arthralgias and pruritus
Marked jaundice/choluria and elevated AST/ALT, bilirubin, ALP, and GGT consistent with acute hepatitis/cholestatic injury
Infectious hepatitis workup negative (HAV IgM, HBV/HCV antibodies, CMV antigenemia)
Acute kidney injury concurrent with systemic reaction supports severe drug hypersensitivity/toxicity

PMC10216786.diagnosis: Acute hyperventilation-induced severe hypophosphatemia causing episodic tetraparesis (with rhabdomyolysis)
rationale_summary: Both episodes occurred with marked hyperventilation and hypocapnia/respiratory alkalosis
Severe hypophosphatemia documented during attacks (as low as 0.2 mmol/L) with no renal phosphate wasting on urinalysis
Neuromuscular exam showed tetraparesis with tetany/Trousseau's sign and normal reflexes; nerve conduction studies/myography and brain/spine MRI were normal
Alternative neuromuscular causes were excluded (negative myasthenia workup; hypokalemic periodic paralysis ruled out by exercise tests and gene analysis)
Second episode included very high CK consistent with rhabdomyolysis, a known complication of profound hypophosphatemia

PMC7325212.diagnosis: Distal hereditary motor neuropathy (length-dependent axonal motor polyneuropathy)

Lumbar MRI normal, arguing against radiculopathy as the primary explanation

PMC9610036.diagnosis: Primary central nervous system lymphoma (multifocal, with leptomeningeal/dural involvement)
rationale_summary: Elderly patient with rapidly enlarging, multifocal enhancing intracranial lesions (suprasellar, pineal, periatrial) and new progressive cranial neuropathy (III nerve palsy with later pupillary involvement)
Initial CT showed a hyperdense suprasellar lesion, a common imaging feature of CNS lymphoma
CSF twice showed lymphocytic pleocytosis, elevated protein, and elevated IgG index with negative infectious/inflammatory markers and no malignant cells (cytology can be negative in CNS lymphoma)
Interval development of focal dural thickening supports lymphomatous meningeal/dural involvement rather than acute ischemic stroke (MRI negative for acute stroke)

PMC3884960.diagnosis: Primary clear cell adenocarcinoma of the renal pelvis (Müllerian-type clear cell carcinoma)
rationale_summary: Renal pelvis tumor replacing pelvicalyceal lining with papillary/solid nodules and hydronephrosis; ureter uninvolved
Histology: complex papillae with clear cells, hobnail morphology, hyaline basement membrane-like cores, focal psammoma bodies, invasion into parenchyma/peripelvic fat
No conventional urothelial carcinoma (in situ or invasive) or other adenocarcinoma component identified

Immunoprofile supports Müllerian/renal epithelial differentiation (CK7+, HNF-1β+, PAX8+, focal CA-125+) and argues against urothelial carcinoma (uroplakin III−, p63−, CK20−) and clear cell RCC (RCC marker−, CD10−)

PMC8957291.diagnosis: Brainstem-predominant acute disseminated encephalomyelitis (post-inactivated SARS‑CoV‑2 vaccination rhombencephalitis)
rationale_summary: Subacute onset of quadriparesis, dysphagia/drooling, and bilateral facial paralysis about 1 month after vaccination, without preceding infection and with negative SARS‑CoV‑2 PCR
Upper motor neuron signs (extensor plantar responses) with preserved reflexes and no sensory level, localizing to brainstem/corticospinal tracts rather than peripheral neuropathy
Brain MRI shows multifocal inflammatory-appearing lesions in cerebral peduncle, pons, and medulla with faint enhancement; cervical spine MRI normal
CSF essentially noninfectious (2 WBCs, mildly elevated protein, no oligoclonal bands), supporting an acute immune-mediated demyelinating process

PMC4012653.diagnosis: Wernicke encephalopathy (thiamine deficiency precipitated by dextrose in severe malnutrition)
rationale_summary: Severe prolonged malnutrition with major weight loss (BMI 14.5) after lymphoma/chemotherapy
Received intravenous dextrose for hypoglycaemia, a known precipitant of symptomatic thiamine deficiency in malnourished patients
Acute neuropsychiatric deterioration with confusion, ataxia, agitation/visual hallucinations, progressing to drowsiness
Episodes of hypothermia with negative infectious work-up support a metabolic/nutritional encephalopathy rather than infection

PMC5470560.diagnosis: Myeloperoxidase (p-ANCA)-associated pauci-immune crescentic glomerulonephritis (ANCA-associated vasculitis, consistent with microscopic polyangiitis)

No improvement with empiric steroid therapy for NSAID interstitial nephritis; course progressed to dialysis

PMC10225816.diagnosis: Neurolymphomatosis (peripheral nerve infiltration by lymphoma) involving the right brachial plexus/median nerve and cervical roots
rationale_summary: Progressive painful asymmetric axonal neuropathy/plexopathy with severe atrophy and areflexia confined to right C5-T1 distributions; lower limbs normal
Marked focal/segmental nerve and plexus enlargement with loss of fascicular pattern and increased vascularity on ultrasound; MRI confirms massive swelling of median nerve, roots, and brachial plexus

PMC10843258.diagnosis: Androgen-secreting ovarian tumor (ovarian steroid/Leydig cell tumor)
rationale_summary: Markedly elevated total testosterone (5.35 nmol/L; 154 ng/dL) with virilization signs (deepened voice, clitoral enlargement) suggests an androgen-secreting tumor rather than PCOS
Basal 17-hydroxyprogesterone is elevated (18.9 nmol/L; 624 ng/dL), which can occur with steroidogenic ovarian tumors and may mimic nonclassic CAH
Pelvic ultrasound shows only a small left ovarian cyst, which can miss a small androgen-producing ovarian tumor

PMC7993351.diagnosis: Classic (nonexertional) heat stroke precipitated by anticholinergic medication-associated impaired sweating
rationale_summary: Found unresponsive in non-air-conditioned home during heatwave
Core temperature 42°C with hypotension and altered mental status
Warm, dry skin and dry mucous membranes consistent with anhidrosis/dehydration
Multiple strongly anticholinergic medications (amitriptyline, cyclobenzaprine, benztropine) increase risk of heat illness
No rigidity or hyperreflexia and normal creatine phosphokinase make neuroleptic malignant syndrome/serotonin syndrome less likely

PMC6601241.diagnosis: Cardiac (myocardial) metastasis from recurrent squamous cell carcinoma of the tongue causing pseudo-ST-elevation MI
rationale_summary: History of tongue squamous cell carcinoma with clinical/radiographic progression (worsening lung lesions, new right clavicular lesion, supraclavicular lymphadenopathy, weight loss) suggesting metastatic recurrence

ECG with inferior ST-segment elevation and anterolateral T-wave inversions with only mild troponin rise (peak 0.29 ng/L), compatible with non-coronary myocardial injury/infiltration rather than classic acute coronary occlusion
Concurrent chest/shoulder pain and right suprascapular swelling/erythema with new bony lesion supports widespread metastases with potential cardiac involvement mimicking STEMI

PMC3459730.diagnosis: Gallbladder volvulus (torsion) causing acute acalculous cholecystitis
rationale_summary: Elderly thin woman with sudden onset severe right upper quadrant pain and vomiting after initial mild symptoms
Imaging showed markedly distended, thick-walled gallbladder without gallstones
Repeat CT demonstrated ~180° rotation of the gallbladder with shift toward the midline and altered position of the cystic duct-findings characteristic of gallbladder torsion

PMC6548910.diagnosis: Sclerosing angiomatoid nodular transformation (SANT) of the spleen
rationale_summary: Incidental solitary, well-circumscribed 40‑mm splenic mass discovered during breast cancer staging
Imaging shows low enhancement in arterial/portal phases with slow centripetal delayed enhancement becoming isodense/isointense to spleen at ~10 minutes on CT/MRI
Lesion is isointense to spleen on both T1- and T2-weighted MRI and has no significant FDG uptake on PET-CT, supporting a benign vascular splenic lesion pattern consistent with SANT

PMC11294736.diagnosis: Sulfonylurea-induced hypoglycemia (surreptitious/unknowing ingestion via counterfeit "diazepam" pills)
rationale_summary: Recurrent severe hypoglycemia (15-44 mg/dL) requiring repeated IV dextrose
Inappropriately elevated insulin (47.4 mIU/mL) during hypoglycemia with elevated C-peptide (5.4 ng/mL) indicates endogenous insulin secretion rather than exogenous insulin
No history of diabetes or prescribed hypoglycemic medications; insulin antibodies normal, arguing against insulin autoimmune syndrome
History of taking unknown pills believed to be diazepam shortly before symptom onset suggests exposure to a hypoglycemic agent such as a sulfonylurea in counterfeit tablets

PMC11447760.diagnosis: Non-islet cell tumor hypoglycemia (IGF-2-mediated/paraneoplastic hypoglycemia) from metastatic myoepithelial carcinoma
rationale_summary: Recurrent fasting hypoglycemia with Whipple's triad and documented glucose ~30 mg/dL
Hypoglycemia labs show suppressed insulin and C-peptide, arguing against endogenous or exogenous insulin effect
Low β-hydroxybutyrate during hypoglycemia suggests insulin-like/IGF-mediated inhibition of ketogenesis
Very low IGF-1 with elevated IGF-2:IGF-1 molar ratio (10) supports IGF-2-driven non-islet cell tumor hypoglycemia in the setting of metastatic cancer
Normal renal function and adequate cortisol make renal failure or adrenal insufficiency unlikely contributors

PMC4689918.diagnosis: Embryonal rhabdomyosarcoma with prominent cytoplasmic lipid vacuolation (lipoblast-like/lipidized rhabdomyoblasts)
rationale_summary: Teen with large intramuscular thigh mass; initial FNA showed rhabdomyoblastic spindle/ovoid cells and was desmin+/myogenin+ with no lipogenic differentiation
FOXO1 rearrangement negative supports embryonal rather than alveolar rhabdomyosarcoma
Resection shows sheets of rhabdomyoblasts with eosinophilic cytoplasm and desmin positivity
Subset of tumor cells contain true lipid vacuoles (Oil Red O+, adipophilin+), PAS negative, producing lipoblast/adipocyte-like morphology
Vacuolated cells retain myogenic marker expression (desmin+) indicating rhabdomyosarcoma with lipidization rather than a primary adipocytic sarcoma

PMC7676809.diagnosis: Right distal ureterolithiasis (ureteral stone) mimicking appendicitis
rationale_summary: RLQ pain with normal WBC and CRP makes appendicitis less likely
Ultrasound shows hyperechoic circular focus with acoustic shadowing consistent with a calculus
The focus is within a hypoechoic, non-peristalsing, non-compressible tubular structure adjacent to the iliac vessels, fitting the distal ureter course
Lack of bowel wall layering and absence of mesenteric fat stranding, free fluid, or hyperemia argues against an inflamed intestinal/appendiceal structure

PMC8557694.diagnosis: Primary central nervous system lymphoma (B‑cell lymphoma, likely diffuse large B‑cell)
rationale_summary: Progressive subacute neurologic decline with deep white-matter lesions evolving to new contrast-enhancing corpus callosum lesions
CSF repeatedly showed atypical lymphoid cells and an abnormal mature B‑cell population despite normal cell count/protein
Negative infectious, oligoclonal band, and paraneoplastic testing with no systemic disease on PET and normal ophthalmologic exam supports a primary CNS process

Fluctuating MRI findings and nondiagnostic inflammatory-appearing biopsy after corticosteroids are compatible with steroid-masked CNS lymphoma and lack of response to broad immunotherapies (IVIG, PLEX, rituximab)

PMC7205540.diagnosis: Seronegative autoimmune hepatitis (possibly triggered by influenza vaccination) with associated immune-mediated cytopenias
rationale_summary: Recurrent marked hepatocellular injury (AST/ALT up to 1750/3110 U/L) with negative hepatitis A/B/C/E and CMV/EBV serologies and negative ANA/AMA, arguing against viral or classic seropositive autoimmune etiologies

Liver biopsies show portal inflammatory hepatitis evolving to bridging portal necrosis and developing fibrosis, compatible with autoimmune hepatitis spectrum despite absent autoantibodies/normal immunoglobulins (seronegative AIH)

Temporal onset after influenza vaccination and relapsing course supports an immune-mediated trigger; persistent leukocytopenia and thrombocytopenia with mild splenomegaly suggests associated immune cytopenias/hypersplenism rather than acute infection

PMC8123563.diagnosis: Metastatic medullary thyroid carcinoma presenting with diffuse (miliary) pulmonary metastases
rationale_summary: Progressive dyspnea, cough, intermittent fever/chills, and profound unintentional weight loss with no response to empiric antituberculosis therapy
Imaging shows diffuse bilateral 1-3 mm random nodules (miliary pattern) plus a confluent right lung mass, suggesting hematogenous metastases rather than infection
Tuberculosis workup negative (AFB smears/cultures and MTB PCR negative; blood cultures sterile)
Markedly elevated serum calcitonin (128 pg/mL), supporting medullary thyroid carcinoma as the source of metastatic disease

PMC7141725.diagnosis: Severe leptospirosis (Weil disease) presenting with pancreatitis, acute kidney injury, thrombocytopenia, and jaundice
rationale_summary: Conjunctival suffusion with scleral icterus and jaundice
Mixed hepatocellular-cholestatic liver injury with thrombocytopenia
Severe acute renal failure with abnormal urinalysis (hematuria/pyuria/casts) and anuria
Myalgias with elevated creatine kinase consistent with rhabdomyolysis, a known leptospirosis feature
Acute pancreatitis (epigastric pain, lipase >3× ULN) with negative evaluation for common causes, compatible with leptospirosis-associated pancreatitis
TTP/HUS and other infectious/autoimmune causes were investigated and not supported (no schistocytes, normal ADAMTS13, negative stool cultures and viral/autoimmune workup)

Plain radiographs show a large tubular gas-filled loop arising from the right iliac fossa and displaced toward the left hypochondrium with multiple air-fluid levels, supporting volvulus of a right-sided colon segment (cecum)

PMC8243071.diagnosis: Severe Chlamydia psittaci infection (psittacosis) causing severe atypical pneumonia/ARDS with encephalopathy
rationale_summary: Subacute febrile illness with dry cough progressing to severe hypoxemic respiratory failure with bilateral patchy infiltrates and pleural effusions
Prominent extrapulmonary manifestations (headache, memory loss, hearing loss, limb weakness, urinary incontinence) with largely unremarkable CSF studies
Systemic inflammatory response with elevated CRP/PCT and transaminitis, with negative routine bacterial cultures and broad viral/fungal/autoimmune testing

PMC10626292.diagnosis: Statin-associated immune-mediated necrotizing myopathy (anti-HMGCR IMNM), triggered after intravesical BCG
rationale_summary: Abrupt, progressive symmetric proximal limb weakness with normal sensation/reflexes and myopathic EMG
Marked rhabdomyolysis (CK >13,000 IU/L) with severe AKI and hyperkalemia
Muscle MRI with diffuse T2 hyperintensity consistent with inflammatory/necrotizing myopathy
Biopsy showing myopathic changes with mild lymphocytic inflammation and sarcolemmal MHC-I and C5b-C9 staining, supporting immune-mediated myopathy
Long-term rosuvastatin exposure with new onset shortly after BCG instillations suggests an immune trigger for statin-associated IMNM

PMC5412606.diagnosis: Typical AV nodal reentrant tachycardia (slow-fast AVNRT) triggered during fascicular VT ablation
rationale_summary: Abrupt transition from wide-complex ILVT to a regular narrow-complex tachycardia with 1:1 VA conduction
Relatively short VA interval with concentric atrial activation pattern is most consistent with AVNRT rather than atrial tachycardia or AVRT
Occurrence during septal RF delivery can provoke AV nodal reentry despite normal baseline AV nodal testing

PMC3287964.diagnosis: Q fever (Coxiella burnetii) presenting as granulomatous hepatitis with fibrin-ring granulomas
rationale_summary: Recurrent right upper quadrant pain with intermittent fevers and multiple hepatic lesions mimicking tumor/abscess with repeatedly negative blood/urine/stool cultures and negative fungal/mycobacterial/CMV stains
Histopathology progressed to extensive granulomatous hepatitis with occasional fibrin-ring granulomas, a classic pattern for Q fever hepatitis

Extensive infectious and autoimmune serologic evaluation (including viral hepatitis, HIV, TB, endemic fungi, amoebiasis, Bartonella) was negative, supporting Coxiella as a remaining key cause of fibrin-ring granulomatous hepatitis

PMC3483658.diagnosis: Transient neonatal myocardial ischemia due to coronary artery spasm (myocardial stunning)
rationale_summary: At day 6 developed acute heart failure/shock with severe metabolic acidosis and very high cardiac injury markers (hs-troponin T 1001 ng/L, CK-MB 37.4 mcg/L)
ECG showed ischemic-type changes (inferior Q waves, lateral repolarization abnormalities) and echocardiography showed regional wall-motion abnormalities (inferior hypokinesis, septal dyskinesis)
Coronary angiography demonstrated normal origin and anatomy of both coronary arteries, arguing against congenital coronary anomaly or fixed obstruction
Rapid clinical, ECG, biomarker, and echocardiographic recovery within hours is most consistent with transient ischemia/stunning from reversible coronary vasospasm

PMC11387283.diagnosis: Iatrogenic parasitic leiomyoma (disseminated leiomyoma implant) after laparoscopic myomectomy
rationale_summary: History of laparoscopic myomectomy with uterine leiomyoma fragmentation/removal, creating risk of implantation of leiomyoma tissue
New well-circumscribed pelvic mass without invasion or metastasis and with low FDG uptake (SUVmax 3.2), supporting a benign smooth-muscle tumor
Imaging shows a capsulated, vascular mass with heterogeneous signals compatible with leiomyoma/degenerative change
Tumor supplied by a pelvic vessel (second sigmoidal artery), consistent with a parasitic implant acquiring new blood supply

PMC11639004.diagnosis: Fat-containing hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) in a non-cirrhotic liver
rationale_summary: Liver nodule shows arterial-phase hyperenhancement with portal/delayed washout and capsule enhancement-classic for HCC
Diffusion-weighted hyperintensity with ADC restriction supports malignancy
Opposed-phase signal drop indicates intralesional fat, consistent with fat-containing (steatotic) HCC
Lesion is well-circumscribed 17 mm in segment VI; AFP and viral hepatitis serologies are normal/negative but HCC can occur without these risk factors

PMC1948010.diagnosis: Gollop-Wolfgang complex (tibial dysplasia/anterolateral tibial bowing associated with preaxial polydactyly)
rationale_summary: Right lower limb shows congenital anterolateral tibial bowing with cortical thinning and a medullary cyst on imaging
Ipsilateral duplicated hallux/duplicated first ray with a supernumerary digit arising from the first cuneiform (preaxial polydactyly)
Additional limb anomaly (radial deviation of the left index finger) supports a syndromic limb malformation pattern
No café-au-lait macules or neurofibromas and MRI shows no tibial pseudarthrosis, arguing against NF1-associated congenital pseudarthrosis as the primary diagnosis

PMC6192136.diagnosis: Genitourinary tuberculosis (tuberculous epididymo-orchitis with scrotal abscess)
rationale_summary: Recurrent unilateral epididymitis with swelling despite multiple antibacterial regimens and repeatedly negative gonorrhea/chlamydia NAAT
Imaging showed complex hydrocele and focal scrotal abscess with possible epididymal necrosis, suggesting chronic/granulomatous infection
CT demonstrated findings supportive of TB dissemination: right apical lung nodular scarring and an enlarged juxtaesophageal lymph node
Patient is from Cameroon, a TB-endemic region, and TB was explicitly considered in the differential

PMC12014310.diagnosis: Obstructive mechanical mitral prosthetic valve thrombosis
rationale_summary: Mechanical mitral valve with subtherapeutic INR (1.3) and prior prosthetic valve thrombosis history
Progressive dyspnea with elevated JVP and muffled mechanical closure click suggesting impaired prosthetic function
Echo shows restricted/immobile prosthetic leaflets with markedly elevated mean mitral gradient (~22-23 mm Hg) consistent with obstruction
TEE/CT demonstrate an echo-dense mass/thrombus (≤1.0 cm) attached to the mitral prosthesis

PMC10586915.diagnosis: Bioprosthetic aortic valve thrombosis with systemic embolization (occipital ischemic stroke)
rationale_summary: Transoesophageal echocardiography showed a 10×7.5 mm mobile mass attached to the bioprosthetic aortic valve with moderate stenosis and severe paravalvular regurgitation/leaflet degeneration
Acute left occipital infarct on diffusion-weighted MRI consistent with an embolic event
Five sets of blood cultures were negative and Coxiella/Bartonella serology was unremarkable, arguing against infective endocarditis
Cardiac FDG-PET showed no uptake at the bioprosthetic valve, further reducing likelihood of active prosthetic valve endocarditis

PMC3253064.diagnosis: Autonomously functioning thyroid nodule (toxic adenoma) causing persistent subclinical hyperthyroidism

PMC10602372.diagnosis: Iatrogenic acute severe aortic regurgitation from aortic valve cusp injury (non-coronary cusp flail/perforation) during PCI
rationale_summary: Aortic valve was normal on pre-PCI TTE, then acute dyspnoea and new early diastolic aortic murmur developed shortly after PCI
Repeat TTE/TEE showed new moderate-to-severe/severe AR with a mobile filamentous 17 mm lesion on the non-coronary cusp causing a coaptation defect and eccentric regurgitant jet
CT excluded aortic dissection and pulmonary embolism, and there was no clinical/laboratory evidence of infective endocarditis, supporting iatrogenic mechanical valve injury

rationale_summary: Progressive edema and pleural effusion refractory to diuretics with echo findings (septal bounce, marked respiratory variation of LV E-velocity) and catheterization showing equalized LV/RV end-diastolic pressures with dip-and-plateau and systolic area index 2.5 consistent with constrictive pericarditis
CT showing partial pericardial calcifications supports constrictive physiology
Markedly elevated serum IgG4 (1168 mg/dL) plus minor salivary gland biopsy with >10 IgG4+ cells/HPF and ~40% IgG4+/IgG ratio and sialadenitis indicate IgG4-related disease as the underlying cause
Infectious/autoimmune mimics are less supported (QuantiFERON-TB negative; ANA/anti-DNA/ANCA/anti-RNP-1 and CRP not elevated)

PMC4389205.diagnosis: Paradoxical cryptococcal immune reconstitution inflammatory syndrome (IRIS) presenting as abdominal (ileocecal/appendiceal) inflammatory mass with lymphadenopathy
rationale_summary: Recent treated cryptococcal meningitis followed by ART initiation 21 days later with very low baseline CD4 (5 cells/µL)
New inflammatory symptoms beginning 3 weeks after ART (abdominal pain, low-grade fevers) with evolving appendicular mass, ileal wall thickening, and para-aortic/adjacent lymphadenopathy
Repeated chest radiographs unremarkable and lymph-node biopsies showed no alternative pathology, supporting an inflammatory IRIS process rather than a new proven infection

PMC4637487.diagnosis: AL (light-chain) amyloidosis causing bilateral phrenic neuropathy with diaphragmatic weakness and hypercapnic respiratory failure
rationale_summary: Known IgG-kappa multiple myeloma with markedly elevated kappa free light chains (K/L ratio 2121)
Progressive dyspnea with restrictive spirometry and worsening hypercapnic respiratory failure
Nerve conduction study showed bilateral phrenic axonal neuropathy, explaining diaphragmatic weakness
Systemic features (weight loss, dysphagia, CKD with Bence Jones proteinuria) support light-chain-related systemic involvement

PMC5054479.diagnosis: Congenital insensitivity to pain with anhidrosis (CIPA; hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy type IV)
rationale_summary: Onset in infancy with recurrent fevers and absence of sweating (anhidrosis with impaired thermoregulation)
Severe self-mutilation beginning with tooth eruption (biting fingers/toes, tongue/skin injuries) consistent with pain insensitivity
Repeated unrecognized trauma causing multiple fractures, recurrent joint dislocations, chronic ulcers/osteomyelitis leading to amputations
Consanguineous parents supports autosomal recessive inherited neuropathy; normal uric acid argues against Lesch-Nyhan despite prior label

PMC11079415.diagnosis: Post-embolisation thrombosis of a residual vein of Galen aneurysmal malformation (thrombosed aneurysmal sac)
rationale_summary: Infant with confirmed mural-type vein of Galen aneurysmal malformation treated by transarterial Onyx embolisation with occlusion of the venous sac
One-month MRI shows thick peripheral mural enhancement with marked internal restricted diffusion within the residual sac, consistent with evolving/organizing thrombosis after embolisation
Persistent obstructive hydrocephalus from aqueductal obstruction by the midline lesion supports a residual thrombosed sac mass effect

PMC11481506.diagnosis: Severe dengue (dengue hemorrhagic fever) with plasma leakage presenting as acute abdomen (acalculous cholecystitis-like)
rationale_summary: Young adult with acute upper abdominal pain, positive Murphy's sign and CT showing gallbladder wall swelling/hyperdense gallbladder consistent with dengue-associated acalculous cholecystitis-like findings
Marked third-spacing on CT and intraoperatively (pleural, pericardial, and abdominal fluid; ~1000 mL ascites; bowel wall edema) indicating systemic plasma leakage
Minimal inflammatory markers (normal procalcitonin and CRP) and no surgical source on laparotomy (no perforation, cholangitis, or gallbladder enlargement) support a nonbacterial systemic cause
ECG abnormalities (ST depression, T-wave inversion, prolonged QT) can occur with dengue-associated cardiac involvement during severe disease

PMC3961933.diagnosis: Cemento-ossifying fibroma (ossifying fibroma) of the mandible
rationale_summary: Slow-growing, painless, hard mandibular swelling over 6 years with marked cortical expansion and facial asymmetry
Imaging shows a well-defined, expansile mixed radiolucent-radiopaque lesion with concentric/ball-like growth and "cotton-wool" calcifications extending across the mandible
Inferior border is bowed but not eroded, supporting a benign, well-circumscribed fibro-osseous neoplasm rather than fibrous dysplasia or Paget disease (labs normal)

Progressive asymmetric limb weakness with muscle wasting, cramps, and widespread fasciculations
Upper motor neuron signs present (brisk reflexes, Babinski, Hoffmann) with lower motor neuron signs (amyotrophy, fasciculations) and no sensory deficits
EMG shows active and chronic denervation in both upper and lower limbs without conduction block, supporting a diffuse motor neuron disorder
Thoracic slitlike syrinx is stable over years and brain/cervical MRI are normal, making the cavity unlikely to explain the progressive generalized UMN/LMN findings

PMC3934455.diagnosis: Generalized arterial calcification of infancy (idiopathic infantile arterial calcification)
rationale_summary: Progressive calcification on serial chest radiographs involving chest soft tissues and vessels of both arms
Severe neonatal cardiopulmonary disease with pulmonary hypertension and biventricular hypertrophy/heart failure
Clinical deterioration despite treatment for presumed viral cardiomyopathy, consistent with an underlying systemic arterial calcification disorder in infancy

PMC10499291.diagnosis: Gorham-Stout disease (massive idiopathic osteolysis) of the left mandible
rationale_summary: Slowly progressive, patchy-to-advancing osteolysis confined to the left mandibular condyle/angle/body over 18 months
No trauma history and extensive workup/imaging excluded inflammatory arthritis, malignancy (e.g., myeloma), eosinophilic granuloma, and other systemic causes
Whole-body PET scan showed no additional lytic lesions, supporting a localized idiopathic 'vanishing bone' process

PMC4722558.diagnosis: Bilateral bipartite medial malleolus (accessory ossification center/os subtibiale) mimicking an acute medial malleolar fracture
rationale_summary: Initial radiographs were read as a displaced medial malleolus fracture, but clinical course was unexpectedly benign with rapid pain/swelling reduction and preserved motion
Comparison radiograph of the uninjured left ankle showed a similarly shaped medial malleolar fragment, arguing against an acute unilateral fracture
Lack of ankle laxity/instability and improving function supported a variant anatomy rather than an acute fracture

PMC11825525.diagnosis: Disseminated (extrapulmonary) tuberculosis reactivation associated with adalimumab
rationale_summary: On TNF-α inhibitor (adalimumab), a major risk factor for TB reactivation despite initially negative IGRA and normal CXR
Systemic symptoms (weight loss, night sweats), fever, and progressive pulmonary infiltrates
PET-CT: hypermetabolic splenic and hepatic lesions with pulmonary nodules and hilar lymphadenopathy, compatible with disseminated infection
Splenic and liver biopsies: granulomatous inflammation (non-necrotizing granulomas can occur in TB, especially with immunosuppression)
Repeat T-SPOT.TB became positive during evaluation
Apparent steroid responsiveness with relapse when tapered is compatible with partially masked underlying TB rather than true sarcoidosis

PMC8895532.diagnosis: Right-sided congenital diaphragmatic hernia with intrathoracic herniation of the stomach (associated markedly shortened esophagus)
rationale_summary: Imaging showed a cystic structure occupying the right thorax with absent gastric bubble in the abdomen and no intestinal filling
Fetal MRI and 3D ultrasound demonstrated a diaphragmatic defect (middle/posterior) with herniation of abdominal organs into the chest
3D ultrasound showed the distal esophagus directly connected to the cystic mass, confirming the cystic mass as an intrathoracic stomach and a markedly shortened esophagus
Associated findings (right lung hypoplasia, small pleural effusion) support significant thoracic organ herniation

PMC3892129.diagnosis: Acute bacterial meningitis secondary to an invasive giant prolactin-secreting pituitary macroadenoma (giant prolactinoma) eroding the sellar floor into the sphenoid sinus (occult CSF fistula)
rationale_summary: 6-day severe headache/vomiting with fever, markedly elevated CRP and leukocytosis
Lumbar puncture shows purulent CSF with very high WBC count, high protein, low glucose, high lactate-consistent with bacterial meningitis
Imaging shows a large invasive sellar/suprasellar mass eroding the sellar floor and extending into the sphenoid sinus/cavernous sinuses, providing a potential route for infection
Extremely elevated prolactin (9000 ng/mL) with panhypopituitarism supports a giant prolactinoma as the underlying lesion

PMC11333128.diagnosis: Acute non-rheumatic streptococcal myocarditis (myopericarditis) following group A streptococcal pharyngitis
rationale_summary: 24-year-old with recent group A streptococcal pharyngitis developed acute central chest pain within 1 day
Elevated troponin T indicates myocardial injury
ECG with T-wave inversions and ST-segment depressions but no evidence of acute coronary syndrome on serial evaluation
Transthoracic echocardiogram essentially normal, supporting myocarditis/myopericarditis rather than structural heart disease or ACS

PMC7986947.diagnosis: Metastatic left upper-lobe lung carcinoma (non-small cell) with extensive osseous metastases causing thoracic spinal cord compression
rationale_summary: CT chest shows a 75 mm lobulated left upper-lobe mass invading chest wall/3rd rib with pleural effusion
MRI spine and CT demonstrate widespread vertebral and pelvic bony lesions with large thoracic paraspinal deposits, including T4/T5 lesion causing significant cord compression
Symptoms (pleuritic chest pain, progressive back pain, constipation, leg weakness/numbness) fit lung primary with metastatic bone disease and spinal cord compression

PMC6158818.diagnosis: Intramedullary osteosclerosis of the right femur
rationale_summary: Young woman with long-standing localized diaphyseal thigh pain and normal biochemical bone markers

Imaging (MRI/PET-CT) shows marked cortical thickening with partial medullary obliteration and irregular sclerosis confined to the femoral diaphysis; Tc-99m scan shows increased uptake along most of the femoral shaft
Biopsy shows thickened lamellar trabeculae with accentuated cement lines and no fibrosis, inflammation, or neoplasia
Genetic testing negative for GNAS1 (argues against fibrous dysplasia) and SQSTM1 (argues against Paget disease)

PMC8367851.diagnosis: Aseptic abscess syndrome (systemic neutrophilic disease with extracutaneous pyoderma gangrenosum involvement)
rationale_summary: Long history of biopsy-proven neutrophilic dermatosis clinically consistent with pyoderma gangrenosum, responsive to immunosuppression
New cavitary lung lesions, splenic abscesses, and pericardial effusion with transbronchial/wedge pathology showing abscesses but no organisms on stains/cultures/molecular studies
Clinical deterioration after stopping immunosuppression despite broad-spectrum antibacterial and antifungal therapy supports sterile neutrophilic abscesses rather than infection
ANCA negative, arguing against ANCA-associated vasculitis as a cause of cavitary pulmonary disease

PMC6544423.diagnosis: Bilateral subclavian artery critical stenosis/occlusion causing coronary-subclavian steal from bilateral internal mammary artery grafts (coronary subclavian steal syndrome) leading to myocardial ischemia and acute pulmonary edema
rationale_summary: History of CABG with LIMA and RIMA grafts supplying coronary circulation
Previously documented 50% stenosis of both subclavian arteries, progressing over time
Progressive ischemic ECG changes, reduced ejection fraction, positive troponin/CK rise consistent with myocardial ischemia/infarction
Markedly low or unmeasurable bilateral arm blood pressures with easily palpable femoral pulses suggests proximal upper-extremity arterial obstruction rather than systemic hypotension
Acute severe pulmonary edema and shock-compatible presentation consistent with ischemia from compromised mammary graft inflow due to subclavian disease

PMC11282747.diagnosis: Scrub typhus (Orientia tsutsugamushi infection) with septic shock and intracerebral hemorrhage
rationale_summary: Acute febrile illness with headache, vomiting, cough/shortness of breath, shock (BP 80/50), hypoxemia, and bilateral pulmonary infiltrates/edema
Characteristic eschar described as a brownish-black scab on the buttock

Multiorgan involvement consistent with scrub typhus: AKI (Cr 1.7), jaundice/cholestatic hepatitis (bilirubin 6.2, elevated AST/ALT/ALP), pleural effusion, borderline splenomegaly, gallbladder wall thickening
Alternative tropical infections were tested and reported negative (dengue, leptospirosis, malaria, brucella)
Acute right frontal intraparenchymal hemorrhage in the setting of severe systemic infection suggests scrub typhus-related vasculitis/encephalopathy complication

PMC10551103.diagnosis: Areca nut (betel nut) poisoning causing acute intravascular hemolysis with hemoglobinuria and severe hepatocellular injury
rationale_summary: Abrupt clinical deterioration (pallor, cold extremities, hypoxia, seizures) occurred immediately after ingestion of two tablespoons of areca nut powder with ibuprofen
Gross hematuria with urinalysis 4+ blood and only 2+ albumin is compatible with pigment (hemoglobin)uria from intravascular hemolysis
Markedly elevated LDH (3570 U/L) with negative direct/indirect Coombs tests supports non-immune hemolysis
Severe hepatocellular injury (AST 2670 U/L, ALT 970 U/L) with coagulopathy (INR 1.76) and negative viral studies supports toxic hepatitis rather than infectious hepatitis
Renal function was initially normal (BUN 14 mg/dL, creatinine 0.42 mg/dL), arguing against primary renal disease as the initial cause of hematuria

PMC8577092.diagnosis: Cerebral salt wasting syndrome (CSW) after intracerebral hemorrhage
rationale_summary: Recent intracerebral hemorrhage followed by several days of marked polyuria and rapid weight loss (71→65 kg) with clinical hypovolemia (dry mucosa, skin-fold dehydration, collapsible IVC) and shock responsive to saline

Normal chest radiograph, preserved LVEF, no tamponade, and normal inflammatory markers argue against cardiogenic or septic shock as the primary cause

PMC6241168.diagnosis: Ticagrelor-induced central sleep apnea (Cheyne-Stokes respiration) with vagally mediated bradyarrhythmia/complete atrioventricular block
rationale_summary: Cheyne-Stokes respiration appeared a few hours after receiving a ticagrelor loading dose post-PCI, without overt heart failure and with preserved LVEF (50%).

Telemetry showed cyclical sinus tachycardia during apneas and profound sinus bradycardia with transient complete AV block/pauses up to 5 seconds during hyperpnea, consistent with respiration-linked central apnea-associated conduction disturbances.
Evaluation for metabolic/endocrine causes of bradyarrhythmia (electrolytes, renal/hepatic function, thyroid) was unrevealing, supporting a drug-related etiology.

PMC9201294.diagnosis: Spontaneous uterine rupture due to placenta percreta (morbidly adherent low-lying placenta) at 17 weeks' gestation
rationale_summary: Sudden severe abdominal pain with rigid abdomen and rebound tenderness, with a tender mass connected to the uterus
Progressive abdominal distention with falling hemoglobin (116→98 g/L) suggesting intra-abdominal bleeding
New ascites on repeat ultrasound after initially no free fluid
Abrupt development of anhydramnios within 12 hours despite a previously normal fluid pocket, consistent with uterine rupture with leakage into the peritoneal cavity
Low-lying placenta on ultrasound supports placenta accreta spectrum/percreta as a cause of rupture in mid-gestation

PMC8784702.diagnosis: Destructive thyroiditis with thyrotoxicosis due to direct invasion of the thyroid by oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma
rationale_summary: Previously normal thyroid function, then acute fever/tachycardia with neck tenderness and new marked thyrotoxicosis (high T3/FT4, suppressed TSH)
Neck CT showed tumor progression with invasion into bilateral thyroid lobes

PMC6709319.diagnosis: Bucillamine-induced interstitial lung disease presenting as organizing pneumonia superimposed on pre-existing usual interstitial pneumonia (RA-associated UIP)
rationale_summary: Progressive dyspnea developed after starting bucillamine for rheumatoid arthritis, on a background of pre-existing lower-lobe fibrotic changes consistent with UIP
HRCT showed new bilateral ground-glass opacities and consolidations sparing the lung bases, not explained by baseline UIP alone
BAL showed mixed inflammatory alveolitis and negative cultures/β-D-glucan, arguing against infection
Transbronchial biopsy demonstrated alveolar organizing pneumonia with inflammatory infiltration, supporting a drug-induced OP pattern in this context

PMC8377818.diagnosis: Papillary tumor of the pineal region (PTPR)
rationale_summary: Pineal-region third-ventricle-obstructing enhancing mass causing hydrocephalus in an adult
Histology with primitive round cells showing focal papillary architecture/rosette-like areas and high mitotic activity with high Ki-67 (60-70%)
Immunoprofile with strong cytokeratin (CK8/18, AE1/AE3, CAM5.2) and neuronal markers (synaptophysin, NSE), with GFAP and EMA negative, supporting PTPR over glial or classic pineal parenchymal tumors
No FDG-avid disease outside the pineal region on PET/CT, supporting a primary pineal tumor rather than metastasis

PMC7546246.diagnosis: Proximal median nerve entrapment (pronator teres syndrome)
rationale_summary: Progressive median-territory numbness (thumb, index, middle) with thenar triangle involvement and marked thenar wasting, with negative carpal tunnel provocative tests
Severe weakness/paralysis of FPL, FDP and FDS to the index finger with inability to make an "O-sign," indicating proximal median nerve involvement affecting AIN-innervated muscles
Tinel sign elicited ~12 cm distal to the medial epicondyle localizes irritation/compression to the proximal forearm region (around pronator/lacertus fibrosus)

Neurophysiology shows severe left median mononeuropathy with absent median motor responses but preserved sensory responses at the wrist and no evidence of carpal tunnel syndrome or brachial plexopathy, supporting a proximal median nerve lesion

PMC8032522.diagnosis: Hypopituitarism from a pituitary macroadenoma (with secondary adrenal insufficiency causing recurrent vomiting and severe hyponatremia)
rationale_summary: Recurrent cyclical vomiting episodes with severe hypotonic hyponatremia (Na 110 mmol/L, serum osmolality 223 mOsm/kg) and inappropriately concentrated urine (urine osmolality 266 mOsm/kg) without polydipsia/polyuria
Low random serum cortisol (105 nmol/L) consistent with adrenal insufficiency as a cause of euvolemic hyponatremia
Evidence of multiple pituitary hormone deficiencies: low TSH with low T3/near-low T4 (central hypothyroidism) and low FSH/LH with secondary amenorrhea (hypogonadotropic hypogonadism)
Hyperprolactinemia (prolactin 90.8 ng/mL) supports pituitary stalk effect/adenoma as the unifying cause

PMC11573733.diagnosis: Cardiac amyloidosis (restrictive infiltrative cardiomyopathy, most consistent with AL amyloidosis)
rationale_summary: Progressive right- and left-sided heart failure with preserved LVEF and restrictive Doppler filling pattern (E/A 11, e′ 3, E/e′ 18)
Marked concentric biventricular wall thickening with biatrial enlargement, interatrial septal thickening, and granular "sparkling" myocardium on echocardiography
ECG-echo discordance: low-voltage limb leads with pseudoinfarction pattern despite severe ventricular hypertrophy
Thickened cardiac valves, plethoric IVC, minimal pericardial effusion-typical infiltrative features
Elevated troponin without regional wall motion abnormalities, supporting myocardial infiltration rather than ischemic cardiomyopathy

rationale_summary: Low-SAAG, lymphocyte-predominant ascites with markedly elevated ascitic ADA (62 U/L)

PMC7850611.diagnosis: Isolated cardiac sarcoidosis presenting as an inflammatory septal mass causing high-degree atrioventricular block
rationale_summary: New conduction disease with intermittent 2:1 and exercise-induced high-degree AV block
CMR shows a focal inferobasal septal mass with late gadolinium enhancement (inflammatory/scar pattern)
FDG-PET shows intense focal uptake confined to the inferobasal septum without extracardiac uptake, consistent with isolated myocardial inflammatory disease
Extensive infectious and systemic inflammatory/autoimmune evaluation is negative and coronary disease is excluded
Endomyocardial biopsies can be non-diagnostic in cardiac sarcoidosis due to patchy involvement, matching the non-specific samples here

PMC10919384.diagnosis: Anisakis simplex-associated allergic myopericarditis with large pericardial effusion

PMC6726171.diagnosis: Subependymoma of the right lateral ventricle (temporal horn)
rationale_summary: 59-year-old asymptomatic patient with an incidental, well-circumscribed intraventricular mass
Imaging shows T1 hypointense, heterogeneously T2 hyperintense lesion with internal cystic components and no/minimal gadolinium enhancement, favoring a low-grade intraventricular glial tumor
SWI punctate hypointense foci and CT tiny hyperdense foci suggest microhemorrhages, which can be seen in subependymoma

MR spectroscopy with partially decreased NAA, normal choline, and increased myoinositol supports a low-grade glial neoplasm such as subependymoma rather than more cellular enhancing tumors (e.g., neurocytoma)

PMC9789769.diagnosis: Primary pulmonary mast cell tumor (with paraneoplastic eosinophilia/basophilia)
rationale_summary: Large lobulated heterogeneous left cranial lung mass with mediastinal lymphadenopathy and additional irregular endobronchial/nodular airway thickening in other lobes suggests malignant pulmonary neoplasia with possible spread.
Marked eosinophilia and concurrent basophilia at presentation are characteristic paraneoplastic hematologic changes most consistent with mast cell disease.

Infectious/parasitic screening (heartworm antigen, fecal parasite analysis) was negative and the lung mass did not regress after antibiotics/bronchodilators, supporting neoplasia over pneumonia/parasites.

rationale_summary: Known SLE with serologic flare at presentation (ANA 1:2560, anti-dsDNA >300 IU/mL, markedly low C3/C4)

Meningism with raised CSF opening pressure and inflammatory CSF (WBC 485 with neutrophil predominance, very high protein) but gram stain/cultures and extensive multiplex PCR/antigen testing for bacterial/viral/mycobacterial/fungal causes were negative and symptoms persisted despite broad antimicrobials

Neuroimaging showed a focal inflammatory parenchymal lesion (left temporal T2 hyperintensity with mild cortical swelling and petechial hemorrhages) with normal MRA/MRV and no lactate peak on spectroscopy, supporting inflammatory NPSLE rather than vascular occlusion or pyogenic infection

PMC8536869.diagnosis: Pacemaker Twiddler syndrome with lead dislodgement causing diaphragmatic/phrenic nerve stimulation
rationale_summary: 3 months after dual-lead pacemaker implantation, chest radiograph showed both leads displaced (atrial lead in SVC, ventricular lead in right atrium)
ECG demonstrated device malfunction: intermittent atrial pacing, ventricular undersensing, ventricular pacing spikes after QRS complexes, and no ventricular capture
Symptoms were rhythmic abdominal wall contractions at ~60 bpm that resolved when the pacemaker was deactivated, consistent with pacing-related diaphragmatic stimulation from displaced leads

PMC10199808.diagnosis: Intestinal spirochetosis due to Brachyspira aalborgii (Crohn's disease mimic)
rationale_summary: Stool culture grew Brachyspira aalborgii in a man with persistent rectal tenesmus, hematochezia, mucus, and ulcerative proctitis.
Symptoms showed transient improvement with metronidazole, consistent with partial treatment of spirochetal infection.

Course was refractory to prolonged antibiotics for STIs and to IBD-directed therapies (systemic steroids, adalimumab, ustekinumab) despite persistently elevated fecal calprotectin, arguing against true Crohn's and supporting an infectious mimic.

PMC6519488.diagnosis: Heparin-induced thrombocytopenia with thrombosis (HIT) due to therapeutic dalteparin
rationale_summary: New and progressive arterial and venous thromboses (worsening pulmonary emboli plus acute aortic mural thrombi) occurring while on therapeutic-dose dalteparin
Marked platelet fall from 468×10^9/L to 29×10^9/L over ~3 weeks after starting dalteparin
Peripheral smear showing fibrin stranding with confirmed true thrombocytopaenia supports a consumptive/drug-immune thrombocytopenia consistent with HIT

PMC9990263.diagnosis: Multiple sclerosis presenting with eight-and-a-half syndrome (left one-and-a-half syndrome plus ipsilateral LMN facial palsy from a left pontine tegmentum demyelinating lesion)
rationale_summary: Exam shows complete left horizontal gaze palsy with impaired left adduction on right gaze (one-and-a-half syndrome) plus ipsilateral lower motor neuron facial palsy, localizing to the left pontine tegmentum (PPRF/abducens nucleus, MLF, and facial fascicle)

PMC9327270.diagnosis: UNC80-related infantile hypotonia with psychomotor retardation and characteristic facies (IHPRF2) with hypomyelinating leukodystrophy
rationale_summary: Early-onset, frequent brief focal seizures starting at 3 months and only partially responsive to levetiracetam
Severe neurodevelopmental impairment/regression with marked axial hypotonia, poor feeding/weak suck, poor eye contact, and decreased social interaction
Brain MRI showing diffuse hypomyelination (diffuse T2 white-matter hyperintensity/T1 hypointensity), absent PLIC myelination, and a thinned corpus callosum
Consanguineous parents suggesting an autosomal-recessive neurogenetic disorder with normal extensive metabolic screening and normal chromosomal microarray

PMC4889796.diagnosis: Cytomegalovirus (CMV)-associated hypertensive anterior uveitis with subsequent nodular scleritis
rationale_summary: Unilateral hypertensive anterior uveitis with diffuse keratic precipitates and elevated IOP (32 mmHg)
Later development of a tender scleral nodule (nodular scleritis) in the same eye
No response to NSAID and only partial/plateaued response to high-dose oral prednisone
Extensive systemic/infectious workup reported negative (syphilis, tuberculosis, herpes simplex, Borrelia), and she was already on valacyclovir (which would not treat CMV)

PMC9334930.diagnosis: Central-variant posterior reversible encephalopathy syndrome (hypertensive encephalopathy with symmetric basal ganglia edema)
rationale_summary: Developed persistent severe systolic hypertension (up to 200 mm Hg) in the setting of end-stage renal failure requiring continuous hemodialysis
After several days of sustained severe hypertension, head CT showed new symmetric swollen hypodense lesions in the putamen and globus pallidus, consistent with vasogenic edema
MRI earlier showed bilateral putaminal involvement without diffusion restriction/ADC abnormality, supporting a reversible edema process rather than infarction

PMC5484965.diagnosis: Mycosis fungoides (early-stage cutaneous T-cell lymphoma), presenting as an erythema annulare centrifugum-like annular eruption
rationale_summary: Chronic (4-year), recurrent, symmetric annular patches with trailing scale on non-sun-exposed trunk/extremities and poor durable response to corticosteroids
Biopsy shows epidermotropic/lichenoid lymphocytic infiltrate with exocytosis of small lymphocytes with mild nuclear contour irregularities and papillary dermal fibrosis
Immunophenotype is T-cell predominant (CD3+) with partial loss of CD7 (~40%), supporting CTCL over reactive dermatoses
Histology lacks features expected for erythema annulare centrifugum (no perivascular "cuffing") and PAS stain is negative for fungi

PMC11249453.diagnosis: Primary small-bowel lymphoma (diffuse large B-cell lymphoma of the jejunum)
rationale_summary: Young man with systemic symptoms (20-lb weight loss) and severe iron-deficiency anemia with hemoccult-positive stool suggesting GI malignancy/bleeding source

CT and PET-CT show markedly thickened heterogeneous jejunal segment with mesenteric adenopathy, ascites, and a hypermetabolic multilobulated mass involving multiple jejunal loops-pattern consistent with intestinal lymphoma
Push enteroscopy demonstrates a large ulcerated malignant-appearing proximal jejunal mass; biopsy described as a high-grade malignant neoplasm, supporting an aggressive lymphoma presentation

PMC11154768.diagnosis: Primary anterior mediastinal nonseminomatous germ cell tumor (yolk sac tumor) with tumor thrombus into SVC/right atrium and pulmonary metastases
rationale_summary: Large lobulated anterior mediastinal mass on CT with multiple pulmonary nodules suggesting malignant metastatic disease
Markedly elevated serum alpha-fetoprotein (5000 ng/mL) strongly supports a nonseminomatous germ cell tumor (yolk sac component) over thymoma/lymphoma/thyroid etiologies
Contiguous thrombosis/tumor extension from brachiocephalic veins through SVC into the right atrium with a right atrial mass fits tumor thrombus/invasion from a mediastinal malignancy
Negative blood cultures, absence of fever/IV drug use make infective endocarditis vegetation unlikely

PMC5702215.diagnosis: CRB1-associated early-onset retinal dystrophy (Leber congenital amaurosis) with Coats-like exudative/serous retinal detachment
rationale_summary: Infantile onset nystagmus with motor/social developmental delay
Full-field ERG showed moderately to severely depressed cone and rod responses (diffuse early-onset retinal dystrophy rather than isolated achromatopsia)
Bilateral retinal/RPE atrophy with macular atrophy in the left eye

Right eye had a large chronic posterior serous retinal detachment with demarcation lines and subretinal fibrotic band without tears/holes (Coats-like exudative detachment pattern seen in some early-onset dystrophies)
Family history of an older sister with visual abnormalities supports an inherited retinal dystrophy; CNGB3 (achromatopsia) result is inconsistent with rod involvement and structural findings

PMC5070099.diagnosis: Parathyroid carcinoma (functioning parathyroid tumor causing primary hyperparathyroidism)
rationale_summary: Marked hypercalcemia (corrected Ca 3.96 mmol/L) with markedly elevated intact PTH (138.4 pmol/L) indicates PTH-dependent primary hyperparathyroidism
Large palpable neck mass with sestamibi SPECT/CT showing a very large (7-10 cm) tracer-avid lesion posterior to the right thyroid lobe

Biochemical severity (very high Ca and PTH, hypophosphatemia 0.35 mmol/L, rising alkaline phosphatase, rebound hypercalcemia after treatment) and tumor size favor parathyroid carcinoma over typical adenoma/hyperplasia
Workup for other syndromic causes was unrevealing (normal prolactin; normal urinary catecholamines/metanephrines)

PMC4664794.diagnosis: Granulomatosis with polyangiitis (GPA, ANCA-associated necrotizing granulomatous vasculitis)
rationale_summary: ENT involvement with left sensorineural hearing loss/aural fullness and gingival hypertrophy (compatible with 'strawberry gingivitis')
Pulmonary involvement earlier with febrile illness and right-lung infiltrate plus elevated ESR

Skin disease progressed from tender erythematous nodules to necrotic ulcerations; biopsy showed vasculitis of superficial and deep dermal vessels with granuloma formation and neutrophilic debris (necrotizing granulomatous vasculitis pattern)
Systemic inflammatory features (recurrent fevers, anemia) with extensive negative infectious workup and sterile cultures/PCR from lesions
Liver biopsy showing large necrotic areas palisaded by epithelioid cells with granulomatous inflammation supports a systemic granulomatous process consistent with GPA

PMC5237164.diagnosis: Paradoxical toxoplasmosis-associated immune reconstitution inflammatory syndrome (IRIS) with cerebral toxoplasmosis relapse/progression and spinal cord involvement (toxoplasmic myelitis) after HAART initiation
rationale_summary: Known advanced HIV with cerebral toxoplasmosis (positive T. gondii serology, multiple ring-enhancing brain lesions) initially improved on anti-toxoplasma therapy
Neurologic deterioration and appearance/worsening of ring-enhancing brain lesions plus new longitudinal thoracic spinal cord T2 lesions occurred ~3 months after starting HAART
Immune recovery at presentation (CD4 rose from 20 to 128 cells/µL; HIV RNA fell to 121 copies/mL) supports paradoxical IRIS rather than uncontrolled HIV
Broad CSF infectious workup negative and PET/CSF phenotype did not support CNS lymphoma despite EBV PCR positivity

PMC4020529.diagnosis: Extranodal marginal zone lymphoma (MALT lymphoma) arising in a Meckel diverticulum (ileum)
rationale_summary: Meckel diverticulum resection shows sheets of monocytoid B-cells with lymphoepithelial lesions infiltrating mucosa/submucosa and focal serosal involvement
Immunophenotype: CD20+, BCL-2+, weak CD43+; negative for CD10, Cyclin-D1, CD5, CD3, supporting marginal zone lymphoma and excluding follicular lymphoma and mantle cell lymphoma
Reactive germinal centers are BCL-2-negative and BCL-6-positive with no follicular colonization; low Ki-67 (<5%) consistent with indolent MALT-type process

Testosterone fell rapidly during hospitalization from 25.4 to 2.1 nmol/L over 10 days, supporting withdrawal of an external androgen source rather than an autonomous tumor
Polycythemia (Hgb 18 g/dL, Hct 54%) is consistent with androgen effect
Subnephrotic-range proteinuria (2.3-2.7 g/day) with bland urine sediment is a nephrotic-pattern glomerular disease presentation
Detectable anti-PLA2R antibodies (1:10) support PLA2R-associated membranous nephropathy
No evidence provided for alternative secondary causes (HIV/hepatitis B/C negative; normal hepatic profile; normal renal ultrasound)

PMC4323579.diagnosis: Neutrophilic dermatosis of the dorsal hands (localized Sweet syndrome)
rationale_summary: Acute febrile presentation with painful, well-demarcated violaceous plaques on the hands/digits
Associated systemic disease: newly diagnosed acute myeloid leukemia
Lesions rapidly progressed to multiple nonadjacent digits and haemorrhagic bullae despite broad antimicrobials
Wound cultures negative; debridement/histology showed acute inflammatory (fibrinopurulent) changes without neoplastic cells, supporting a sterile neutrophilic dermatosis rather than infection

PMC9676116.diagnosis: Acute biliary (gallstone/sludge) pancreatitis presenting with pseudo-ST-elevation and troponin rise
rationale_summary: Severe right upper-quadrant/epigastric pain with nausea/vomiting and pain aggravated by eating
Markedly elevated pancreatic enzymes (amylase 2420 U/L, lipase 987 U/L) consistent with acute pancreatitis
Cholestatic/hepatocellular injury pattern (AST 800, ALT 760, ALP 361) with hyperbilirubinemia (total 3.27, direct 2.37) and gallbladder sludge suggesting biliary etiology

ECG shows ST-segment elevation with very high troponin, but echocardiogram shows preserved EF (55%) and only trivial MR, supporting a pancreatitis-associated pseudo-infarction pattern rather than primary cardiac dysfunction

PMC11018330.diagnosis: Durvalumab-associated acute zonal occult outer retinopathy (AZOOR)-like autoimmune retinopathy
rationale_summary: New temporal C-shaped scotoma after starting durvalumab for small-cell lung cancer
Fundus autofluorescence shows broad C-shaped hypoautofluorescent zones with hyperautofluorescent margins bilaterally, typical of zonal outer retinal loss
OCT shows peripapillary ellipsoid zone loss in the left eye corresponding to the visual field defect, with minimal fundus changes
Fluorescein angiography shows normal vascular perfusion, arguing against vascular/ischemic causes
Brain metastasis location did not account for the visual field defect, supporting a retinal origin

CT angiography showed a large heterogeneous left pelvic retroperitoneal mass at the left iliac chain with probable compression/occlusion of the adjacent iliac vein

PMC6421145.diagnosis: Mid-ventricular obstructive hypertrophic cardiomyopathy with left ventricular apical aneurysm and intraventricular thrombus
rationale_summary: Transthoracic echocardiography showed asymmetric LV hypertrophy with midventricular high-velocity flow (2.00 m/s systolic, 3.67 m/s diastolic) and paradoxical early-diastolic jet from apex to base, consistent with midventricular obstruction
Imaging (echo and enhanced CT) demonstrated an LV apical aneurysm containing a 21×14 mm thrombus
Frequent PVCs and nonsustained ventricular tachycardia support an arrhythmogenic substrate seen in HCM with apical aneurysm
Coronary CT showed no significant stenosis, making ischemic aneurysm unlikely
Alternative infiltrative/metabolic causes were not supported (normal serum amyloid A, ACE, and α-galactosidase A)

PMC10962233.diagnosis: Disseminated cryptococcosis with laryngeal involvement (laryngeal cryptococcosis with pulmonary disease)
rationale_summary: Immunosuppressed patient (5 years of prednisone plus hydroxychloroquine for sarcoidosis) presenting with subacute sore throat, cough, hoarseness, and marked weight loss

Flexible laryngoscopy shows polylobulated epiglottic lesion with additional small epiglottic and ulcerated arytenoid mucosal lesions, compatible with infectious laryngitis in an immunocompromised host
Whole-body PET-CT shows multiple progressive ground-glass lung masses and bilateral nodules, suggesting an opportunistic pulmonary infection rather than isolated benign sarcoidosis activity

rationale_summary: Established diffuse lepromatous leprosy features: facial infiltration with saddle-nose, shiny infiltrated back skin with intact sensation, symmetric nerve thickening with glove-and-stocking anesthesia, grade 2 deformities, and slit-skin smear with globi and BI 4+
Acute, painless hemorrhagic bullae with purpuric macules on extremities and ears rapidly rupturing into deep angulated ulcers-pattern consistent with Lucio phenomenon in diffuse lepromatous leprosy
Lack of fever/constitutional symptoms argues against necrotic erythema nodosum leprosum and supports Lucio phenomenon
Negative urine porphyrins and negative cryoglobulins/RF/ANA make pseudoporphyria and cryoglobulinemic/autoimmune vasculitis less likely

PMC8633751.diagnosis: Primary cardiac leiomyosarcoma (left atrial) with metastatic spread to the spine
rationale_summary: Initial left atrial lesion was a densely adherent fibrotic sheet-like mass rather than a typical myxoma, and frozen section showed organizing fibrous tissue/inflammation without myxoma cells
Sixteen months later, vertebral biopsies showed a malignant spindle cell tumor with smooth muscle differentiation (desmin+, smooth muscle actin+)
No extracardiac primary malignancy was found on mammography, Pap testing, or CT chest/abdomen/pelvis, supporting the prior cardiac mass as the primary source

PMC8138282.diagnosis: Cerebral fat embolism syndrome from bone marrow necrosis during sickle cell vaso-occlusive crisis
rationale_summary: Known sickle cell β-thalassemia with severe diffuse bone pain consistent with vaso-occlusive crisis and prior bone infarcts/avascular necrosis
Acute deterioration with high fever, tachypnea/respiratory failure and new lung infiltrate
Brain MRI showed extensive punctate diffusion-restricted lesions in basal ganglia, thalami, and bilateral white matter (typical for cerebral fat emboli pattern)

Thrombocytopenia with coagulopathy and transaminitis; smear with sickled cells and no schistocytes, ADAMTS13 not severely reduced-argues against TTP and supports embolic/inflammatory crisis related to marrow necrosis

PMC3350282.diagnosis: Ocular syphilis (neurosyphilis) presenting as optic perineuritis with disc edema
rationale_summary: Subacute asymmetric optic disc edema with trace afferent pupillary defect and blind-spot enlargement
Neuroimaging excludes intracranial mass/venous sinus thrombosis and lumbar puncture shows normal opening pressure (18 cm H2O), arguing against papilledema from raised ICP
CSF lymphocytic pleocytosis (72 WBC/mm³) suggests infectious/inflammatory meningitis compatible with neurosyphilis
Orbital MRI shows optic nerve sheath fluid and sheath enhancement consistent with optic perineuritis
Preceding unexplained groin lymphadenopathy supports a systemic sexually transmitted infection such as syphilis despite absent classic lesions/rash

PMC4754484.diagnosis: IgG4-related inflammatory pseudotumor (orbital/cavernous sinus IgG4-related disease)
rationale_summary: Progressive cranial neuropathies (bilateral vision loss to blindness, left-sided deafness) with an MRI mass-like lesion involving the cavernous sinus/retroocular region suggests an inflammatory pseudotumor process in this location

Biopsy showed predominantly chronic inflammatory cells with only a small whorled focus of oval cells, leading to an initial diagnosis of inflammatory pseudotumor and meningioma mimic-typical of IgG4-related mass-forming inflammation in the orbit/cavernous sinus
No systemic infection/malignancy clues on basic labs/imaging (normal ESR, normal chest/sinus radiographs, no lymphadenopathy) supports a localized inflammatory fibroinflammatory disorder

PMC8085510.diagnosis: Combined hepatocellular carcinoma-neuroendocrine carcinoma (mixed primary hepatic tumor)
rationale_summary: Markedly elevated AFP (2198 ng/mL) and AFP-L3% (86.6%) strongly support an HCC component in a patient with chronic hepatitis C and hepatic mass.
Core biopsy from the liver lesion shows high-grade neuroendocrine morphology with synaptophysin positivity and Ki-67 30%, indicating a neuroendocrine carcinoma component.
No somatostatin-receptor uptake on Ga-68 dotatate PET and no extrahepatic disease make recurrent/metastatic low-grade rectal NET less likely as the explanation for the liver lesion.

PMC6402948.diagnosis: Ophthalmia nodosa from retained caterpillar (insect) seta causing conjunctival/episcleral foreign-body granuloma
rationale_summary: Painless unilateral red eye with chemosis and a conjunctival granuloma adherent to sclera with central epithelial necrosis

Ultrasound biomicroscopy shows a high-reflectivity linear echo embedded in conjunctiva/episclera/superficial sclera with surrounding low-reflective granulomatous mass and multiple pseudocysts-typical of an embedded foreign body (seta) with granulomatous reaction
Infectious/inflammatory work-up (TB testing, chest CT, syphilis serology) was negative, supporting a localized foreign-body etiology

PMC10962068.diagnosis: Complex Müllerian outflow tract obstruction: bicornuate (unicollis) uterus with cervical/upper vaginal atresia causing hematometra and primary amenorrhea
rationale_summary: Primary amenorrhea despite normal secondary sexual characteristics (Tanner stage 4 breasts/pubic hair) suggests intact ovarian function with outflow obstruction
Long-standing cyclic cramping pain progressing to regular 45-50‑day intervals is consistent with retained menstrual flow

PMC8379665.diagnosis: Iatrogenic carotid-cavernous fistula after mechanical thrombectomy
rationale_summary: New right-sided ptosis with mild ophthalmoplegia (limited abduction and supraduction) the day after endovascular ICA thrombectomy suggests cranial nerve involvement in/near the cavernous sinus
Mild conjunctival chemosis OD supports an orbital venous congestion process typical of a carotid-cavernous fistula
Fundus exam and orbital CT were unremarkable and there was no new intracranial lesion on noncontrast head CT, making an intraocular cause or new parenchymal stroke less likely

PMC2396655.diagnosis: Dedifferentiated (immunohistochemically marker-negative) metastatic melanoma involving the stomach/upper abdomen
rationale_summary: Known history of high-risk nodular cutaneous melanoma (Breslow 8.3 mm, Clark IV)
Subsequent widespread metastatic disease on imaging (liver, lung nodules, subcutaneous recurrences, bone metastases)
New confluent upper abdominal mass indistinguishable from gastric corpus/pancreas with an endoscopically visible hard gastro-fundal mass

Biopsy showed an undifferentiated neoplasm; broad immunohistochemistry negative for carcinoma, GIST, and lymphoma markers, with only weak focal vimentin, supporting a dedifferentiated metastasis in the context of advanced melanoma

PMC11843684.diagnosis: Transient brachial plexus palsy from unintended local anesthetic spread during superficial cervical plexus block (SCPB)
rationale_summary: New painless unilateral (left) arm weakness noted shortly after surgery and SCPB, without altered consciousness, speech changes, leg weakness, or vital-sign instability
Weakness pattern involves proximal shoulder and arm more than hand (shoulder elevation 2/5; elbow/wrist/finger abduction 4/5), consistent with brachial plexus involvement
No intraoperative complications reported; neurologic deficit temporally associated with bilateral neck block using ropivacaine

PMC4140119.diagnosis: Neuropathic spinal arthropathy (Charcot spine) at L2-L3
rationale_summary: Progressive severe low back pain with neurologic deficits despite prior decompressive surgery

Imaging shows extensive vertebral body destruction/collapse (L2), paravertebral hypertrophic ossification, gross segmental instability, and severe lateral dislocation at L2-L3-features typical of Charcot (neuropathic) arthropathy rather than simple degenerative stenosis
Long-standing neurologic disease (idiopathic Parkinson disease) provides a setting for neuropathic joint degeneration leading to destructive spinal arthropathy

PMC7752573.diagnosis: HDR syndrome (Barakat syndrome; GATA3-related hypoparathyroidism with sensorineural deafness)
rationale_summary: Biochemical profile shows hypocalcemia with hyperphosphatemia and inappropriately low intact PTH, consistent with primary hypoparathyroidism
History of moderate bilateral sensorineural hearing loss requiring hearing aids
Long-standing hypocalcemia manifestations (tetany/spasms/cramps) with basal ganglia calcifications compatible with chronic hypoparathyroidism
Normal renal imaging does not exclude HDR syndrome, as renal involvement can be absent/variable

PMC6243317.diagnosis: Groove pancreatitis (paraduodenal pancreatitis / cystic dystrophy of the duodenal wall)
rationale_summary: Progressive epigastric pain worsened after meals with postprandial vomiting and weight loss
Mildly elevated amylase with normal blood counts
CT/MRI show cystic lesions within a circumferentially thickened second portion of the duodenum plus inflammatory fat stranding/fluid in the pancreaticoduodenal groove
Sheet-like enhancing soft tissue in the groove abutting the pancreatic head while the pancreatic head and pancreatic duct are normal
MRCP shows no dilation of the common bile duct or pancreatic duct, supporting an inflammatory groove/duodenal wall process rather than duct-obstructing pancreatic cancer

PMC4929803.diagnosis: Atypical hemolytic uremic syndrome (complement-mediated thrombotic microangiopathy)
rationale_summary: Progressive thrombocytopenia and anemia with blood film showing red cell fragments (schistocytes) and elevated LDH, consistent with microangiopathic hemolysis
Acute kidney injury with oliguria, rising urea/creatinine, enlarged kidneys, and pulmonary edema/effusions
Neurologic dysfunction (obtundation) with persistently normal PT/aPTT and fibrinogen, arguing against DIC and supporting thrombotic microangiopathy
No diarrheal prodrome described, making typical Shiga-toxin HUS less likely

PMC3459806.diagnosis: Paraneoplastic leukemoid reaction from cytokine (e.g., G-CSF)-producing metastatic lung adenocarcinoma
rationale_summary: Extreme leukocytosis to 146,200/µL with marked neutrophilia and left shift (myelocytes/metamyelocytes) in a patient with metastatic lung adenocarcinoma
Bone marrow shows massively increased granulopoiesis with no excess blasts; flow cytometry consistent with mature granulocytes
Myeloproliferative neoplasm workup negative: normal karyotype, BCR-ABL/t(9;22) negative, JAK2 V617F negative
Infectious source not supported: afebrile, procalcitonin low, cultures sterile, no response to broad-spectrum antibiotics
Leukocytosis and inflammatory markers increased with progressive metastatic disease (adrenal/iliopsoas lesions) during therapy

PMC9109486.diagnosis: Noncirrhotic hyperammonemic encephalopathy due to a portosystemic shunt (type B hepatic encephalopathy)
rationale_summary: Recurrent episodes of confusion, dysphasia, and fluctuating consciousness with EEG triphasic waves consistent with metabolic encephalopathy
Marked hyperammonemia (121 μg/dl) despite normal liver and kidney function argues for noncirrhotic hyperammonemia
Clinical and biochemical improvement with lactulose/rifaximin/enemas supports ammonia-driven encephalopathy from gut nitrogen load
Abdominal imaging showed no cirrhosis or significant portal hypertension, fitting encephalopathy from portosystemic shunting rather than liver failure
Valproic acid precipitated severe stupor, consistent with exacerbation of an underlying hyperammonemic state

PMC10481824.diagnosis: Fat-poor (epithelioid) renal angiomyolipoma with hemorrhagic/cystic change
rationale_summary: Very large heterogeneous complex cystic mass posterior to and displacing the left kidney, encasing renal vasculature/collecting system with mild hydronephrosis (suggesting renal/exophytic origin)
MRI shows multiple T1-hyperintense foci consistent with hemorrhage, a common feature of angiomyolipoma
Chemical-shift signal loss on out-of-phase images indicates microscopic lipid despite lack of macroscopic fat (fat-poor AML)
Avid enhancement of thick peripheral walls/septations with diffusion restriction in peripheral solid components is compatible with epithelioid AML presenting as a solid-cystic renal mass
