PMC7036447.diagnosis: Retinal neovascularization with vitreous hemorrhage secondary to chronic central serous chorioretinopathy (CSCR)
rationale_summary: Longstanding chronic CSCR with extensive RPE/photoreceptor degeneration and gravitational tracks on autofluorescence
Left eye had vitreous hemorrhage and a branched vascular proliferation projecting into the vitreous from the inferior temporal arcade, with otherwise normal peripheral retina and vasculature
Angiography/OCT-A showed an active, flow-positive preretinal vascular network at that site with surrounding retinal atrophy and underlying choriocapillaris/choroidal vessel voids
Diabetes and infectious causes of neovascularization were excluded and no anterior segment neovascularization was present

PMC8832220.diagnosis: Cytomegalovirus (CMV) corneal endotheliitis causing DMEK graft failure
rationale_summary: Late-onset recurrence of corneal edema 15 months after DMEK shortly after switching to weaker topical steroid (fluorometholone)
Inflammatory signs on the graft: conjunctival hyperemia, anterior-chamber cells, diffuse inferior mutton-fat keratic precipitates, Descemet's membrane folds
Rapid endothelial decompensation with abrupt drop from measurable ECD (980 cells/mm²) to unmeasurable alongside increased corneal thickness (to 704 µm)
HSV/VZV antibody tests not elevated, supporting a non-HSV/VZV viral endotheliitis pattern consistent with CMV

High rheumatoid factor and repeatedly negative standard blood cultures/serologies are compatible with culture-negative endocarditis presenting as vasculitis/GN, with steroid responsiveness but relapse on tapering

PMC9361795.diagnosis: Borderline tuberculoid leprosy (Hansen disease) presenting as granulomatous cheilitis
rationale_summary: Chronic lower-lip swelling initially diagnosed as cheilitis granulomatosa but unresponsive to intralesional corticosteroids
New erythematous plaques on face/chin developed within weeks
Biopsies from lip and plaque showed epithelioid granulomas with periadnexal and perineural lymphocytic infiltration, a histopathologic pattern characteristic of tuberculoid-spectrum leprosy
Workup for tuberculosis was unremarkable (normal chest radiograph, negative/normal Mantoux), supporting a non-tuberculous granulomatous etiology

PMC4470191.diagnosis: Central nervous system invasive aspergillosis (cerebral aspergillosis) complicating temozolomide-related neutropenia
rationale_summary: Profound febrile neutropenia (WBC 0.4 K/mm3, ANC 0.2 K/mm3) after chemoradiation and corticosteroids
New right temporal MRI abnormality not present previously, with acute fever and confusion suggesting an infectious CNS process rather than stable known tumor changes
Possible right lower lobe infiltrate on chest radiograph supports concomitant invasive fungal infection with potential hematogenous CNS dissemination
CSF with markedly elevated protein and low glucose but no cells/negative Gram stain is compatible with fungal CNS infection in an immunosuppressed host

PMC4310044.diagnosis: Neuroepithelial (glioependymal/neuroglial) cyst of the right middle cerebellar peduncle
rationale_summary: Well-defined lobulated cystic lesion within the right middle cerebellar peduncle adjacent to the fourth ventricle
Uniformly CSF-isointense on all MRI sequences with no enhancement and no diffusion restriction (argues against epidermoid or neoplasm)
Mild surrounding parenchymal edema supports an intraparenchymal neuroepithelial cyst rather than an extra-axial arachnoid cyst
MR spectroscopy shows unremarkable metabolites with normal ratios, supporting a benign non-neoplastic cyst

PMC4506805.diagnosis: Giant benign ovarian cyst (likely serous cystadenoma) mimicking massive ascites
rationale_summary: 11-year-old with 18 months progressive painless abdominal distention and fluid thrill initially read as ascites
MRI shows a very large (39×29×18 cm), homogeneous, well-defined unilocular cystic abdominopelvic mass occupying entire abdomen/pelvis without septations or solid components
Normal CBC/renal/liver tests and negative urine protein argue against systemic causes of true ascites
Normal tumor markers (CA-125, CEA, AFP, β-hCG) and absence of solid components favor a benign ovarian cystic neoplasm

PMC8962241.diagnosis: Cerebral air embolism secondary to lung cancer invasion into the left atrium
rationale_summary: Acute focal neurologic deficits (aphasia, left weakness, neglect) with NIHSS 7
Head CT showed multiple small low-density areas interpreted as intracranial air emboli
Chest CT and echocardiography showed left hilar tumor invading the left atrium, providing a route for air to enter systemic arterial circulation
Brain MRI findings (cortical/subcortical DWI hyperintensities with SWI dot-like low signals) support embolic cerebral injury

PMC3608321.diagnosis: Ectopic ACTH syndrome (ACTH-dependent Cushing's from an occult ectopic neuroendocrine tumor)
rationale_summary: Marked hypercortisolism (24-h urinary free cortisol 1300 nmol; serum cortisol 600-1000 nmol/L without diurnal variation) with Cushingoid features
ACTH is not suppressed (30-45 ng/L) and cortisol fails to suppress with 1-mg and 8-mg dexamethasone, supporting ACTH-dependent Cushing's

Pituitary MRI shows no lesion and inferior petrosal sinus sampling after CRH shows no central-to-peripheral ACTH gradient and no ACTH rise, arguing against Cushing disease and favoring ectopic ACTH secretion
Mildly elevated chromogranin A and flushing support a possible neuroendocrine source despite negative CT and 111In-pentetreotide scintigraphy

PMC8186929.diagnosis: Anomalous right coronary artery arising from the pulmonary artery (ARCAPA) with coronary steal causing cardiogenic shock

Clinical presentation with acute biventricular systolic dysfunction, shock, and large troponin rise despite no flow-limiting coronary disease

PMC10191493.diagnosis: Isolated gastroduodenal Crohn disease causing a duodenal stricture (gastric outlet obstruction)
rationale_summary: 12-year-old with 2 months of weight loss, abdominal pain, bilious vomiting and exam showing epigastric fullness consistent with proximal obstruction
Imaging shows marked gastric and proximal duodenal distention with decompressed distal duodenum/small bowel and endoscopy shows complete obstruction in the second portion of the duodenum
Labs show thrombocytosis and elevated inflammatory markers, supporting inflammatory bowel disease
Biopsies show chronic gastritis and active duodenitis proximal to the stricture with no malignancy or infection identified; granulomas can be absent in Crohn disease

PMC10901261.diagnosis: Severe secondary (functional) mitral regurgitation due to ischaemic cardiomyopathy with annular dilatation and leaflet tethering (Carpentier I/IIIb), contributing to low-output advanced heart failure
rationale_summary: Advanced ischaemic cardiomyopathy with NYHA IV symptoms and inability to wean from dobutamine with end-organ dysfunction (renal, hepatic, respiratory)
TOE: markedly dilated LV (LVEDD 72 mm) with LVEF ~20% and severe central MR from A2-P2 due to annular dilatation and tethering (functional MR)
Quantitatively severe/disproportionate MR (EROA 80 mm², regurgitant volume 76 mL)
Haemodynamic consequence of severe MR: giant left atrial V waves to 65 mmHg and pulmonary hypertension (sPAP 60 mmHg), plus pulmonary oedema on chest radiograph

PMC6257925.diagnosis: Prominent iris processes (benign iris strands inserting into the trabecular meshwork)
rationale_summary: Left eye gonioscopy showed multiple quadrants of dark brown raised mounds in the trabecular meshwork resembling iris tissue
Anterior segment OCT confirmed elevated tissue within an otherwise open angle recess and deep anterior chamber, without an iris/ciliary body mass

No retrocorneal pigment deposition, iris transillumination defects, or intraocular inflammation; IOP and axial length were stable and the angle appearance was unchanged over 6 months, supporting a benign anatomic variant rather than progressive pathology

PMC5807253.diagnosis: Metformin-associated lactic acidosis precipitated by acute kidney injury from dehydration/shock
rationale_summary: On metformin 1000 mg twice daily
Profound high-anion gap metabolic acidosis (pH 6.57, HCO3 2, anion gap 30) with very high lactate (16.3 mmol/L)
Severe acute kidney injury (creatinine 8.07 mg/dL from baseline 0.86) consistent with impaired metformin clearance
Preceding 3 days of vomiting/diarrhea with hypotension/shock requiring vasopressors, a common trigger for metformin-associated lactic acidosis

PMC5896219.diagnosis: Acute right forearm/hand compartment syndrome secondary to acute tophaceous gout flare
rationale_summary: Progressive severe forearm/hand pain and swelling with tense compartments, pain on passive stretch, sensory changes, and decreased capillary refill
Elevated compartment pressures (20-24 mm Hg volar forearm; 45-48 mm Hg carpal tunnel/palm) requiring urgent fasciotomy

PMC9723480.diagnosis: Cutaneous atypical mycobacterial infection presenting as nodular lymphangitis (most consistent with Mycobacterium marinum)
rationale_summary: Painful tender erythematous nodules in a linear (sporotrichoid) distribution along the upper limb with hand swelling

Immunosuppression (10 months of corticosteroids, methotrexate, hydroxychloroquine) increases risk for atypical mycobacterial skin infection

PMC6342725.diagnosis: Flecainide-induced acute hypersensitivity pneumonitis (drug-induced interstitial lung disease)
rationale_summary: Acute onset of fever, chills, dry cough, and rapidly progressive hypoxemia within hours of starting flecainide, with recurrence/worsening shortly after the second dose
Leukocytosis and CT showing diffuse bilateral ground-glass opacities with multifocal consolidation consistent with acute inflammatory pneumonitis
Cardiogenic pulmonary edema less likely: preserved LVEF, small/collapsed IVC, relatively low BNP (186 pg/mL), and no mitral stenosis
Clinical deterioration temporally linked to flecainide and prompted discontinuation

PMC11625114.diagnosis: Spontaneous left iliopsoas (psoas/iliacus) hematoma due to anticoagulation/antiplatelet therapy
rationale_summary: Developed sudden severe left lower abdominal and thigh pain while receiving heparin and antiplatelet therapy for NSTEMI
CT abdomen/pelvis showed marked swelling and heterogeneous density of the left psoas/iliacus muscles consistent with hemorrhage
CT angiogram showed active contrast extravasation into the left psoas region, supporting active retroperitoneal muscle bleeding
Angiography found no arterial source, consistent with spontaneous/anticoagulation-related iliopsoas hematoma rather than a focal arterial lesion

PMC10699832.diagnosis: Malignant gastrointestinal neuroectodermal tumor (GNET) with EWSR1-ATF1 fusion (extra-gastrointestinal presentation)

Clinical course with early local recurrence on PET-CT supports malignant behavior

PMC4300562.diagnosis: Familial atypical juvenile pityriasis rubra pilaris (type V PRP; psoriasis-spectrum/CARD14-associated)
rationale_summary: Both siblings developed chronic, diffuse nonexudative erythroderma with generalized scaling and severe pruritus beginning in infancy
Associated features include nail dystrophy and ectropion, compatible with an erythrodermic papulosquamous disorder such as PRP
Strong paternal family history of plaque psoriasis/psoriatic arthritis supports a familial PRP-psoriasis spectrum disorder rather than infection or metabolic disease
Normal routine labs and negative skin cultures make infectious or systemic causes of erythroderma less likely

PMC7481490.diagnosis: Benign metastasizing leiomyoma (pulmonary metastases from uterine leiomyoma)
rationale_summary: 46-year-old woman with a very large central pelvic soft-tissue mass planned for hysterectomy, consistent with uterine leiomyoma
Chest imaging shows innumerable bilateral soft-tissue pulmonary nodules/masses (up to 4 cm) without mediastinal/hilar lymphadenopathy, pleural effusion, bone lesions, or other metastatic features
Indolent 2-year dry cough with minimal symptoms and mild PFT abnormalities supports a slow-growing process compatible with benign metastasizing leiomyoma rather than aggressive malignancy

PMC2914667.diagnosis: Paraneoplastic autoimmune parkinsonism (basal ganglia encephalitis) associated with metastatic colon adenocarcinoma
rationale_summary: Subacute, rapidly progressive symmetric parkinsonism with rigidity, bradykinesia, resting tremor, postural instability, freezing gait, and dystonia
No prior neurologic disease and cognition largely preserved aside from slowed responses
Occurs in the setting of newly diagnosed stage IV colon adenocarcinoma
Marked clinical improvement after high-dose intravenous corticosteroids supports an immune-mediated/paraneoplastic process rather than idiopathic Parkinson disease or structural lesion

PMC10310912.diagnosis: Occult duodenal injury (contained perforation/serosal injury) during difficult cholecystectomy
rationale_summary: Severe acute cholecystitis with dense adhesions and gangrenous gallbladder adherent to the duodenum requiring open conversion and separation from the duodenum
Duodenum showed patchy bile discoloration intraoperatively, raising concern for occult injury
IOC showed an unexpected streak of contrast inferior to the cystic duct consistent with possible extraluminal contrast
No leak seen with pressurized methylene blue testing and repeat IOC 10 minutes later showed resolution of the streak, suggesting a small/contained injury that sealed or was nonpersistent

PMC6726168.diagnosis: Hereditary tyrosinemia type I (fumarylacetoacetate hydrolase deficiency)
rationale_summary: Onset in early infancy with progressive hepatosplenomegaly, jaundice, coagulopathy, and cirrhotic liver with multiple nodules
Markedly elevated serum α-fetoprotein in the setting of liver disease

Radiographic rickets consistent with renal Fanconi-type tubular dysfunction

PMC11337001.diagnosis: Cardiac sarcoidosis presenting as FDG-avid right atrial inflammatory masses (sarcoid pseudotumors) causing atrioventricular block
rationale_summary: Symptomatic bradycardia with second-degree atrioventricular block is a classic conduction manifestation of cardiac sarcoidosis
Cardiac MRI showed two right atrial masses with delayed gadolinium enhancement, consistent with infiltrative/inflammatory myocardial disease rather than simple thrombus
FDG-PET demonstrated both atrial masses were FDG-avid, supporting active inflammatory/granulomatous tissue
Family history includes sarcoidosis (father), increasing pre-test likelihood despite non-diagnostic/negative biopsies

PMC7463383.diagnosis: Trastuzumab emtansine (T‑DM1)-associated upper aerodigestive tract mucosal bleeding (hemorrhagic laryngitis/mucositis causing pseudohemoptysis)
rationale_summary: Two-week episodic 'hemoptysis' with choking on blood plus odynophonia/odynophagia
Nasopharyngolaryngoscopy showed friable nasal septum and epithelial bleeding with vocal fold edema/ulceration on supraglottic/glottic surfaces, indicating an upper-airway source
CT head/neck/chest and pulmonology evaluation found no pulmonary parenchymal lesion or lung source of bleeding
History of chemotherapy-related thrombocytopenia and ongoing T‑DM1 therapy (15 infusions; last 26 days prior) supports drug-related mucosal bleeding rather than metastatic progression

PMC10407195.diagnosis: Pituitary metastasis from metastatic sublingual adenocarcinoma
rationale_summary: History of widely metastatic adenocarcinoma (lung and bone metastases) while on treatment
MRI showed an enlarging, homogenously enhancing pituitary gland/stalk mass with invasive extension into cavernous sinus and adjacent dura

PMC9006328.diagnosis: Retained lens material (lens fragment) in the anterior chamber/angle causing chronic postoperative anterior uveitis
rationale_summary: After uncomplicated phaco/IOL, slit-lamp on postoperative day 1 showed a poorly defined intraocular foreign body at 12 o'clock with anterior chamber cells and keratic precipitates

Inflammation repeatedly recurred whenever topical steroids were tapered and improved when steroids were restarted, suggesting a persistent intraocular inflammatory stimulus rather than idiopathic uveitis
Systemic/rheumatologic evaluation was negative, and gonioscopy was planned specifically to further assess the anterior chamber foreign body (consistent with retained lens fragment in the angle)

PMC10971827.diagnosis: Macrophage activation syndrome (secondary hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis) complicating new-onset systemic lupus erythematosus after uterine curettage
rationale_summary: Meets SLE criteria (very high ANA and anti-dsDNA, low C3/C4, cytopenias, proteinuria, antiphospholipid antibodies) with persistent inflammatory syndrome despite antibiotics and negative cultures/procalcitonin
Progressive pancytopenia with hyperferritinemia, hypertriglyceridemia, hypofibrinogenemia, fever, and reactive bone marrow findings, yielding H score 195 (80-88% probability of MAS/HLH)

Clinical deterioration with serositis/effusions and systemic symptoms while on high-dose steroids supports severe immune activation rather than infection or cardiopulmonary failure (preserved EF, no PE)

PMC9976724.diagnosis: Osseous Langerhans cell histiocytosis (eosinophilic granuloma) involving the left T10 costovertebral junction
rationale_summary: Focal destructive/scalloping lesion with marrow edema and enhancement at the T10 costovertebral junction without systemic symptoms or lab inflammatory elevation
Needle biopsy showed fibrous/myxoid tissue with chronic inflammatory infiltrate and rare histiocytic elements, prompting CD1a immunostaining to characterize histiocytes
CD1a is a key diagnostic marker for Langerhans cells, supporting Langerhans cell histiocytosis as the cause of the lesion

PMC9523897.diagnosis: Primary cutaneous aspergillosis of the lower lip
rationale_summary: 10-month slowly enlarging, painful, thick-crusted ulcer on the lower lip with fluid discharge, refractory to topical antimicrobials/antifungals and empiric antibiotics/immunosuppression
Repeated biopsies showed inflammatory/reactive epidermal changes and mixed dermal infiltrate without malignancy, supporting an infectious/inflammatory rather than neoplastic process
Workup argued against key alternatives: leishmaniasis smear negative, direct immunofluorescence negative for immunobullous disease, no systemic immunodeficiency on HIV/hepatitis testing

Clinical differential explicitly included opportunistic fungal infection; chronic localized ulceration in an otherwise immunocompetent host (with steroid exposure) is consistent with primary cutaneous aspergillosis

Workup argued against key alternatives: leishmaniasis smear negative, direct immunofluorescence negative for immunobullous disease, no systemic immunodeficiency on HIV/hepatitis testing

Clinical differential explicitly included opportunistic fungal infection; chronic localized ulceration in an otherwise immunocompetent host (with steroid exposure) is consistent with primary cutaneous aspergillosis

PMC9361806.diagnosis: Transfusion-related acute lung injury (TRALI) causing acute noncardiogenic pulmonary edema/ARDS
rationale_summary: Severe acute tachypnea progressing to hypoxemic respiratory failure requiring intubation after receiving 2 packed RBC transfusions
Large volume of serosanguineous/icteric fluid recovered via endotracheal tube consistent with acute pulmonary edema/acute lung injury
Initial ultrasound showed volume contraction and no pulmonary B-lines/effusion, arguing against pre-existing cardiogenic overload before transfusion
Rapid deterioration temporally associated with transfusion despite supportive care, consistent with transfusion-associated acute lung injury

PMC6293179.diagnosis: Fulminant eosinophilic myocarditis (acute necrotizing eosinophilic myocarditis)
rationale_summary: Young woman with febrile illness and pleuritic chest pain plus very high troponin-T (5423 ng/L) and markedly elevated CRP
ECG with new LBBB, first-degree AV block progressing to complete AV block, asystolic pauses, atrial flutter, and ventricular ectopy suggesting severe inflammatory myocardial involvement
Peripheral eosinophilic predominance supporting eosinophilic myocarditis
Echocardiography rapidly deteriorated from regional LV apical dysfunction to severe global systolic failure (LVEF <25%) without dilatation, with cardiogenic shock requiring VA-ECMO planning
Coronary angiography normal, arguing against acute coronary syndrome as the cause of troponin elevation and LV dysfunction

PMC8876503.diagnosis: Corticosteroid-induced drug-induced liver injury (hepatocellular hepatitis from betamethasone/prednisolone)
rationale_summary: Marked ALT/AST rise began after starting IV betamethasone then oral prednisolone, peaking at ALT 1473 U/L/AST 722 U/L
Liver enzymes improved after stopping all medications, with viral and autoimmune hepatitis excluded by serologies
Rechallenge occurred when corticosteroids were resumed and she was discharged on prednisolone; severe hepatitis recurred 5 days later (ALT 1652 U/L/AST 766 U/L)
Prednisolone was stopped immediately on readmission due to suspected causality

PMC7869384.diagnosis: Acquired hemophilia A (autoantibody inhibitor to factor VIII)
rationale_summary: Previously healthy child with new mucosal bleeding (recurrent epistaxis) and later large soft-tissue hematoma with severe anemia
Isolated markedly prolonged aPTT (~116-117 s)
aPTT failed to correct on 1:1 mixing study after incubation, consistent with an inhibitor
Very low/absent factor VIII activity (0.7% then 0%) with normal other clotting factors and negative lupus anticoagulant screen
Confirmed factor VIII inhibitor titer 8.9 Bethesda units

PMC5743514.diagnosis: Wernicke encephalopathy due to thiamine deficiency (TPN-associated) after liver transplantation
rationale_summary: Received total parenteral nutrition from 3 days pre-op through post-op day 22 without specific thiamine supplementation
Subacute neuropsychiatric deterioration on post-op day 15 with confusion/irritability, lethargy, dysarthria, impaired coordination (difficulty grasping), and memory loss
MRI with symmetric thalamic and pontine involvement and periventricular abnormalities, a characteristic distribution for Wernicke encephalopathy
No alcohol use, normal ammonia/electrolytes/ABG, and tacrolimus trough not elevated, making alternative metabolic/toxic causes less supported by the case facts

PMC11724029.diagnosis: McArdle disease (glycogen storage disease type V; myophosphorylase deficiency) presenting as an exertional metabolic myopathy mimicking polymyalgia rheumatica
rationale_summary: Prominent proximal myalgia (hips/shoulders) with persistently elevated CK (811 IU/L) not typical for isolated polymyalgia rheumatica
Marked exertion-related CK spike to 3473 IU/L with normal CRP (<5 mg/L), consistent with exercise-induced muscle breakdown seen in metabolic myopathies
Autoimmune/myositis serologies negative and muscle MRI lacked inflammatory edema, arguing against idiopathic inflammatory myositis
Repeat EMG showed a symmetrical active myopathy with a proximal-to-distal severity gradient, supporting a primary myopathic process

PMC6510746.diagnosis: Primary giant cell tumor of the breast (giant cell tumor of soft tissue with osteoclast-like giant cells)
rationale_summary: Both core needle and vacuum-assisted biopsies showed spindle/oval mononuclear cells admixed with osteoclast-like multinucleated giant cells, stromal hyalinization, lymphocytic infiltration and focal hemorrhage without necrosis
Multinucleated giant cells were strongly CD68-positive and mononuclear cells weakly CD68-positive, supporting a histiocytic/giant cell tumor rather than epithelial carcinoma
No atypical epithelial cells were identified (only rare non-epithelial atypical cells in a duct), and tumor cells were ER/PR/HER2-negative
Axillary node FNA yielded only normal lymphocytes despite imaging concern for nodal metastasis, fitting a non-epithelial, non-metastatic breast giant cell tumor

PMC10876917.diagnosis: Autosomal recessive hypercholesterolemia (LDLRAP1-related homozygous familial hypercholesterolemia)
rationale_summary: Childhood-onset (before age 10) recurrent cutaneous and tendon xanthomas with markedly thickened Achilles tendons (15 mm)
Severe isolated hypercholesterolemia with very high LDL-C (357 mg/dL) and near-normal triglycerides
Sibling with childhood xanthomas and persistent hypercholesterolemia suggests an inherited disorder consistent with autosomal recessive inheritance

Alternative secondary/genetic mimics are less supported: sitosterol not elevated (argues against sitosterolemia) and lysosomal acid lipase activity is normal (argues against cholesteryl ester storage disease)

PMC5602830.diagnosis: Purpuric dermatitis herpetiformis (hemorrhagic/petechial variant of dermatitis herpetiformis)
rationale_summary: Known 7-year history of dermatitis herpetiformis with flare after stopping dapsone and relaxing gluten-free diet
New generalized petechiae/purpura and burning erythematous macules occurring alongside typical pruritic papulovesicles
Biopsy shows subepidermal blister with neutrophil aggregates in papillary dermal tips and pronounced dermal hemorrhage without leukocytoclastic vasculitis
Direct immunofluorescence shows granular IgA deposition in papillary dermal tips, consistent with dermatitis herpetiformis

PMC6090673.diagnosis: Antiphospholipid syndrome-related cutaneous thrombotic vasculopathy (livedoid vasculopathy/APS skin necrosis)
rationale_summary: Longstanding SLE with secondary antiphospholipid syndrome and persistent lupus anticoagulant positivity
Painful, progressive, non-healing ulcers with necrosis/eschar at pressure sites and new lesions at multiple sites
Therapeutic warfarin (INR ~2.5; prior 2.3-3.6) yet worsening lesions, arguing for ongoing thrombotic process
Skin biopsies show ischemia/necrosis with numerous microthrombi and thrombo-occlusion/recanalizing venous thrombus
Biopsies show no vasculitis and no calciphylaxis, supporting thrombotic vasculopathy rather than inflammatory vasculitis or calcific disease

PMC6984423.diagnosis: Cavernous sinus meningioma (parasellar meningioma) causing carotid compromise and ischemic stroke
rationale_summary: MRI shows a solid parasellar mass expanding the cavernous sinus with extension to sphenoid sinus/greater wing of sphenoid and uniform strong gadolinium enhancement-typical meningioma pattern
DSA demonstrates a parasellar tumor blush supplied by the right internal maxillary artery (external carotid supply), supporting meningioma

Lesion attenuates the internal carotid artery with angiographic absence/occlusion of ICAs and the patient has an MCA-territory infarct and acute contralateral hemiparesis consistent with carotid-compromise-related ischemia
Pituitary hormones and stalk/chiasm are normal, arguing against a functioning pituitary adenoma with suprasellar involvement

MRI shows large T2-hyperintense edema with irregular ring-enhancing lesions and peripheral diffusion restriction, mimicking mass/abscess but without tumor markers (normal AFP/β-hCG)
Susceptibility/CT demonstrate engorged veins traversing lesion centers (central vein sign), supporting inflammatory demyelination rather than neoplasm or abscess
Perfusion/ASL shows increased blood flow only in lesion periphery/merged area rather than centrally, consistent with demyelinating activity at lesion edge

PMC10715750.diagnosis: Ciliary body melanoma causing recurrent spontaneous hyphema with secondary ocular hypertension/glaucoma
rationale_summary: Recurrent transient monocular vision loss with later marked IOP spikes in the affected eye (32-38 mm Hg)
Gonioscopy showed blood in the anterior chamber angle (microhyphema) without iris transillumination defects, suggesting a non-IOL-chafing source
Vascular/amaurosis fugax workup and cerebral angiography were negative, lowering likelihood of embolic disease or carotid-cavernous/dural fistula
Ultrasound biomicroscopy demonstrated a circumscribed mass contacting the posterior surface of the iris, consistent with a ciliary body tumor

PMC8318494.diagnosis: Isolated unilateral absence of the right pulmonary artery (UAPA) with severe pulmonary arterial hypertension
rationale_summary: Progressive dyspnea/edema with exam and echo showing right heart failure and right ventricular enlargement/dysfunction
Right heart catheterization: severe precapillary pulmonary hypertension (mPAP 74 mm Hg, PAWP 12 mm Hg, PVR 35.4 WU, low cardiac index)
Perfusion scan: absent perfusion of right lung with normal left lung

Pulmonary angiography and 4D-CT: absence of right pulmonary artery from main pulmonary trunk; systemic collaterals supplying hypoplastic right lung; no chronic thromboembolic features (no pouch defects/intimal irregularities/abrupt narrowing)

PMC7857373.diagnosis: Hodgkin lymphoma (cervical and mediastinal nodal disease causing SVC syndrome with extensive central venous thrombosis)
rationale_summary: Fever with very high ESR/CRP and bulky cervical, pretracheal, and mediastinal lymphadenopathy on CT
Right middle lobe collapse/consolidation and visible chest-wall collateral veins consistent with mediastinal mass effect/SVC obstruction
Marked venous thrombosis involving right internal jugular, subclavian, brachiocephalic veins and superior vena cava, with imaging showing venous compression by enlarged node
Eosinophilia and markedly elevated IgE can be seen with Hodgkin lymphoma; infectious (TB/fungal/pyogenic) studies and autoimmune/thrombophilia workup were negative
FNAC was non-diagnostic, which can occur in lymphoma and does not exclude it

PMC11460033.diagnosis: Central (neurogenic) diabetes insipidus associated with partial empty sella syndrome
rationale_summary: Marked polydipsia (~15 L/day) and polyuria with very dilute urine (specific gravity 1.005; urine osmolality 150 mOsm/kg)
Water deprivation increased serum osmolality (to 298 mOsm/kg) without appropriate urine concentration (urine osmolality only 210 mOsm/kg)
Urine concentration normalized on desmopressin (specific gravity 1.020; urine osmolality 600 mOsm/kg), supporting ADH deficiency rather than nephrogenic DI
Pituitary MRI showed partial empty sella with flattened pituitary and no mass lesion; anterior pituitary hormones were within normal limits

PMC7399002.diagnosis: Periureteral (retroperitoneal) schwannoma involving the left ureter
rationale_summary: Solitary, well-defined soft-tissue mass involving the left ureter near the common iliac bifurcation with slow growth over 17 months
FDG-PET shows focal uptake (SUVmax 8.93) confined to this lesion with no other abnormal accumulations
No hydronephrosis, lymphadenopathy, or intraluminal ureteral abnormality on ureterography; urine cytology negative, arguing against urothelial carcinoma
History of colon cancer raised concern for recurrence, but isolated, slowly enlarging periureteral FDG-avid mass is consistent with a benign peripheral nerve sheath tumor such as schwannoma

No focal neurologic deficits aside from long-standing left-eye blindness, consistent with mass effect from an extra-axial/osseous destructive metastatic process

PMC4364689.diagnosis: Chronic multifocal bacterial osteomyelitis of both tibiae (chronic hematogenous osteomyelitis)
rationale_summary: 40-day history of bilateral leg pain, swelling, erythema, warmth with purulent discharge from proximal tibiae
Inflammatory markers and leukocytosis elevated (WBC 15,640/mm3, ESR 63 mm/h, CRP 5.29 μg/mL)

Imaging shows destructive bone infection: radiographs with cortical destruction and periosteal reaction; MRI with marrow edema and purulent debris in medullary canals/metaphyses plus adjacent pyomyositis; bone scan with increased metaphyseal uptake
Alternative specific infections screened and not supported (repeatedly negative PPD; negative Brucella/Salmonella serology; normal chest radiograph)

PMC6259034.diagnosis: Bilateral renal artery stenosis from fibromuscular dysplasia (ischemic nephropathy/Pickering syndrome)
rationale_summary: Young woman with severe new-onset, drug-refractory hypertension
CTA showed near-complete left renal artery occlusion and significant right renal artery stenosis with otherwise normal aorta/branches
Progression to malignant hypertension with recurrent flash pulmonary edema and severe, dialysis-requiring acute kidney injury despite preserved urine output
Asymmetric small, poorly perfused kidneys and later collateral supply to the right kidney indicate chronic renovascular disease
Right kidney biopsy showed viable, normal-appearing tissue, supporting a hemodynamic (renovascular) cause rather than intrinsic parenchymal nephritis

PMC10688243.diagnosis: Primary high-grade endometrial mucinous adenocarcinoma with intestinal (enteric) differentiation

Histology shows high-grade mucinous adenocarcinoma with glandular/solid growth, necrosis, clear-to-eosinophilic cytoplasm, intracytoplasmic mucin, high mitotic activity
Immunoprofile is intestinal-type (diffuse CK20 and CDX2) but with Müllerian support (focal CK7 and PAX8) and ER/PR negative
No gastrointestinal or other primary identified on EGD/colonoscopy, PET, or MRI, and imaging shows only minimal residual superficial endometrial lesion

PMC10888719.diagnosis: Neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorder (NMOSD) presenting with longitudinally extensive transverse myelitis and optic neuritis after COVID-19 vaccination
rationale_summary: Acute bilateral eye pain with MRI lesions in optic nerve/optic chiasm and delayed visual evoked potentials consistent with optic neuritis
Spinal MRI shows a longitudinally extensive intramedullary T2 hyperintense lesion from T2 to T12, characteristic of NMOSD-associated transverse myelitis
Severe rapidly progressive paraparesis with sensory symptoms and dysuria supports inflammatory myelitis
CSF shows elevated protein with otherwise unremarkable findings, compatible with NMOSD pattern
Infectious PCR testing (SARS-CoV-2 and other viruses, HIV) was negative, supporting an autoimmune demyelinating etiology

PMC6088471.diagnosis: IgG4-related disease presenting as chronic otitis media with effusion (Eustachian tube/nasopharyngeal and sinonasal involvement)
rationale_summary: Persistent unilateral conductive hearing loss with thick, viscid, red-yellow middle-ear effusion and ongoing otorrhea despite ventilation tubes and negative swab

Imaging showed extensive mucosal inflammation/enhancement extending from middle ear/mastoid along the Eustachian tube into the nasopharynx and sinonasal cavity, suggesting a systemic inflammatory process rather than simple infection

Routine autoimmune/infectious testing was unrevealing (normal ANCA, ANA, RF, IgE, ESR; unremarkable chest radiograph/QuantiFERON), while total IgG was elevated, supporting an IgG4-related inflammatory condition

PMC6099017.diagnosis: Primary hepatic B‑cell lymphoma (T‑cell-rich B‑cell lymphoma)
rationale_summary: Discrete, well‑vascularized hypoechoic hepatic mass with a smaller second hepatic lesion and no thoracic abnormalities, consistent with hepatic neoplasia
FNA cytology most consistent with lymphoma (mixed lymphoid population with lymphoglandular background) and inflammation considered unlikely
PARR showed a reproducible clonal immunoglobulin heavy‑chain rearrangement with polyclonal T‑cell receptor rearrangements, supporting a clonal B‑cell lymphoma
Flow cytometry showed predominance of small CD3+ T cells with only a small CD21+ B‑cell population, consistent with a T‑cell-rich B‑cell lymphoma pattern in the sampled mass

PMC10300258.diagnosis: Deep cerebral venous thrombosis (cerebral venous sinus thrombosis)
rationale_summary: Subacute headaches, seizure, markedly elevated CSF opening pressure (38 cm H₂O) with papilledema and severe visual loss suggest intracranial hypertension from impaired venous drainage

Neuroimaging shows bilateral temporal/insular and periventricular white-matter edema with involvement of corpus callosum, basal ganglia, and thalamus-an anatomic pattern compatible with deep venous outflow obstruction
Lesions are nonenhancing, without diffusion restriction or hemorrhage, and HSV PCR/CSF studies are negative, arguing against herpes encephalitis and favoring venous edema/infarction

PMC11388267.diagnosis: Intermittent small-bowel intussusception caused by a terminal ileal intramural lipoma (lead point)
rationale_summary: Acute severe cramping periumbilical/right iliac pain with vomiting and persistent symptoms despite supportive therapy
Three-month history of intermittent right-sided abdominal discomfort suggests episodic/intermittent obstruction
Abdominal radiograph showed small-bowel distension and absence of ileocecal gas, consistent with distal small-bowel obstruction
CT angiography identified a small intramural lipoma in the terminal ileum, a typical lead point for adult intussusception
Colonoscopy could not be advanced beyond the terminal ileum, supporting a mechanical lesion/obstructing process at the terminal ileum

PMC3130996.diagnosis: HSV-2-associated lumbosacral radiculitis with postherpetic neuralgia (sacral radiculopathy after genital herpes)
rationale_summary: Acute bilateral vulval blisters diagnosed as genital herpes with rapid response to valacyclovir
Subsequent persistent burning pain and exquisite hyperaesthesia localized to left S1-S3 dermatomes without motor deficit
No recurrent ulcers during the prolonged pain episode and minimal further response to continued antiviral therapy, consistent with neuropathic/postinfectious pain
Recurrent vulval blisters followed again by identical dermatomal hyperaesthesia that improved with amitriptyline (neuropathic pain treatment)

CSF and extensive infectious/autoimmune/paraneoplastic workup unrevealing except mildly elevated protein, and no response to high-dose steroids or IVIG

PMC10810688.diagnosis: X-linked hypophosphatemia (FGF23-mediated renal phosphate wasting osteomalacia) masquerading as ankylosing spondylitis
rationale_summary: Chronic hypophosphatemia (phosphate 1.9 mg/dL) with inappropriately non-low 24-hour urine phosphate (1300 mg) indicates renal phosphate wasting

Skeletal findings include diffuse enthesophytes/ligament calcification and sacroiliac fusion mimicking ankylosing spondylitis, but HLA-B27 is negative and there was no benefit from NSAIDs or adalimumab
Osteomalacia features: proximal femoral bowing with subtrochanteric insufficiency fractures and elevated alkaline phosphatase (148 U/L)
Extra-skeletal features supportive of XLH: progressive hearing loss since childhood and long history of severe dental disease/abscesses
Biochemistry compatible with FGF23-mediated hypophosphatemia: normal calcium, normal-high 1,25(OH)2 vitamin D, and elevated PTH

PMC8225966.diagnosis: Central (intraosseous) mucoepidermoid carcinoma of the mandible
rationale_summary: Elderly patient with a 15-month history of a slow-growing, painless bony mandibular expansion and intact mucosa (suggesting an intraosseous primary process)
Panoramic radiograph shows a large multilocular radiolucent lesion extending from ramus to canine region and involving the inferior alveolar canal
CT demonstrates an expansile lytic mandibular lesion with cortical destruction, consistent with an aggressive intraosseous neoplasm
No cervical pathologic lymphadenopathy and no distant metastatic disease on staging CT, fitting a primary intraosseous salivary-type malignancy presentation

PMC11359919.diagnosis: Neuroretinitis (most consistent with Bartonella henselae/cat-scratch disease)
rationale_summary: Acute, painless unilateral central vision loss with grade-1 RAPD and centrocecal scotoma
Fundus: diffuse optic disc swelling with macular edema followed by development of a macular star (hard exudates)
Fluorescein angiography: optic disc leakage; OCT: optic-nerve-to-macula subretinal/intraretinal fluid
Systemic prodrome with low-grade fever and painful cervical/submandibular lymphadenopathy
MRI and lumbar puncture unremarkable, supporting an ocular (neuroretinitis) rather than demyelinating/central cause

PMC10798835.diagnosis: Traumatic (irritation) fibroma of the palatal mucosa
rationale_summary: Small, sessile, smooth, non-tender palatal mucosal nodule with slight whitish coloration consistent with a benign fibrous reactive lesion
History of local dental trauma to the adjacent maxillary central incisor preceding lesion development
Radiographs show no bone involvement and adjacent teeth are vital, arguing against a periapical/intraosseous odontogenic process

PMC10834926.diagnosis: Fat-containing focal nodular hyperplasia (FNH) / FNH-like lesion
rationale_summary: Two small, well-circumscribed hepatic lesions in a patient without hepatitis, alcohol use, cirrhosis, or tumor marker elevation
Arterial-phase hyperenhancement with hepatobiliary-phase retention of ethoxybenzyl contrast supports a benign hepatocellular lesion with preserved OATP function (typical of FNH/FNH-like lesions)
MRI showed intralesional fat (opposed-phase signal drop) in a mildly fatty liver, consistent with a steatotic variant of FNH that can mimic well-differentiated HCC on dynamic imaging

PMC7869131.diagnosis: Williams-Campbell syndrome (congenital deficiency of bronchial cartilage causing distal cystic bronchiectasis with expiratory airway collapse)
rationale_summary: Recurrent pneumonias since childhood with bronchiectasis and infections including Nocardia and Mycobacterium avium-intracellulare
CT shows ballooning cystic bronchiectasis distal to the third-generation bronchi, involving 4th-6th order bronchi, with marked collapse of these bronchiectatic airways on expiratory imaging
Pulmonary function tests show progressive fixed obstructive physiology without bronchodilator reversibility

Common alternative causes of bronchiectasis were not supported: normal immunoglobulins, normal alpha-1 antitrypsin, normal sweat chloride, negative autoimmune and Aspergillus testing, negative AFB smear/fungal cultures

PMC4857264.diagnosis: Autoimmune myelofibrosis associated with systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE)
rationale_summary: Severe transfusion-dependent anemia with bone marrow showing hypercellularity, panhyperplasia, and moderate reticulin fibrosis without atypical megakaryocytes or dysplasia (not typical PMF)
Negative JAK2 (V617F/exon 12) and MPL mutation testing argues against clonal myeloproliferative neoplasm
Marked clinical fluctuation and clear steroid responsiveness (dexamethasone-induced remission) supports immune-mediated marrow failure/fibrosis
Photosensitivity, arthralgias, night sweats, and family history of SLE prompted evaluation for connective-tissue-disease-related myelofibrosis

Regular narrow-complex long-RP SVT with earliest atrial activation at the His-bundle electrogram indicates an atrial source near the septum
Ventricular burst pacing during tachycardia showed ventriculoatrial dissociation, arguing against AV reentrant mechanisms (AVRT/AVNRT) and supporting atrial tachycardia

PMC8780476.diagnosis: COVID-19-associated sinus node dysfunction (sick sinus syndrome) causing cardioinhibitory syncope
rationale_summary: Recurrent positional syncope with gradual transition from sinus tachycardia to marked sinus bradycardia and loss of consciousness
Holter documented sinus pauses up to 3.4 seconds with idioventricular rhythm and slow ventricular tachycardia, without AV-conduction abnormality
No structural heart disease on echocardiography and normal troponin/TSH, supporting primary nodal/autonomic dysfunction rather than ischemic or structural cause
Acute SARS-CoV-2 infection with fever and COVID-19 pneumonia temporally associated; CSF evaluation for other neurologic dysautonomia was unremarkable

PMC5204013.diagnosis: Secondary hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (HLH) triggered by scrub typhus (Orientia tsutsugamushi infection)
rationale_summary: Confirmed scrub typhus (eschar; rising O. tsutsugamushi IgG titer) with persistent fever despite appropriate antibiotics
Hepatosplenomegaly on exam/CT
Progressive cytopenias (WBC 2610→1800/mm3; hemoglobin 11.9→9.8 g/dL; platelets 237k→105k)
Marked hyperferritinemia (2,000 ng/mL), hypofibrinogenemia (70 mg/dL), and severe transaminitis/LDH elevation consistent with HLH physiology prompting bone marrow evaluation

PMC7396068.diagnosis: Intraosseous schwannoma (neurilemmoma) of the anterior wall of the right maxillary sinus
rationale_summary: Severe localized pain just below the right infraorbital rim with a small lesion adjacent to the infraorbital foramen suggests a benign peripheral nerve sheath tumor involving/irritating the infraorbital nerve region

CT showed a small (3.5 mm), well-defined, corticated, spherical intraosseous radiolucency with soft-tissue attenuation and minimal expansion-typical imaging appearance of a benign intraosseous schwannoma
No facial swelling/tenderness and the lesion's benign, corticated margins supported a benign bone neoplasm prompting excisional biopsy

PMC11625115.diagnosis: Thoracic spinal chondrosarcoma (primary chondroid malignancy of the T5-T6 posterior elements/costovertebral region)
rationale_summary: 36-year-old with 3-year progressive painful enlarging posterior thoracic mass and evolving myelopathic symptoms (bilateral weakness, hypoesthesia below xiphoid, Babinski/Romberg, constipation)
Imaging shows a large expansile lytic-sclerotic lesion centered in the right posterolateral T5-T6 vertebrae/transverse process with involvement of the costovertebral junction and ribs 5-6
CT describes a chondroid matrix with calcification, supporting a cartilaginous tumor

MRI shows heterogeneous mass with necrosis and aggressive local extension (T4-T8 spread, spinal canal stenosis, intradural/intramedullary infiltration, thoracic cavity extension), favoring malignant chondroid tumor over benign lesions

PMC12008005.diagnosis: Functional neurological symptom disorder (conversion disorder) presenting with bilateral leg paralysis and aphonia
rationale_summary: Acute onset inability to walk with temporary bilateral leg paralysis and loss of voice while remaining conscious and able to communicate by gestures

PMC9611706.diagnosis: Gastric fundal arterial varices (hypertrophied gastric arterial collaterals) secondary to splenic artery thrombosis

PMC6590559.diagnosis: Drug-resistant right frontal lobe epilepsy due to focal cortical dysplasia
rationale_summary: Longstanding focal hyperkinetic seizures with impaired awareness and bilateral tonic-clonic seizures at awakening, refractory to multiple antiseizure medications

PMC6172599.diagnosis: Symptomatic giant Virchow-Robin (perivascular) spaces secondary to prior cranial radiotherapy

PMC5474342.diagnosis: Extraskeletal myxoid chondrosarcoma (EMC) of the masticator space/parotid region
rationale_summary: Rapidly enlarging painless infiltrative soft-tissue mass in masticator space with multinodular pattern on MRI
Biopsy shows multiple well-demarcated nodules of tumor cells in abundant myxoid stroma separated by fibrous septa (classic multinodular myxoid architecture)
Tumor cells variably round-to-spindle with occasional epithelioid/lipoblast-like forms and low mitotic activity
PAS-positive diastase-labile cytoplasmic granules consistent with glycogen (reported in EMC)
Immunoprofile supports EMC: vimentin+, NSE+, chromogranin+ with broad negativity for cytokeratins/EMA, S-100, and myogenic markers (desmin/actin)

Young patient with progressive peripheral thinning over years and associated ocular discomfort/photophobia fits Terrien-type marginal degeneration

PMC6495497.diagnosis: Dengue-associated Miller Fisher syndrome (Guillain-Barré syndrome variant)
rationale_summary: Acute onset of the classic triad: complete external ophthalmoplegia, gait ataxia (broad-based with dysmetria/dysdiadochokinesia), and generalized areflexia with normal limb strength and sensation
Cranial nerve involvement (ptosis, palatal weakness, dysarthria/dysphagia) with sluggish pupils supports a GBS-spectrum cranial neuropathy rather than central lesion
Concurrent confirmed acute dengue infection (positive NS1 antigen and RT-PCR) with thrombocytopenia and transaminitis
Brain CT/MRI normal, and nerve conduction studies show peripheral neuropathy without neuromuscular junction decrement, favoring Miller Fisher syndrome over brainstem stroke/encephalitis or myasthenia

PMC10460928.diagnosis: Adult-onset Still's disease complicated by macrophage activation syndrome (secondary HLH)
rationale_summary: Daily high-grade fevers with inflammatory arthritis (swollen/tender wrists and PIP joints)
Diffuse maculopapular rash with prominent linear erythematous plaques (flagellate-type eruption described in Still's)
Marked hyperinflammation with ferritin >15,000 ng/mL, IL-18 11,000 pg/mL, and soluble IL-2 receptor >4,000 pg/mL
Cytopenias (anemia, thrombocytopenia) and hepatitis/cholestasis (AST/ALT and alkaline phosphatase markedly elevated)

PMC11092545.diagnosis: Cholinergic urticaria (heat/exercise-induced urticaria) temporally associated with clozapine and recurrent with olanzapine
rationale_summary: Generalized intensely pruritic pinpoint erythematous papules triggered by heat exposure or exercise-induced sweating and resolving within ~1 hour at rest
No other medications or environmental exposures reported; lesions occurred even in evening hours (argues against photosensitivity)
Onset after clozapine initiation and persisted despite antihistamine and dose reduction; resolved after stopping clozapine
Recurred with olanzapine and resolved again when clozapine was replaced with haloperidol, supporting antipsychotic-associated cholinergic urticaria

PMC10217392.diagnosis: Giant right ovarian mucinous cystadenoma (benign epithelial ovarian tumor)
rationale_summary: 16-year-old with 1 year progressive abdominal enlargement and a giant, relatively mobile abdominal mass
Ultrasound/MRI: very large (115×224×350 mm), thin-walled multilocular cystic tumor arising from the right ovary with septa and mild mural/septal enhancement; radiology suggested cystadenoma
Tumor markers largely normal (CEA, AFP, β-HCG, LDH, CA19-9), supporting a benign epithelial cyst; CA-125 moderately elevated, which can occur with large benign ovarian cysts

Karyotype 46,XX and normal brain MRI argue against central or syndromic causes

PMC3080319.diagnosis: IgG4-related chronic sclerosing sialadenitis (Küttner tumor) of the submandibular gland

PMC6581986.diagnosis: Cat eye syndrome (22q11 partial tetrasomy/duplication syndrome)
rationale_summary: Multiple congenital anomalies typical of cat eye syndrome: ocular coloboma (iris/retinochoroidal/optic nerve), anal atresia, cleft lip/palate, and genitourinary defects (hypospadias, vesicoureteral reflux)
Skeletal anomaly (supernumerary 11th ribs) supports a syndromic chromosomal disorder

PMC3371498.diagnosis: Post-dural puncture cerebrospinal fluid leak causing intracranial hypotension (post-dural puncture headache) with secondary seizures
rationale_summary: Severe frontal headache began day 1 after a difficult lumbar epidural and became markedly postural (worse upright, relieved supine), classic for CSF leak/post-dural puncture headache
Postpartum seizures occurred with normal blood pressure, no proteinuria, and normal renal/liver tests, arguing against eclampsia/HELLP
Head CT and MRI/MR venography were normal, arguing against intracranial hemorrhage and cerebral venous sinus thrombosis
Lumbar puncture showed clear CSF with no cells, supporting a noninfectious process

PMC10557406.diagnosis: Warfarin-associated hemorrhage (concurrent gastrointestinal bleeding with pulmonary/alveolar hemorrhage due to supratherapeutic anticoagulation)
rationale_summary: On chronic warfarin with elevated INR 3.1 at presentation
Developed persistent hematochezia followed by hemoptysis/hematemesis without another clear source on CT abdomen/pelvis or endoscopy
Chest imaging showed new focal left lower-lobe opacity suspicious for pulmonary hemorrhage and bronchoscopy found old blood in the left lower lobe without an identifiable lesion
EGD showed only erosive gastropathy without active bleeding, supporting anticoagulation-related mucosal bleeding rather than a focal GI lesion

PMC5995707.diagnosis: Takayasu arteritis with inflammatory left main coronary artery stenosis causing acute coronary syndrome/cardiogenic shock
rationale_summary: 25-year-old Japanese woman without atherosclerotic risk factors presenting with acute coronary syndrome pattern (aVR ST elevation with diffuse ST depression) and cardiogenic shock/arrhythmias
Marked systemic inflammation (WBC 19,400/μL, CRP 8.18 mg/dL) with negative autoantibodies and normal lipids argues against typical atherosclerosis

Coronary angiography showed critical (99%) left main trunk stenosis; IVUS demonstrated no calcification and concentric vessel wall thickening with an abnormal three-layer appearance, supporting inflammatory arteritis rather than plaque rupture/dissection

PMC9096471.diagnosis: Transient perivascular inflammation of the carotid artery (TIPIC syndrome; carotidynia)
rationale_summary: Unilateral severe pain and focal tenderness/swelling over the right carotid bifurcation radiating to the ipsilateral ear, triggered by neck movement
Inflammatory markers largely normal/low and no neurologic deficits, arguing against systemic vasculitis or dissection-related ischemia
Ultrasound shows eccentric hypoechoic wall thickening at distal CCA/proximal ICA without plaque, stenosis, or abnormal Doppler flow

Minimal hemoglobin rise (9.8→10.2 g/dL at 6 months; 10.7 g/dL at 18 months) despite prolonged oral iron, with persistent low iron stores, indicating refractory iron deficiency typical of IRIDA

PMC7303445.diagnosis: Idiopathic hypereosinophilic syndrome with eosinophilic arteritis (eosinophilic vasculitis involving temporal and peripheral arteries)
rationale_summary: Subcutaneous nodules corresponded to enlarged superficial temporal arteries with hypoechogenic wall thickening and stenosis on ultrasound, but ESR/CRP were normal (atypical for giant cell arteritis)
Progressive marked eosinophilia over months (up to 1,954/µL) with no secondary cause found (negative parasites; unremarkable autoimmune/ANCA/viral serologies)

Development of peripheral ischemia (cool, cyanotic fingertips) with ulnar and digital artery occlusions on duplex and angiography indicates systemic medium/small-artery involvement consistent with eosinophilic vasculitis in a hypereosinophilic disorder

PMC11802228.diagnosis: Miller Fisher syndrome (anti-GQ1b-associated Guillain-Barré spectrum) triggered by herpes zoster ophthalmicus
rationale_summary: Abrupt onset diplopia with multi-directional right ophthalmoplegia (abduction most affected) after a recent varicella-zoster infection
Generalized tendon reflex diminution with normal strength and no structural lesion on gadolinium MRI argues against orbital apex/cavernous sinus disease
Repeat CSF showed normal cell count with elevated protein (albuminocytologic dissociation) and VZV PCR negative, supporting a postinfectious immune neuropathy rather than ongoing viral invasion
Blink reflex abnormalities (absent right R1, prolonged bilateral R2) are consistent with cranial neuropathy/brainstem reflex pathway involvement seen in Guillain-Barré spectrum disorders

Extensive evaluation negative for viral hepatitis (A, B, C, E) and other viral infections (CMV, EBV, HSV, HIV)
Autoimmune hepatitis markers negative (ANA, SMA, AMA, anti-LKM1, SLA) and imaging (US Doppler, MRCP) shows no biliary obstruction

PMC4528349.diagnosis: Ectopic ACTH syndrome from an intra-abdominal neuroendocrine tumor (small-intestinal/mesenteric carcinoid)
rationale_summary: Clinical features and labs consistent with endogenous Cushing syndrome (moon facies, striae, truncal obesity, proximal wasting; elevated urinary free cortisol and loss of diurnal variation; failure to suppress with 1 mg dexamethasone).
ACTH is not suppressed (32.6 pg/mL), indicating ACTH-dependent hypercortisolism.
Repeated pituitary MRIs were normal and prior inferior petrosal sinus sampling did not localize a pituitary source.
CT shows a large enhancing intra-abdominal mass adjacent to small intestine, providing a plausible ectopic ACTH source.

PMC8638666.diagnosis: Primary cutaneous mast cell sarcoma (localized mast cell sarcoma of the skin)
rationale_summary: Adult-onset solitary yellowish plaque without systemic findings and normal serum tryptase
Dermal tumor composed of sheets of atypical epithelioid mast cells with metachromatic granules on Giemsa
Mast cell phenotype on immunohistochemistry (CD117+, tryptase+, MiTF+), with aberrant markers (heterogeneous CD30, focal CD25)
Progression toward malignancy on excision (increased pleomorphism, up to 9 mitoses/mm2, Ki-67 ~29.5%)
No KIT mutation detected and no evidence of systemic mastocytosis on clinical/imaging workup

PMC6258140.diagnosis: Ocular calciphylaxis (calcific uremic arteriolopathy) causing bilateral occlusive retinal/choroidal vasculopathy with proliferative retinopathy
rationale_summary: End-stage renal disease on peritoneal dialysis with anemia, very high creatinine, elevated alkaline phosphatase and hypoalbuminemia (systemic context for uremic calcific vasculopathy)
Bilateral severe ocular ischemia signs: rubeosis iridis, optic-disc neovascularization, vitreous/subhyaloid hemorrhages, and peripheral capillary nonperfusion on fluorescein angiography
Imaging supports vascular wall pathology: hyperreflective arterial vessel walls on OCT and thickened/enlarged choroidal vessels on EDI-OCT with leopard-spot choroidal hyperfluorescence

Extensive infectious, autoimmune, and thrombophilia testing negative, and Doppler shows reduced ophthalmic/central retinal artery flow, supporting an occlusive vasculopathy related to systemic renal disease rather than inflammatory or hypercoagulable causes

PMC10302296.diagnosis: Cranial mediastinal abscess with septic pleuritis/pleural effusion causing right-sided cardiac compression and right-sided congestive heart failure
rationale_summary: 10-day history of fever, depression, anorexia, absent rumination and chronic inflammatory leukogram/protein changes
Clinical signs of right-sided heart failure and thoracic disease (jugular distension with positive jugular test, dewlap edema, attenuated heart/lung sounds, costo-abdominal breathing)

Pleural fluid is a cloudy orange-brown septic exudate, supporting an infectious intrathoracic process consistent with an abscess and septic pleuritis

PMC7883144.diagnosis: Nitrofurantoin-induced acute hypersensitivity reaction (sepsis-like systemic inflammatory response)
rationale_summary: Abrupt onset of fever, chills, pleuritic chest pain/congestion, weakness and hypotension within <24 hours of starting nitrofurantoin
Recurrence of an almost identical severe episode within <24 hours on re-exposure two months later (positive rechallenge)
Marked neutrophilic leukocytosis during both episodes
Rapid resolution of symptoms within ~24 hours after stopping nitrofurantoin, with no alternative source of infection established despite cultures/empiric antibiotics

PMC4632472.diagnosis: Functioning struma ovarii (ovarian teratoma with ectopic thyroid tissue) causing persistent thyrotoxicosis after total thyroidectomy
rationale_summary: Recurrent thyrotoxicosis (high free T4, suppressed TSH) occurring 2 weeks after total thyroidectomy
Near-absent radioiodine uptake in thyroid bed (0.2%) argues against residual cervical thyroid tissue as the source
I-131 whole-body scan shows intense pelvic uptake with star artifact, indicating ectopic functioning thyroid tissue in the pelvis
Pelvic SPECT/CT localizes uptake to a large right adnexal mixed cystic-solid mass with calcification, compatible with struma ovarii
Mildly elevated CA-125 supports an ovarian mass process

PMC3920344.diagnosis: Paradoxical reaction to antituberculous therapy (tuberculosis-associated IRIS) presenting as persistent necrotic splenic tuberculous lesion
rationale_summary: Microbiologically confirmed tuberculosis (acid-fast bacilli in sputum) with initial improvement on standard four-drug therapy
After 2 weeks of treatment, recurrent fever/asthenia with negative repeat cultures/BAL argues against ongoing uncontrolled infection

Follow-up CT shows mediastinal lymph nodes decreasing (treatment response) while the splenic lesion persists/enlarged and necrotic, consistent with a paradoxical inflammatory worsening at a disease site during effective TB therapy
HIV serology negative, compatible with paradoxical reaction in an immunocompetent patient

PMC2740172.diagnosis: Intravenous leiomyomatosis with intracardiac extension (right atrium/right ventricle) originating from pelvic/iliac venous tumor

PMC5956711.diagnosis: CACNA1F-associated X-linked retinal disorder (incomplete congenital stationary night blindness with progressive cone dysfunction)
rationale_summary: Male child with early-onset nystagmus and abnormal visual behavior plus family history of nyctalopia/CSNB in relatives consistent with an X-linked night-blindness phenotype

Full-field ERG shows rod-predominant attenuation with electronegative mixed rod-cone response (reduced b:a ratio) and normal implicit times, typical of CACNA1F-related synaptic/bipolar pathway dysfunction
Cone responses progressively decline over serial ERGs while rod responses remain stable, consistent with CACNA1F spectrum that can show progressive cone dysfunction
Fundus/OCT show stable foveal hypoplasia and macular changes without structural progression over time, fitting a congenital retinal disorder rather than acquired degeneration

PMC6878082.diagnosis: Recurrent malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumor (MPNST) of the right forearm/elbow surgical scar

PMC7806373.diagnosis: Panhypopituitarism with secondary (central) adrenal insufficiency causing euvolemic hypotonic hyponatremia
rationale_summary: Severe hypotonic hyponatremia with inappropriately concentrated urine (serum Osm 245, urine Osm 309) and high urine sodium (68) in an apparently euvolemic patient suggests inappropriate ADH effect
Normal potassium and normotension argue against primary adrenal insufficiency; central adrenal insufficiency can cause hyponatremia without hyperkalemia

Marked short stature and feminine facial features in an elderly man suggest long-standing pituitary hormone deficiencies (e.g., GH and gonadotropins), supporting hypopituitarism as the underlying cause rather than ectopic SIADH (lung malignancy ruled out)

PMC8564683.diagnosis: Caseous calcification of the mitral annulus (caseous mitral annular calcification)
rationale_summary: Echocardiography showed an echo-lucent, partially calcified mass at the posterolateral/posterior mitral annulus without valve dysfunction, consistent with a mitral annular lesion rather than an intracavitary tumor
CMR demonstrated no first-pass perfusion (avascular content) with late gadolinium enhancement limited to the periphery, matching a caseous/necrotic center with a calcified/fibrotic rim
Transesophageal echocardiography excluded mobile vegetations and there were no clinical signs of infection, making endocarditis unlikely
Older patient with atherosclerotic disease (aortic arch plaque) supports a degenerative calcific annular process

PMC7040145.diagnosis: Ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS) of the left breast (ER/PgR/HER2-negative)
rationale_summary: Screening mammography showed clustered pleomorphic calcifications and imaging demonstrated a small irregular enhancing breast lesion with internal echogenic foci.
Core-needle biopsy diagnosed ductal carcinoma in situ, nuclear grade 2.
Immunohistochemistry on biopsy was ER-, PgR-, HER2- with elevated Ki-67 (32.4%), supporting a high-proliferative DCIS phenotype.
No palpable mass, normal tumor markers, and PET-CT showed no abnormal uptake outside the breast, consistent with non-metastatic in situ disease.

PMC11785469.diagnosis: Aggressive thoracic vertebral hemangioma (T2-T3) causing spinal cord compression
rationale_summary: 26-year-old with progressive paraparesis, T4 sensory level, hyperreflexia/clonus/Babinski, and bladder/bowel incontinence indicating thoracic myelopathy from compressive lesion
Spinal CT shows expansile osteolysis with vertebral body collapse, residual trabeculae, and sclerotic rim at T2-T3 with kyphosis-features consistent with aggressive vertebral hemangioma
MRI shows enhancing vertebral lesions involving posterior elements with high-grade cord compression; lesions are T1 isointense and slightly T2 hyperintense with gadolinium enhancement

Extensive systemic workup and surveillance CT found no primary malignancy; tumor markers and inflammatory/infectious labs were negative, supporting a primary vertebral vascular lesion over metastasis or infection

PMC4144080.diagnosis: Tacrolimus-associated acute inflammatory demyelinating polyneuropathy (Guillain-Barré-like syndrome) after lung transplantation
rationale_summary: Subacute, progressive symmetric lower-extremity weakness with evolving hypoactive reflexes and no sensory level or CNS lesion (normal brain/spine MRI)
CSF shows elevated protein with normal glucose (albuminocytologic dissociation pattern)
EMG/NCS demonstrates acute demyelinating polyneuropathy with axonal changes
Infectious triggers were not identified (negative cultures; negative CMV/EBV PCR), and symptoms occurred in the setting of tacrolimus-based immunosuppression post-transplant

PMC10092393.diagnosis: Chronic expanding hematoma of the left posterior thigh
rationale_summary: Mass slowly enlarged over 12 years with acute worsening pain and enlargement without trauma or anticoagulation
CT/MRI showed a very large multiloculated cystic lesion with fluid-fluid levels and a thick fibrous rim, consistent with recurrent internal hemorrhage within a capsule

Aspiration and operative evacuations yielded large volumes of serosanguinous/bloody fluid that rapidly reaccumulated, with diffuse capillary oozing and thrombosed vessels rather than a single bleeding source
Cytology showed blood clot and hemosiderin-laden macrophages without atypical cells, supporting a chronic hemorrhagic process rather than neoplasm

PMC8167300.diagnosis: Multisystem inflammatory syndrome in children (MIS-C) associated with prior SARS-CoV-2 infection
rationale_summary: 16-year-old with persistent high fever and systemic symptoms (abdominal pain/vomiting, headache/myalgias, cough)
Evidence of inflammation: elevated CRP (11 mg/dL) and ESR (26 mm/hr)
Multisystem involvement: gastrointestinal symptoms, conjunctival injection, and neck tenderness
Recent SARS-CoV-2 infection (PCR positive 1 month prior) with positive SARS-CoV-2 IgG antibody
Imaging suggested possible appendicitis but pediatric surgery felt presentation was not consistent, supporting inflammatory mimic rather than surgical appendicitis

PMC3974450.diagnosis: Juvenile psammomatoid ossifying fibroma (multifocal jaw ossifying fibroma)
rationale_summary: 6-year-old with rapidly developing, firm bilateral jaw expansions and well-demarcated mixed radiolucent-radiopaque lesions with a radiolucent rim and sclerotic border, causing tooth germ displacement and primary root resorption
Biopsies show fibroblastic fibrous stroma containing spherical calcified bodies (psammomatoid calcifications), more abundant in the maxillary lesion
Normal calcium/phosphorus/alkaline phosphatase/PTH and negative GNAS/HRPT2 sequencing argue against fibrous dysplasia and hyperparathyroidism-jaw tumor syndrome

PMC7026587.diagnosis: Lipomatous hypertrophy of the interatrial septum
rationale_summary: Homogeneous, echo-dense 4×4 cm mass located within/attached to the superior interatrial septum and moving synchronously with the atrial walls, consistent with a septal fatty mass rather than a pedunculated tumor
Patient is older and markedly obese (BMI 35 kg/m2), a typical risk profile for interatrial septal lipomatous hypertrophy
Normal atrial sizes and no valvular abnormality or enzyme/ECG evidence of acute ischemia, supporting an incidental septal mass finding on echocardiography

PMC11273566.diagnosis: Biliary tract vegetal foreign body (migrating grass awn) causing septic cholangitis/cholecystitis
rationale_summary: Ultrasound showed spindle-shaped hyperechoic linear structures with shadowing in the common bile duct and extending to the duodenal papilla, considered most consistent with vegetal foreign bodies
Bile cytology demonstrated septic suppurative inflammation and bile culture grew Escherichia coli, supporting bacterial cholangitis/cholecystitis
Intrahepatic biliary dilation and gallbladder wall thickening were present with hyperbilirubinaemia and marked ALT elevation
Follow-up ultrasound suggested retrograde migration of the spindle-shaped structures within the biliary tract despite medical therapy

PMC11783208.diagnosis: Torsion of a pedunculated subserosal uterine leiomyoma with ischemic/infarcted degeneration (with secondary right adnexal involvement and ureterohydronephrosis)
rationale_summary: Known uterine leiomyomas and MRI shows a solid mass arising from the right uterine cornua via a broad pedicle with bridging-vessel sign
Pedicle is thickened and twisted in a spiral configuration, consistent with torsion
Most of the mass shows no post-gadolinium enhancement with only a thin enhancing rim/small residual enhancing area near attachment, supporting ischemia/infarction
Right ovary is enlarged with stromal hemorrhagic signal and peripheral follicles, compatible with associated torsion/congestion
Right ureter is kinked at the twisted pedicle causing ipsilateral ureterohydronephrosis, indicating mass/pedicle torsion effect

PMC5918180.diagnosis: Antidromic atrioventricular reentrant tachycardia via a decremental right-sided atriofascicular (Mahaim) accessory pathway
rationale_summary: Clinical regular WCT with LBBB morphology/left superior axis and termination with adenosine
EP study showed abrupt preexcitation after fast-to-slow AV nodal switch with His potential displaced into ventricular electrogram
Incremental atrial pacing produced progressive stimulus-delta prolongation consistent with a decremental accessory pathway

Induced WCT matched clinical tachycardia and terminated with rapid ventricular pacing or adenosine, supporting an AV node-dependent reentrant mechanism using the accessory pathway anterogradely and AV node retrogradely

PMC10959544.diagnosis: Post-enucleation conjunctival inclusion (epithelial implantation) cyst of the anophthalmic socket
rationale_summary: History of childhood enucleation without orbital implant with new difficulty fitting ocular prosthesis
Socket exam shows a central translucent, vascularized round area of bulbar conjunctiva with associated symblepharon, consistent with a conjunctival/epithelial cyst
Orbital CT/MRI demonstrate a well-circumscribed bilobated cystic structure with no contrast enhancement, arguing against recurrent/expanding tumor
Lesion was absent on CT 6 years earlier, supporting an acquired post-surgical inclusion cyst developing over time

PMC5368394.diagnosis: Disseminated Mycobacterium marinum infection (fish-tank/soil-associated nontuberculous mycobacterial tenosynovitis with sporotrichoid nodules)
rationale_summary: Initial hand infection followed finger laceration while working in soil and he maintained multiple hobby aquariums (classic exposure for M. marinum)
Progressive unilateral hand/wrist/elbow swelling with firm nodules along extensor surfaces and spread to lower extremities suggests nodular lymphangitic (sporotrichoid) dissemination
Repeated standard bacterial cultures and joint/bursa aspirations were negative

Marked worsening of nodular lesions after TNF-α inhibitors (adalimumab then etanercept) and other immunosuppression supports disseminated nontuberculous mycobacterial infection rather than seronegative RA

PMC10838460.diagnosis: Idiopathic thoracic spinal cord herniation (ventral herniation at T4-5)
rationale_summary: Recurrent episodes of severe left upper back/chest pain with non-dermatomal hyperesthesia and weakness, with normal cervical MRI and negative thoracic outlet tests

Thoracic MRI shows loss of cerebrospinal fluid anterior to the spinal cord and an unusually short distance between the cord and adjacent vertebral bodies, consistent with ventral displacement/herniation of the cord at T4-5
Tiny T4-5 disc herniation present but key finding is anterior CSF obliteration suggesting a ventral dural defect with cord herniation

PMC6519503.diagnosis: Primary testicular lymphoma (diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma) with contiguous spread/tumor thrombus in the gonadal vein and adrenal involvement

PMC11978730.diagnosis: Perigastric schwannoma (extra-gastrointestinal schwannoma of the lesser curvature/lesser omentum)
rationale_summary: Incidentally found, well-defined ~4 cm dumbbell-shaped soft-tissue mass adjacent to the stomach's lesser curvature with apparent origin near but not involving gastric mucosa
Upper endoscopy showed only chronic superficial gastritis without mucosal lesion, supporting an extraluminal, non-epithelial tumor
3D reconstruction showed the mass encircling the left gastric artery and splenic vessels, a pattern consistent with a benign neurogenic/perivascular tumor rather than an intramural gastric malignancy
Normal tumor markers and lack of systemic symptoms support a benign soft-tissue tumor

PMC7921180.diagnosis: Coral reef aorta causing bilateral renal artery occlusion (severe calcific atherosclerotic plaque of the visceral aorta)
rationale_summary: Refractory hypertensive emergency with posterior reversible encephalopathy syndrome and abrupt severe rise in creatinine requiring dialysis
CTA/aortography show large calcific plaque in the visceral aorta (from below SMA to IMA) involving both renal artery origins
Both renal arteries occluded at their origins with distal reconstitution via collaterals, consistent with flow-limiting visceral aortic calcification rather than focal stenosis amenable to stenting
Extensive calcification made endovascular stenting unfeasible and suggested high-morbidity open endarterectomy, matching coral reef aorta behavior

PMC8473659.diagnosis: Chronic nonbacterial osteomyelitis (chronic recurrent multifocal osteomyelitis, CRMO) with associated recurrent inflammatory optic neuropathy
rationale_summary: Child with recurrent multifocal bone pain and periosteal reactions; bone scan showed multiple longitudinal uptake sites (humeri, forearms, ribs, femora, tibiae) with unremarkable marrow biopsy and no infectious evidence (negative tuberculin test).
Inflammatory labs (ESR/CRP elevated) with episodic fevers and rash support an autoinflammatory, noninfectious osteitis process.

Later developed recurrent, steroid-responsive bilateral optic neuritis/optic nerve enhancement with negative aquaporin-4-IgG and MOG-IgG and normal CSF opening pressure, consistent with extraosseous inflammatory manifestations linked to CNO/CRMO.

Subsequent central retinal artery occlusion occurred with negative vascular imaging and hypercoagulable/vasculitis testing, fitting an inflammatory systemic context rather than a primary embolic cause.

PMC7082872.diagnosis: Nutritional vitamin D deficiency (nutritional rickets/secondary hyperparathyroidism)
rationale_summary: Young kitten with bowed long bones and CT showing marked osteopenia and abnormal metaphyseal mineralization consistent with rickets/osteomalacia
Ionized and total hypocalcaemia with elevated parathyroid hormone supports secondary hyperparathyroidism from impaired calcium/vitamin D status
Low 25-hydroxyvitamin D3 indicates vitamin D deficiency; high 1,25-dihydroxyvitamin D3 is compatible with PTH-driven upregulation despite low substrate
Normal magnesium, phosphorus, bile acids and total thyroxine make hypomagnesemia, hepatic disease and thyroid disease less likely causes of hypocalcaemia

PMC7546301.diagnosis: Pseudotumor deltoideus (benign cortical irregularity at the deltoid insertion of the humerus)
rationale_summary: 44-year-old with lateral shoulder pain and pain on abduction/internal rotation without trauma or abnormal labs
X-ray shows focal cortical thickening/irregularity in mid-humerus at the deltoid insertion
CT shows an intracortical hypodense lesion with cortical expansion and focal protuberance

MRI shows eccentric cortical thickening with intracortical T1 isointense/T2 hyperintense signal and loss of normal cortical low-signal band, consistent with symptomatic deltoid insertional cortical irregularity rather than aggressive bone lesion

PMC4666013.diagnosis: Primary pigmented nodular adrenocortical disease (PPNAD) causing ACTH-independent Cushing syndrome (often within Carney complex)
rationale_summary: Both siblings have clinical Cushing features (rapid weight gain, purple striae, acne/bruising/myopathy/oligomenorrhea) with severe low bone density and fragility fracture
Biochemistry shows ACTH-independent hypercortisolism (0800 cortisol 808 nmol/L with ACTH <5 pg/mL; repeated in brother) and failure to suppress with low-dose dexamethasone
Paradoxical rise in urinary free cortisol during Liddle's test supports PPNAD
Adrenal imaging shows small nodule/enlargement and bilateral nodularity rather than a single large cortisol-secreting adenoma, fitting nodular adrenal disease in a familial setting

PMC6443550.diagnosis: Refeeding-associated acute liver injury (refeeding-induced hepatitis/hepatic steatosis) in severe malnutrition
rationale_summary: Profound malnutrition (BMI 11.2) with prolonged very low caloric intake (<400 kcal/day) and low visceral proteins (transthyretin, transferrin, retinol-binding protein)
After initiating and increasing enteral feeding (300→500 kcal/day), AST/ALT rose markedly starting day 8, peaking >1000 IU/L despite patient remaining asymptomatic
Electrolytes stayed normal, making classic refeeding syndrome less likely while timing with refeeding supports refeeding-related hepatic injury
Negative viral hepatitis serologies and anti-mitochondrial antibodies and repeatedly normal liver ultrasound argue against infectious, autoimmune, biliary, or obstructive causes

PMC11635634.diagnosis: Left anterior descending coronary artery pseudoaneurysm with associated pericardial hematoma (post-traumatic, post-CABG)
rationale_summary: New hyperattenuating structure in the left pulmonic pericardial recess on repeat chest CTA consistent with a hematoma developing after recent left-sided chest trauma
Delayed onset chest pain and dyspnea with marked troponin elevation suggests an acute coronary complication rather than pulmonary embolism (CTA negative for PE)

Differential for pericardial hematoma considered; negative blood cultures make mycotic aneurysm less likely, and LAD pseudoaneurysm was leading based on trauma and imaging location in a post-CABG patient

PMC2769463.diagnosis: Sarcoidosis with mediastinal lymphadenopathy and pericardial involvement (sarcoid-related pulmonary hypertension with large pericardial effusion)
rationale_summary: Progressive dyspnea with established severe pulmonary hypertension (RV systolic pressure 75-80 mmHg) and right-heart dilation
Large pericardial effusion with "water bottle" cardiac silhouette
CT shows enlarged mediastinal and hilar lymph nodes
