PMC2495002.diagnosis: Metastatic squamous cell carcinoma of the uterine cervix (advanced cervical cancer presenting with supraclavicular/cervical lymphadenopathy)
rationale_summary: Fine-needle aspiration from a cervical lymph node showed squamous carcinoma cells with no head-and-neck primary found on exam/endoscopy
CT demonstrated extensive mediastinal, para-aortic, and iliac lymphadenopathy with bilateral hydronephrosis/proximal ureteral dilatation suggesting pelvic obstructing malignancy
FDG PET-CT showed nodal disease extending to para-aortic/iliac chains and a focal hypermetabolic pelvic lesion consistent with a lower genital tract primary such as cervical/vaginal cancer

PMC4387347.diagnosis: Pituitary thyrotroph hyperplasia secondary to severe primary hypothyroidism (Hashimoto thyroiditis) mimicking a pituitary macroadenoma
rationale_summary: Severe primary hypothyroidism: free T4 0 ng/dL, free T3 low, TSH >100 µUI/mL with classic hypothyroid symptoms/signs (bradycardia, weight gain, dry skin, periorbital edema, macroglossia)
Autoimmune thyroiditis supported by elevated anti-thyroid peroxidase antibodies and atrophic hypoechoic thyroid on ultrasound
Pituitary MRI shows a sellar/suprasellar enhancing lesion with stalk/chiasm elevation and cavernous sinus involvement, compatible with pituitary enlargement from chronic TRH/TSH axis stimulation
Mild hyperprolactinemia (22.5 ng/mL) consistent with TRH-driven prolactin elevation seen in primary hypothyroidism
Visual field defect (bitemporal hemianopsia) explained by suprasellar pituitary enlargement compressing the optic chiasm

No evidence of inflammatory/autoimmune alternative (negative ANA, normal ESR, normal CBC/CMP)
Contrast CT showed a 4.5×3.3 cm mildly enhancing soft-tissue mass at the right cardiophrenic angle/posterior mediastinum
The mass was described as separate from the orthotopic liver, supporting ectopic hepatic tissue presenting as a mediastinal mass
Presentation with dyspnea and otherwise unremarkable exam/imaging aside from the mediastinal lesion is consistent with an incidental thoracic ectopic liver mass effect

Biopsy shows adenocarcinoma with CK7+/CK19+ and CK20−/TTF1− immunophenotype, supporting pancreatobiliary origin rather than colorectal or lung

PMC8927845.diagnosis: Gallbladder carcinoma with metastatic pulmonary lymphangitic carcinomatosis (paraneoplastic eosinophilia)
rationale_summary: Abdominal CT showed a 3 cm gallbladder mass with adjacent hepatic lesion and porta hepatis/para-aortic lymphadenopathy, and tumor markers were elevated (CA19-9, CEA, CA-125), supporting gallbladder cancer with metastasis

Chest CT showed interlobular septal and bronchovascular bundle thickening with diffuse ground-glass/reticular opacities, small nodules, pleural effusions, and mediastinal/supraclavicular/hilar lymphadenopathy-pattern concerning for lymphangitic carcinomatosis rather than infection
Marked peripheral eosinophilia persisted despite antibiotics, fitting a malignancy-associated (paraneoplastic) eosinophilia in this context

PMC3440930.diagnosis: Multilocular thymic cyst associated with HIV (often in diffuse infiltrative lymphocytosis syndrome)
rationale_summary: Contrast CT shows a lobulated anterior mediastinal mass made of multiple nonenhancing low-attenuation cystic spaces separated by enhancing septations (cystic regions ~14 HU, septa ~43 HU) without local invasion-typical imaging appearance of a multilocular thymic cyst rather than an invasive tumor

Patient with long-standing HIV has concurrent bilateral parotid enlargement and cervical lymphadenopathy, a clinical constellation reported with HIV-related lymphoid hyperplasia/DILS and thymic cystic disease
Relatively preserved CD4 count (551/mm³) supports a non-opportunistic, lymphoid hyperplasia-associated process rather than an aggressive infectious or malignant mediastinal mass

PMC9208679.diagnosis: Primary pulmonary tuberculosis with necrotic mediastinal (right paratracheal) tuberculous lymphadenitis causing airway compression
rationale_summary: CT showed a peripherally enhancing lobulated right paratracheal lesion contiguous with diseased lung and extending into the posterior mediastinum, consistent with necrotic lymphadenopathy rather than a purely intrapulmonary congenital lesion
Associated right upper lobe and superior right lower lobe consolidation with multiple cystic nonenhancing areas suggests necrotizing/caseating infection
Clinical course was subacute (2-week cough/tachypnea) with minimal systemic inflammation and negative HIV, compatible with primary TB presentation in an infant
No visible BCG scar and unknown family history increase concern for tuberculosis exposure/unimmunized status

PMC5120475.diagnosis: ROHHAD syndrome (Rapid-onset Obesity with Hypothalamic dysfunction, Hypoventilation, Autonomic Dysregulation)
rationale_summary: Abrupt, rapid-onset severe weight gain with marked hyperphagia beginning at 4 years of age
Prominent autonomic dysregulation: decreased lacrimation/nasal secretions/sweating, cold blue extremities, alternating diarrhea/constipation, polyuria/polydipsia, nocturnal enuresis
Neurobehavioral and sleep changes after obesity onset: mood lability/aggression, fatigue, social withdrawal, prolonged sleep and daytime somnolence

Endocrine workup largely normal despite symptoms, and MRI shows mild pituitary enlargement without other structural brain lesions-supporting hypothalamic/autonomic syndrome rather than primary endocrine obesity

PMC7372856.diagnosis: Calcifying fibrous tumor (calcifying fibrous pseudotumor) of the thyroid
rationale_summary: Progressively enlarging, firm, lobulated thyroid mass with prominent calcifications on CT and gross exam
Epicenter within the thyroid gland without invasion of adjacent cartilage on MRI, suggesting a well-circumscribed benign fibrous lesion
Additional smooth, homogeneous contralateral thyroid nodules consistent with potential multifocality
Elevated TSH with low-normal thyroid hormones supports associated thyroid dysfunction seen with fibrosing thyroid lesions

PMC8065272.diagnosis: Renin-mediated hypertensive crisis from renal hypoperfusion due to abdominal packing (packing-induced renovascular hypertension)
rationale_summary: Previously healthy 20-year-old developed new, severe hypertension after damage-control laparotomy with extensive abdominal packing (20 drapes) and massive resuscitation (+10 L balance).
Hypertension was refractory to multiple antihypertensives/diuretics and occurred in abrupt, self-limited paroxysms with anxiety, chest pain, and dyspnea.
Major alternative secondary causes were not supported by imaging: CTA showed no aortic dissection; renal Doppler was normal; CT showed no renal hematoma (argues against Page kidney).
Clinical context is most consistent with intermittent renal hypoperfusion from packing/raised intra-abdominal pressure causing neurohumoral (renin-angiotensin) surges and hypertensive crises.

PMC4898247.diagnosis: False-positive I-131 uptake in axillary/inguinal lymph nodes due to chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLL) involvement rather than differentiated thyroid cancer metastases
rationale_summary: Known CLL with enlarged axillary and retroperitoneal nodes on CT before ablation
Pretherapy WBS showed only minimal thyroid remnant uptake and no definite distant iodine-avid disease
After I-131 therapy, new bilateral axillary and inguinal foci appeared; SPECT/CT localized uptake to enlarged axillary lymph nodes
Distribution and timing are more consistent with radioiodine accumulation in lymphoma/CLL-involved nodes than metastatic spread from a 1.2-mm papillary thyroid microcarcinoma

PMC11522587.diagnosis: Vinyl chloride-associated systemic sclerosis-like disease with acro-osteolysis (vinyl chloride disease)
rationale_summary: Occupational exposure at a PVC factory handling chemicals without gloves
Progressive painless shortening of fingers over decades with hand radiographs showing complete distal phalangeal resorption (acro-osteolysis) and phalangeal fusion/sclerosis

Scleroderma-spectrum features without classic idiopathic systemic sclerosis stigmata: hypopigmented patches, one episode of digital cyanosis, extremity skin thickening/edema but no sclerodactyly/telangiectasias

Multisystem involvement compatible with vinyl chloride disease: peripheral neuropathy with hand muscle atrophy, chronic kidney disease on ultrasound, cardiomyopathy and pulmonary hypertension, lung abnormalities on CT

PMC7934766.diagnosis: Secondary (vascular) mania due to cerebral small-vessel ischemic disease
rationale_summary: New-onset late-life manic/psychotic syndrome (irritability, disinhibition, impulsive spending, hyperphagia, pressured/disorganized speech with flight of ideas, grandiose/paranoid delusions)
Prominent executive dysfunction with relatively preserved memory/orientation (FAB 9/18; MMSE 27/30) and frontal behavioral signs (perseveration, impaired abstraction)
Neurologic gait abnormality (broad-based shuffling gait) suggesting subcortical/frontal circuit involvement
MRI shows advanced small-vessel ischemic disease with confluent periventricular/subcortical white-matter hyperintensities, frontal-predominant atrophy, and microbleeds
Significant vascular risk profile (hypertension, heavy smoking, high LDL, high ASCVD risk) supporting a cerebrovascular etiology for late-onset mania

PMC10832606.diagnosis: Desmoid-type fibromatosis (aggressive fibromatosis) of the groin compressing the common femoral vein
rationale_summary: Progressive unilateral non-pitting leg edema with DVT excluded suggests proximal venous/lymphatic obstruction
CT/duplex/MRI showed a ~5 cm soft-tissue mass in the right groin dorsal to and compressing the common femoral vein causing focal narrowing
MRI signal (T1 isointense to muscle, mildly T2 hyperintense) with slight contrast enhancement is compatible with a fibrous spindle-cell lesion
Fine-needle biopsy demonstrated proliferating spindle cells, fitting desmoid-type fibromatosis

PMC3978404.diagnosis: Constrictive pericarditis causing chronic hepatic congestion (cardiac cirrhosis/congestive hepatopathy)
rationale_summary: Incidentally noted nodular cirrhotic liver biopsy showed sinusoidal dilatation, supporting hepatic venous congestion rather than primary liver disease
Negative viral, autoimmune, and metabolic liver serologies and no alcohol use argue against common intrinsic causes of cirrhosis

PMC6751912.diagnosis: Late-onset ornithine transcarbamylase (OTC) deficiency causing hyperammonemic encephalopathy (urea cycle disorder), precipitated by valproate/topiramate
rationale_summary: Recurrent episodic akinetic mutism/coma with postepisode amnesia over 5 years suggests intermittent metabolic encephalopathy
Continuous 2.5 Hz generalized discharges on EEG with lack of sustained response to multiple antiseizure/anesthetic therapies argues against primary nonconvulsive status epilepticus
Mild hyperammonemia with normal liver-function tests indicates nonhepatic hyperammonemia
Clinical deterioration with rising ammonia and cerebral edema after starting valproate/topiramate is consistent with valproate-triggered hyperammonemia in an underlying urea cycle disorder
Use of sodium benzoate/phenylbutyrate, dextrose infusion, and hemodialysis reflects treatment for urea cycle-related hyperammonemia

PMC6416959.diagnosis: Post-transplant lymphoproliferative disorder (PTLD) involving the colon/mesocolon
rationale_summary: Renal transplant recipient (10 months post-transplant) on triple immunosuppression (prednisone, mycophenolate, tacrolimus), a major risk setting for PTLD

CT showed two separate, ill-defined mesocolonic masses/phlegmon-like lesions, including a proximal mass completely separate from the diverticular segment and containing no air, making uncomplicated perforated diverticulitis less unifying

Alternative considerations explicitly raised included PTLD; multiplicity and soft-tissue mesenteric/colonic masses fit PTLD better than a single-site perforated diverticulitis or isolated perforated carcinoma with implant

PMC9941870.diagnosis: Desmoplastic fibroblastoma (collagenous fibroma) of the breast
rationale_summary: Core biopsy showed a hypocellular proliferation of bland spindle cells in collagenous and focally myxoid stroma without breast glandular elements, consistent with a benign paucicellular fibroblastic lesion
Immunohistochemistry was negative for CK5/6 and p63, arguing against an epithelial/myoepithelial process
β-catenin showed no nuclear staining (only cytoplasmic), arguing against desmoid-type fibromatosis and supporting collagenous fibroma/desmoplastic fibroblastoma morphology

PMC8426609.diagnosis: Diabetic hemichorea-hemiballismus (diabetic striatopathy) due to severe nonketotic hyperglycemia
rationale_summary: Acute unilateral left-sided chorea/ballistic movements with contralateral (right) basal ganglia involvement
Marked hyperglycemia (glucose 776 mg/dL, HbA1c 15.8%) without acidosis/anion-gap elevation consistent with nonketotic hyperglycemia
Initial CT showed right lentiform nucleus hyperattenuation sparing internal capsule, a typical imaging pattern
Symptoms improved after inpatient glucose control, then recurred with ongoing glycemic instability
Repeat MRI later demonstrated right caudate/lentiform T1 hyperintensity and corresponding signal change, supporting diabetic striatopathy

PMC8904927.diagnosis: Drug-induced hypersensitivity myopericarditis (perimyocarditis) related to myeloma therapy
rationale_summary: 19 days after starting lenalidomide/bortezomib/dexamethasone she developed pruritic erythematous rash plus GI symptoms and elevated inflammatory markers

Marked myocardial injury with very high hs-troponin T and ECG lateral ST-elevation with reciprocal inferior ST-depression, but echocardiography showed preserved EF and no regional wall-motion abnormalities (argues against acute coronary occlusion)
Presence of localized pericardial effusion supports pericardial involvement consistent with myopericarditis in a drug-hypersensitivity context

PMC5378861.diagnosis: Anti-Sox1 (AGNA)-associated autoimmune limbic encephalitis (paraneoplastic-type) presenting with nonconvulsive temporal lobe seizures

PMC6958744.diagnosis: Epstein-Barr virus-positive mucocutaneous ulcer (EBVMCU) of the colon
rationale_summary: Elderly man with circumferential colonic ulcer at splenic flexure and large inflammatory-appearing mass on CT with elevated soluble IL-2 receptor
Initial biopsies non-diagnostic for carcinoma; tumor markers normal
Lesion and associated lymphadenopathy regressed spontaneously on repeat CT and endoscopy, leaving scar-related stricture
Repeat biopsy showed granulation tissue with mixed inflammatory infiltrate and atypical cells; CD20+ B cells with EBER-1 positivity on ISH, supporting EBVMCU

PMC7016271.diagnosis: Gadolinium contrast-induced acute lung injury presenting as diffuse alveolar hemorrhage (noncardiogenic pulmonary edema)
rationale_summary: Sudden-onset dyspnea and hypoxemia (<30 min) after gadolinium-enhanced MRI
CT pulmonary angiography negative for embolism but shows diffuse perihilar ground-glass opacities with bilateral crackles
No evidence of cardiogenic pulmonary edema (normal troponin, only mildly elevated NT-proBNP, normal ECG, LVEF 56%, no pulmonary hypertension)
Infectious and systemic rheumatologic causes not supported (negative multiplex PCR/urine antigens; negative ANA/ANCA/RF/anti-CCP)
Temporal relationship to contrast exposure with otherwise unrevealing workup supports contrast-induced acute lung injury/DAH evaluated by BAL

PMC9254356.diagnosis: Primary central nervous system lymphoma with vitreoretinal involvement (masquerading as Vogt-Koyanagi-Harada syndrome)
rationale_summary: Bilateral exudative retinal detachments with choroidal thickening and pinpoint leakage/pooling initially mimicked VKH but there were no anterior chamber or vitreous cells
Neurologic deterioration with progressive subcortical white-matter hyperintensities and new hemorrhagic foci on MRI is atypical for VKH
Marked but transient improvement after high-dose corticosteroids followed by rapid relapse of vision and mental status suggests steroid-responsive CNS malignancy such as lymphoma rather than VKH

PMC10354359.diagnosis: Disseminated candidiasis (hepatosplenic candidiasis with multifocal subcutaneous and intramuscular fungal abscesses)
rationale_summary: Profound pancytopenia during AML therapy predisposes to invasive fungal infection
Multiple thick-walled cystic lesions with internal debris and peripheral Doppler vascularity in subcutaneous tissue and muscle are consistent with fungal abscesses
Multiple target-shaped/hypoechoic lesions in both liver and spleen are characteristic of hepatosplenic candidiasis
Multifocal distribution of soft-tissue and visceral lesions suggests disseminated infection rather than isolated hematoma or leukemic masses

PMC4712557.diagnosis: Hemosuccus pancreaticus from a splenic artery aneurysm (bleeding into the pancreatic duct)
rationale_summary: Six months of intermittent relapsing melena with repeated endoscopies failing to localize a GI bleeding source
CT shows a saccular aneurysm in the pancreatic segment of the splenic artery projecting into pancreatic parenchyma and contacting the pancreatic duct
Anemia consistent with chronic blood loss; no other abdominal source identified on imaging
Ductal contact and mild distal duct dilation support bleeding via the pancreatic duct as the cause of occult upper GI bleeding

PMC4799621.diagnosis: Systemic epinephrine absorption from epinephrine-containing arthroscopic irrigation fluid (iatrogenic catecholamine surge)
rationale_summary: Abrupt severe hypertension (229-234/111-116 mm Hg) and sinus tachycardia (115-125 bpm) occurred immediately after arthroscopic irrigation was initiated
Hemodynamics were minimally responsive to increased anesthetic depth (higher end-tidal isoflurane, propofol, midazolam) and esmolol boluses, consistent with ongoing catecholamine effect
Phenylephrine infusion was stopped but the hypertension/tachycardia persisted, suggesting a different adrenergic source temporally linked to irrigation onset

PMC6582827.diagnosis: Persistent ceruloplasmin-mediated hypercupremia following vertical sleeve gastrectomy (non-Wilson disease)
rationale_summary: Plasma copper elevated in association with elevated serum ceruloplasmin
Normal 24-hour urinary copper excretion and no Kayser-Fleischer rings argue against Wilson disease
Normal liver enzymes and albumin and no other secondary causes identified (no estrogen therapy, no copper IUD, negative autoimmune/celiac testing)

Hypercupremia persisted despite stopping copper-containing multivitamins and switching to a copper-free multivitamin, consistent with a post-bariatric, ceruloplasmin-driven elevation rather than copper overload

PMC3318239.diagnosis: Acute primary adrenal insufficiency from bilateral adrenal hemorrhage (warfarin-associated, in setting of antiphospholipid syndrome)

PMC5038105.diagnosis: Lucio phenomenon (necrotizing vasculopathy) in diffuse lepromatous leprosy
rationale_summary: Slit-skin smear with very high bacillary load (BI 6+) supports multibacillary leprosy

Diffuse facial/ear infiltration with ciliary/supraciliary madarosis, nasal septal perforation, and glove-and-stocking anesthesia with symmetrically thickened peripheral nerves are consistent with diffuse lepromatous leprosy

Painful stellate purpura progressing to hemorrhagic bullae, angular infarcts, gangrene, and deep necrotic ulcers with palpable pulses and normal arterial Dopplers fits Lucio phenomenon rather than large-vessel occlusion

PMC6341359.diagnosis: Right ventricular cardiac metastases from clear-cell renal cell carcinoma
rationale_summary: Known metastatic clear-cell RCC with rapid progression on sunitinib (worsening lung nodules, nephrectomy-bed recurrence, new liver lesions)

PMC5491825.diagnosis: Sporotrichosis (Sporothrix schenckii) causing chronic wrist tenosynovitis with rice bodies
rationale_summary: Farmer with subacute (1 month) bilateral wrist swelling/pain and marked flexor/extensor tenosynovial thickening containing multiple rice bodies on ultrasound
Immunosuppression (ulcerative colitis treated with prednisolone) increases risk for fungal tenosynovitis
Findings mimic rheumatoid arthritis (RF positive, elevated MMP-3/CRP) but anti-CCP negative and radiographs show no erosions
Aspiration shows inflammatory fluid without crystals or organisms; TB PCR negative, supporting a non-tuberculous chronic infectious tenosynovitis such as sporotrichosis

PMC9531072.diagnosis: Chronic recurrent multifocal osteomyelitis (chronic nonbacterial osteomyelitis)
rationale_summary: Young adult with a 2-year history of paroxysmal bone pain and swelling, recently worsening
Multifocal skeletal involvement on X-ray/CT/MRI (pelvis, spine, ribs, scapulae, femurs) with predominantly sclerotic lesions
Whole-body SPECT shows widespread increased bone metabolism consistent with active multifocal inflammatory bone lesions

Workup for hematologic malignancy/myeloma unrevealing (normal bone marrow aspiration and urine protein electrophoresis) and other systemic markers largely non-diagnostic (HLA-B27 negative; only mild NSE elevation)

PMC5073681.diagnosis: Multiple acute ischemic cerebral infarctions (multi-territory embolic-appearing stroke)
rationale_summary: Sudden severe headache with markedly elevated blood pressure (240/140 mm Hg) and hypertensive emergency on arrival (180/130 mm Hg)
Diffusion-weighted MRI showed restricted diffusion in multiple lesions involving different vascular territories, consistent with acute infarcts
Noncontrast head CT was unremarkable, compatible with early ischemic stroke
No extracranial or intracranial arterial stenosis on MRA and carotid ultrasound, making large-artery stenosis less likely
Significant vascular risk factors present (diabetes with A1C 11.0%, LDL 189 mg/dL, obesity)

PMC7174963.diagnosis: Heparin-induced thrombocytopenia with thrombosis (HITT) due to enoxaparin
rationale_summary: Received enoxaparin thromboprophylaxis after knee replacement and presented 15 days later with severe thrombocytopenia (platelets 43,000 ×10^9/L) confirmed on citrate sample
Markedly elevated D-dimer and multiple new thrombotic events: acute ischemic strokes, pulmonary emboli, hepatic vein branch thrombosis, and lower-extremity DVT
No alternative embolic source identified (sinus rhythm, normal echocardiography, normal carotid duplex) and no mucocutaneous bleeding despite thrombocytopenia, consistent with prothrombotic HIT

PMC8133085.diagnosis: Chronic spontaneous urticaria associated with Vespula (wasp) venom allergy (post-Hymenoptera sting sensitization)
rationale_summary: Recurrent migratory urticarial wheals, intensely pruritic, each lasting <24 hours and occurring without identifiable triggers, persisting despite high-dose antihistamines and prolonged low-dose prednisone
Extensive evaluation for infectious, autoimmune/thyroid, inflammatory, and complement-mediated causes is negative/normal; autologous serum skin test is negative

History of multiple wasp stings with acute hives preceding onset; subsequent testing shows venom sensitization (positive intradermal tests to Vespula/Vespa crabro, elevated specific IgE including rVes v 5) with normal baseline tryptase

PMC9549187.diagnosis: Giant partially thrombosed saccular left renal artery aneurysm causing severe hydronephrosis from extrinsic compression
rationale_summary: CT/CTA demonstrated a 109×102×94 mm saccular left renal artery aneurysm that was almost completely thrombosed with parietal calcifications and scant contrast filling
Large left flank palpable mass corresponds to the aneurysmal lesion seen adjacent to the calcified aortic wall on ultrasound/CT
Aneurysm compressed the renal pelvis causing severe hydronephrosis with extreme cortical thinning (2 mm)
Clinical presentation of chronic left low back pain radiating to the left leg is consistent with mass effect from a giant retroperitoneal vascular aneurysm

PMC11296012.diagnosis: Primary terminal ileal lymphoma (non-Hodgkin, likely EBV-associated)
rationale_summary: Subacute intermittent obstructive abdominal pain with weight loss and asthenia
CT shows a short (36 mm) regular circumferential pseudo-stenotic wall thickening of the terminal ileum causing proximal bowel/colon distension, a pattern compatible with lymphoma
Discrete adjacent mesenteric fat infiltration supports a neoplastic/infiltrative ileal process
EBV serology positive, which can be associated with certain gastrointestinal lymphomas

PMC5845136.diagnosis: Idiopathic hypereosinophilic syndrome with eosinophilic myocarditis (Loeffler endomyocarditis) causing acute heart failure
rationale_summary: Marked hypereosinophilia (AEC 21.6×10^3/μL, 63.5%) with systemic findings (fever, malaise, hepatosplenomegaly)

Acute cardiac involvement: dyspnea, elevated BNP (1280.5 pg/mL), very high troponin I (35.4 ng/dL), ST-segment depressions, echo showing global LV hypokinesia with EF 40% and small pericardial effusion
Extensive infectious/parasitic and autoimmune evaluation negative; no drug exposure/allergy/asthma history
Bone marrow: marked eosinophilia without malignancy; FIP1L1-PDGFRA and BCR-ABL negative, supporting idiopathic HES with eosinophilic cardiac involvement

PMC5507571.diagnosis: Migrainous infarction (acute ischemic stroke of the right medial longitudinal fasciculus causing internuclear ophthalmoplegia)
rationale_summary: History of migraine with visual aura and current episode began with typical migrainous headache symptoms (pulsatile frontal headache with nausea, photophobia, phonophobia)
Developed focal brainstem signs during the attack, including diplopia with right eye adduction paresis and preserved convergence (internuclear ophthalmoplegia) plus gait imbalance/ataxia
MRI showed diffusion restriction with T2/FLAIR hyperintensity in the paramedian right dorsal midbrain involving the ipsilateral medial longitudinal fasciculus, consistent with an acute infarct
CT/CTA were normal, supporting a small posterior circulation ischemic lesion rather than hemorrhage or large-vessel pathology

PMC11299065.diagnosis: Whipple disease-associated severe pulmonary hypertension causing right-heart failure and cardiogenic shock
rationale_summary: Known, biopsy-proven Whipple disease with severe malnutrition

Hemodynamics show severe pulmonary hypertension with markedly elevated pulmonary vascular resistance (mPAP 55 mm Hg, PVR 10 Wood units) and severe right-sided failure (RA pressure 32 mm Hg, severely reduced RV function)

No evidence of myocardial Whipple involvement or myocarditis on right-ventricular endomyocardial biopsy, and Whipple PCR in blood culture was negative, supporting a non-myocardial mechanism for shock dominated by pulmonary vascular disease

PMC6961360.diagnosis: Early prosthetic aortic valve regurgitation with combined transvalvular and paravalvular leak (stent-post-related) after surgical aortic valve replacement
rationale_summary: Post-CPB TEE showed turbulent regurgitant jets adjacent to a stent post of the new 23 mm bovine pericardial valve
One jet originated between the stent post and sewing ring (transvalvular leak)
Additional jets/flow convergence originated from outside the sewing ring with flow directed below the ring into the LVOT (paravalvular leak)
Prosthesis appeared well-seated with good leaflet mobility, localizing the problem to leak pathways near the stent post rather than gross malposition

PMC8436276.diagnosis: Catecholamine-induced takotsubo syndrome (midventricular variant) precipitated by pheochromocytoma resection
rationale_summary: Symptoms, adrenal mass, and elevated urinary metanephrines indicate a catecholamine-producing adrenal tumor
Acute postoperative hypotension with new LBBB and severe LV systolic dysfunction after tumor removal
Coronary angiography showed no obstructive disease or vasospasm, arguing against acute MI
Left ventriculography/echo showed circumferential midventricular (with apical) akinesia and reduced LVEF (25%), consistent with takotsubo pattern
Marked troponin and NT-proBNP elevation supports acute stress cardiomyopathy

PMC9721027.diagnosis: Diastatic cecal perforation secondary to obstructing left-sided colorectal carcinoma (closed-loop large-bowel obstruction)
rationale_summary: Elderly patient with 9 days of progressive constipation, vomiting, abdominal distension and colicky pain consistent with large-bowel obstruction
Abdominal radiographs show dilated small and large bowel loops with markedly dilated cecum (≥12 cm), suggesting a distal colonic obstruction with a competent ileocecal valve and high perforation risk
Free subdiaphragmatic air plus peritonitis signs (guarding, absent bowel sounds, diffuse severe pain) indicate bowel perforation
Inflammatory response (WBC 22,000/mm^3, CRP 189 mg/dL) supports perforation/peritonitis complicating obstruction

PMC6831833.diagnosis: Sai-rei-to (herbal medicine)-induced diffuse alveolar hemorrhage (drug-induced lung injury)
rationale_summary: Respiratory symptoms (dry cough, dyspnea, fever/chills) began ~2 weeks after starting Sai-rei-to
Imaging showed new bilateral ground-glass opacities/infiltrates with pleural effusions
Bronchoalveolar lavage demonstrated diffuse alveolar hemorrhage with neutrophil predominance

No prior interstitial lung disease or allergy history; bacteriological tests were negative and echocardiography showed preserved EF/normal wall motion, reducing likelihood of infection or cardiogenic pulmonary edema
Clinical decision to stop Sai-rei-to and start steroid pulse therapy consistent with suspected drug-induced DAH

PMC4422990.diagnosis: Low-grade endometrial stromal sarcoma involving the sigmoid colon (extrauterine endometrial stromal sarcoma)
rationale_summary: Obstructive sigmoid-colon mass with sheets/nests of uniformly small bland cells and prominent small arterioles infiltrating muscular wall and mesocolic fat
Extensive lymphovascular invasion with regional nodal metastases, but no necrosis and low proliferative activity (Ki-67 ~8%)
Immunophenotype: diffuse CD10+, estrogen receptor+, progesterone receptor+ supports endometrial stromal differentiation
Negative c-kit, DOG1, CD34 excludes gastrointestinal stromal tumor; negative cytokeratin and other lineage markers argues against carcinoma or other sarcomas

PMC11228663.diagnosis: Rhino-orbital-cerebral mucormycosis (angioinvasive fungal sinusitis) with cavernous sinus thrombosis and ischemic cerebral/orbital infarction
rationale_summary: Severely immunocompromised post-allogeneic HSCT with profound neutropenia and persistent/refractory sinusitis despite broad-spectrum antibiotics and voriconazole
Progression to orbital apex syndrome: proptosis, chemosis, ophthalmoplegia, nonreactive pupil, severe eye pain/swelling
Imaging shows invasive sinus disease with bony demineralization/erosion and orbital cellulitis
Angioinvasive pattern: absent opacification of left ophthalmic/facial artery and vein; cavernous sinus nonenhancement consistent with thrombosis
Brain MRI with multifocal restricted diffusion and SWI hypointensities (ischemic/hemorrhagic infarcts) plus leptomeningeal enhancement, consistent with contiguous invasive fungal spread

PMC7455415.diagnosis: Thymoma (anterior mediastinal thymic epithelial tumor) mistaken for ectopic parathyroid adenoma
rationale_summary: Anterior mediastinal 18-mm mass on CT with sestamibi uptake prompted concern for ectopic parathyroid adenoma
Frozen section showed epithelial sheets/nests with pseudoglandular structures and a patchy/peripheral lymphoid component-typical thymic epithelial tumor pattern rather than parathyroid tissue
Intraoperative PTH failed to fall after resection (149.3 at 10 min, rising to 259.5 at 30 min), arguing the removed mass was not the source of hyperparathyroidism

PMC8803407.diagnosis: Nocardia endophthalmitis with necrotizing scleral involvement
rationale_summary: Severe unilateral hypopyon uveitis with vitreous opacities (heterogeneous vitreous echogenicity) and very poor vision suggests endophthalmitis
Routine vitreous stains/cultures and broad bacterial/fungal/mycobacterial PCR were negative, suggesting an atypical organism
Marked worsening after starting high-dose systemic corticosteroids supports an uncontrolled infection rather than primary autoimmune uveitis
Scleral thinning/melting with uveal prolapse at intravitreal injection sites is consistent with infectious necrotizing scleritis seen with Nocardia/atypical infections

PMC6188590.diagnosis: Statin-associated immune-mediated necrotizing myopathy (anti-HMG-CoA reductase antibody-associated myopathy)
rationale_summary: Developed recurrent proximal myalgias/weakness with creatine kinase elevation while on two different statins (atorvastatin, then pravastatin)
CK continued to rise despite statin discontinuation, reaching 1400 U/L, suggesting more than simple toxic statin myalgia
Initial episode improved with corticosteroids with CK normalization, consistent with an immune-mediated process
Myositis panel was negative; anti-HMG-CoA reductase antibodies were not tested, leaving this statin-associated autoimmune myopathy as the best unifying diagnosis

PMC4840426.diagnosis: Vildagliptin-induced interstitial pneumonia (drug-induced interstitial lung disease/organizing pneumonia with acute alveolar injury)
rationale_summary: Respiratory symptoms began several days after starting vildagliptin and progressed over 2 weeks
CT showed extensive ground-glass opacities with irregular reticulation in peribronchovascular, basal-dominant distribution with restrictive PFTs
BAL showed marked lymphocytosis with eosinophils and negative microbiology, supporting noninfectious inflammatory/drug reaction
Biopsy demonstrated organizing pneumonia with acute alveolar injury pattern and eosinophilic/lymphoplasmacytic infiltration
Connective-tissue disease evaluation was negative, supporting a drug-induced cause

PMC3914239.diagnosis: Renovascular (secondary) hypertension from left renal artery stenosis in an ectopic intrathoracic kidney, with advanced hypertensive nephrosclerosis/renal failure
rationale_summary: Severe long-standing uncontrolled hypertension with hypertensive end-organ damage (grade III retinopathy, S4/loud A2, pulmonary edema symptoms)
Marked azotemia/renal failure (creatinine 900 µmol/L, urea 30 mmol/L) with proteinuria and small echogenic kidney with loss of corticomedullary differentiation
Doppler shows left renal artery peak velocity 195 cm/s consistent with renal artery stenosis, supporting renovascular hypertension
Left kidney described as intrathoracic/poorly visualized on ultrasound, consistent with ectopic intrathoracic kidney associated with renovascular abnormalities

PMC6787862.diagnosis: Primary adrenal insufficiency (Addison disease), likely autoimmune (polyglandular autoimmune syndrome type 2) presenting with adrenal crisis and severe hypotonic hypovolemic hyponatremia
rationale_summary: Severe hypotonic hyponatremia (Na 101, plasma osmolality 225) with hypovolemia/orthostasis and inappropriately high urine sodium (128) and urine osmolality (128) without diuretic use suggests mineralocorticoid deficiency
Symptoms/signs over 1 month (fatigue, poor appetite, weight loss, vague abdominal pain) plus recent skin tanning/hyperpigmentation support primary adrenal insufficiency
Mild hyperkalemia (K 5.2) and metabolic acidosis (CO2 16) are consistent with aldosterone deficiency
History of long-standing hypothyroidism increases likelihood of autoimmune adrenal insufficiency (autoimmune polyglandular syndrome)

PMC2958974.diagnosis: Sepsis-induced cardiomyopathy from postpartum Escherichia coli urosepsis (pyelonephritis)
rationale_summary: Developed acute pyelonephritis signs 12 hours postpartum with marked costovertebral tenderness, pyuria/hematuria, leukocytosis, and fever
Blood and urine cultures grew Escherichia coli, followed by rapid clinical deterioration with hypoxemic respiratory failure and bilateral pleural effusions

Echocardiogram showed new biventricular hypokinesis with reduced ejection fraction (35%) and normal chamber size/valves, consistent with acute reversible myocardial dysfunction in sepsis rather than dilated peripartum cardiomyopathy
Troponins were normal and CT angiography showed no pulmonary embolus, supporting non-ischemic, non-thromboembolic cause of cardiopulmonary failure

PMC8082195.diagnosis: Thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura (TTP) with central nervous system involvement (atypical PRES-like MRI pattern)
rationale_summary: Acute encephalopathy with transient neurologic symptoms and fever
Severe thrombocytopenia (7,000/mcL) with microangiopathic hemolytic anemia (drop in hemoglobin, elevated LDH, very low haptoglobin)
MRI shows multifocal T2/FLAIR hyperintense lesions with punctate microhemorrhages and no enhancement, compatible with TTP-related microvascular injury/PRES-like changes
CSF essentially normal and broad infectious PCR/serologies negative, arguing against viral encephalitis

PMC5015421.diagnosis: Right common peroneal (fibular) nerve compression neuropathy from thromboembolic deterrent stocking at the fibular neck
rationale_summary: New painless foot drop 3 weeks after surgery with no back/leg pain or swelling
Weak ankle dorsiflexion, eversion, toe/hallux extension with relative preservation of inversion and normal ankle jerk/plantar reflexes localizes to common peroneal nerve
Sensory loss over lateral lower leg and dorsum of foot including first webspace matches superficial/deep peroneal distribution with preserved plantar sensation
Well-demarcated indentation at proximal stocking rim at fibular neck (site of peroneal nerve) suggests external compression from the stocking

PMC7735781.diagnosis: Sterile marginal keratitis from blepharitis/meibomian gland dysfunction with secondary mild diffuse lamellar keratitis (DLK) after SMILE, likely exacerbated by CPAP-related ocular surface dryness
rationale_summary: Recent onset of dryness on awakening after starting CPAP therapy
Signs of blepharitis/MGD (inspissated Meibomian glands, posterior marginal telangiectasias) and superficial punctate keratopathy

Concurrent grade I DLK beneath the SMILE cap margin in the same area, suggesting secondary interface inflammation rather than infectious keratitis

PMC10098721.diagnosis: Non-accidental trauma (child physical abuse with repeated inflicted fractures/abusive head injury)
rationale_summary: Postmortem imaging and exam showed multiple injuries typical of inflicted trauma: numerous cranial vault fractures and metaphyseal fractures of the left humerus/radius/ulna.

Anthropology and histology demonstrated many (14) antemortem skull fractures with healing at varying stages (rounded margins, subperiosteal new bone, fibrous/cartilaginous bridging; multiple distinct histologic healing phases), indicating repeated injury over time.
Left upper extremity had multiple healed metaphyseal fractures and a remodeled diaphyseal ulna fracture with malalignment, supporting prior significant trauma.
External petechiae and scalp soft-tissue hemorrhages overlying skull fractures support traumatic injury rather than a single acute natural process.

PMC11442918.diagnosis: Intestinal tuberculosis (ileocecal/colonic TB) mimicking Crohn's disease
rationale_summary: Refractory to 5-ASA and high-dose prednisolone with subsequent high fever, severe anemia, and hypoalbuminemia

CT shows calcified mediastinal/hilar nodes and right middle-lobe atelectasis with calcification, plus diffuse intra-abdominal lymphadenopathy and ascites-suggestive of tuberculosis rather than Crohn's

Repeat endoscopy lacks Crohn's longitudinal ulcers/cobblestoning and instead shows circumferential transverse/round ulcers, patulous ileocecal valve with pseudodiverticular deformity, pseudopolyposis, and rectosigmoid sparing-features typical of intestinal TB
Origin from a tuberculosis-endemic area (Myanmar)

PMC8811182.diagnosis: Orbital siliconoma (foreign-body granuloma) at the left enucleation site with contralateral silicone implant migration into the oral cavity and subsequent vomiting
rationale_summary: Two months after bilateral enucleation with unshaped silicone orbital implants, the dog developed left orbital swelling and a draining tract at the incision midpoint.
A black spherical object consistent with a silicone implant was recovered from vomitus after premedication, indicating implant migration into the gastrointestinal/oral tract.
Despite vomiting an implant, a firm spherical structure was still palpable in the left orbit, suggesting the palpable mass was not an implant.

Surgical exploration of the left orbit revealed a firm pinkish-white 2-cm mass occupying most of the orbit rather than an implant, consistent with a foreign-body granulomatous reaction to silicone (siliconoma) causing wound breakdown/drainage.

PMC4898367.diagnosis: Primary sebaceous carcinoma of the parotid gland
rationale_summary: Parotid mass with cervical lymphadenopathy; resection shows malignant epithelial tumor
Histology shows lobules/nests of large foamy cells with vacuolated clear cytoplasm (sebaceous-type cells) surrounded by basaloid cells, with foci of squamous differentiation and keratin pearls
Foamy cells are PAS-negative (argues against glycogen-rich clear cell tumors)
Immunoprofile supports carcinoma (EMA and pancytokeratin positive) with p63 positivity and excludes common mimics (negative S100, CD10, vimentin, melan A, CD45, and CEA)

PMC8136328.diagnosis: Paraproteinemic keratopathy due to an underlying monoclonal gammopathy (e.g., MGUS/multiple myeloma)
rationale_summary: Progressive bilateral subepithelial/Bowman-layer and stromal corneal opacities with additional posterior stromal and granular endothelial deposits

AS-OCT shows hyperreflective band at Bowman/anterior stroma and posterior stromal hyperreflective deposits; IVCM shows epithelial, stromal, and endothelial hyperreflective deposits with preserved endothelial architecture
Genetic testing for TGFBI and COL17A1 negative, making inherited corneal dystrophies less likely and prompting concern for paraprotein-related corneal deposition

PMC8028090.diagnosis: Chemotherapy-induced eosinophilic pneumonia (drug-induced pneumonitis) from FOLFOX6 plus bevacizumab
rationale_summary: Recurrent high fevers temporally following each chemotherapy course, culminating in persistent respiratory symptoms after the fifth course
Peripheral eosinophilia during episodes (up to 736/µL) with elevated interstitial lung disease markers (SP-D 250 U/L, KL-6 538 U/mL)
CT shows bilateral nonsegmental, subpleural-predominant consolidations consistent with drug-induced pneumonitis/eosinophilic pneumonia pattern
Infectious workup negative (urine antigens) and no improvement/worsening despite 5 days of broad-spectrum piperacillin-tazobactam

PMC5632850.diagnosis: Transient perivascular inflammation of the carotid artery (TIPIC syndrome, formerly carotidynia)
rationale_summary: Acute unilateral pain localized over the left carotid bifurcation radiating to jaw/ear and worsened by palpation/movement, with normal neurologic exam
Imaging shows eccentric perivascular soft-tissue/wall thickening at the left carotid bifurcation with intense post-contrast enhancement and T2 hyperintensity
No evidence of carotid dissection/hematoma or aneurysm on Doppler/MRI (normal intraluminal flow; no intimal dissection; no T1 wall hematoma)

No systemic/other-vessel involvement (other supra-aortic vessels and aortic arch normal; abdominal MRI negative for vasculitis/fibromuscular dysplasia) and negative ANA/ANCA with minimal inflammatory markers

PMC7284054.diagnosis: Paraneoplastic Raynaud phenomenon/digital ischemia secondary to high-grade serous ovarian (primary peritoneal) carcinoma
rationale_summary: Acute-onset severe Raynaud-type symptoms with fingertip pain, paresthesia, color change progressing to digital ulcerations refractory to multiple vasodilator therapies
Systemic symptoms (malaise, fatigue, night sweats, alopecia, dry eyes) with autoimmune/inflammatory lab panel reportedly normal

CT showed peritoneal carcinomatosis with omental caking and markedly elevated CA-125 (862 U/mL) with normal CEA and CA19-9, supporting ovarian/primary peritoneal serous carcinoma presenting with a paraneoplastic vasospastic/ischemic syndrome

PMC10577163.diagnosis: Acute cerebellitis (post-COVID-19 vaccination) associated with SARS-CoV-2 detected in CSF
rationale_summary: Subacute onset of dysarthria, nystagmus, dysmetria, and severe truncal/appendicular ataxia localizing to the cerebellum
Brain MRI shows bilateral cerebellar hemispheric/middle cerebellar peduncle hyperintense lesions with faint enhancement, without spinal cord involvement
CSF shows lymphocytic pleocytosis and elevated protein with negative bacterial culture and negative PCR for other neurotropic viruses
CSF PCR positive for SARS-CoV-2 supports SARS-CoV-2-associated inflammatory cerebellar syndrome
Temporal association: symptoms began ~2 weeks after first Sinopharm COVID-19 vaccine dose

PMC9446685.diagnosis: Post-cardiac surgery constrictive pericarditis (transient constrictive pericarditis)
rationale_summary: 4 weeks after CABG/bioprosthetic AVR with new right-sided heart failure symptoms (oedema, orthopnoea/PND)
Markedly elevated JVP with Kussmaul's sign and dilated IVC without respiratory collapse
Echocardiographic constrictive physiology: septal bounce (ventricular interdependence) and exaggerated respiratory variation in mitral (>25%) and tricuspid (>40%) inflow
Bright, thickened pericardium with small pericardial effusion; preserved LV systolic function and no valve dysfunction to explain symptoms

PMC5953188.diagnosis: Congenital pulmonary airway malformation (CPAM/CCAM), type 1, of the right lung causing contralateral pulmonary hypoplasia
rationale_summary: Prenatal ultrasound showed a large cystic mass occupying the right hemithorax with reduced left thoracic volume
Autopsy revealed mediastinal shift to the left and a hyperinflated right lung with multiple large contiguous cysts (up to 7 cm) and an immature small left lung

Histology showed cysts formed by proliferating terminal respiratory structures lined by ciliated pseudostratified epithelium with mucosal projections, goblet cells and mucinous glands-features characteristic of CPAM type 1

PMC3574658.diagnosis: Pseudohypoparathyroidism type 1A (Albright hereditary osteodystrophy due to GNAS-related hormone resistance)
rationale_summary: Early-onset severe obesity with insatiable appetite and round facies suggest an AHO/pseudohypoparathyroidism phenotype
Persistently elevated PTH despite normal calcium and phosphate is consistent with evolving PTH resistance
Repeated elevated TSH with normal free T4 and confirmed mild primary hypothyroidism indicates TSH resistance, which commonly co-occurs with PTH resistance in pseudohypoparathyroidism type 1A

PMC4405311.diagnosis: Cardiac tamponade from massive pericardial effusion due to severe hypothyroidism
rationale_summary: Clinical tamponade: hypotension (60/30 mmHg), raised JVP (11 cm H2O), very distant heart sounds, impalpable apex beat, low pulse volume and pulsus paradoxus with severe dyspnea
Imaging and exam support large effusions: enlarged cardiac silhouette plus bilateral pleural effusions; ascites and hepatomegaly suggest systemic serous effusions

Pleural fluid was a clear transudate and Mantoux test was negative, arguing against tuberculous or inflammatory exudative causes and fitting hypothyroid-related serous effusions with anemia (Hb 7.6 g/dL)

PMC4158173.diagnosis: Spontaneous rupture of the left main pulmonary artery causing a large anterior mediastinal hematoma with extrapericardial compression of the left atrium (tamponade physiology)
rationale_summary: Sudden central chest and thoracic back pain with profound hypotension and tachycardia
CTA showed a 7.3×6.8 cm left anterior mediastinal hematoma with active contrast extravasation from the left main pulmonary artery
No aortic aneurysm or dissection on CTA, arguing against acute aortic syndrome
Echocardiography showed an extracardiac mass compressing the left atrium, consistent with hemodynamic collapse from mediastinal bleeding/compression

PMC9857465.diagnosis: Erdheim-Chester disease (non-Langerhans cell histiocytosis) with secondary sclerosing cholangitis
rationale_summary: Marked cholestatic liver enzymes with multifocal intrahepatic biliary dilation/strictures and later a common hepatic duct stricture mimicking PSC, with negative autoimmune/viral workup and normal IgG4
Systemic findings characteristic of Erdheim-Chester disease: bilateral perinephric fat stranding, diffuse large-vessel inflammation, and diffuse symmetric long-bone sclerosis
Late development of dome-shaped cutaneous lesions consistent with systemic histiocytic infiltration
Biliary cytology/biopsy benign reactive tissue, arguing against cholangiocarcinoma as the cause of progressive obstructive jaundice

PMC7924147.diagnosis: Disseminated histoplasmosis with bilateral adrenal involvement causing primary adrenal insufficiency
rationale_summary: Advanced untreated HIV (CD4 27) with systemic symptoms (weight loss, cough), lymphadenopathy, rash, and oral/maxillary mass
Biopsy shows granulomatous tissue with macrophages containing well-demarcated fungal spores consistent with intracellular Histoplasma infection

PMC11057788.diagnosis: Atrial standstill (severe atrial mechanical dysfunction/atrial myopathy) after orthotopic heart transplantation causing restrictive right-heart physiology
rationale_summary: Mitral inflow Doppler showed a large E wave with absent A wave despite sinus rhythm, indicating loss of atrial contraction

TR was only moderate on TEE/ICE, insufficient to explain severe refractory right heart failure

PMC4647021.diagnosis: Posterior elbow impingement due to hypertrophied posterior fat pad (posterior fat pad impingement syndrome)
rationale_summary: Hyperextension axial-load injury followed by persistent posterior elbow pain and mechanical "catching" with motion
Pain, tenderness, and palpable crepitus specifically at full extension; mild extension loss
MRI shows hypertrophied posterior fat pad interposed between olecranon and olecranon fossa, interpreted as posterior impingement
No ulnar or lateral ulnar collateral ligament instability to explain symptoms
Limited and transient response to anti-inflammatory medication and intraarticular anesthetic/steroid injection supports an impingement source

PMC10618618.diagnosis: Morpheaform scleromyxedema associated with IgG kappa monoclonal gammopathy (smoldering myeloma)
rationale_summary: Longstanding IgG kappa monoclonal gammopathy with recurrent indurated dusky plaques
Skin biopsies show dermal sclerosis extending into subcutis with abundant interstitial mucin (colloidal iron positive)
Diminished/absent CD34+ dermal dendrocytes in sclerotic zones
Prior complete lesion resolution with lenalidomide (a therapy used for monoclonal gammopathy-associated scleromyxedema)
No systemic mucinosis on imaging, supporting a morpheaform/atypical cutaneous presentation rather than generalized systemic disease

PMC4356029.diagnosis: Smoking-related interstitial fibrosis (SRIF) associated with emphysema
rationale_summary: 45-pack-year active smoker with mild upper-lobe emphysema on HRCT
Progressive interstitial changes and elevated KL-6 with largely preserved spirometry and DLCO
HRCT showed lower-lobe ground-glass opacities and reticulation without honeycombing (NSIP-like pattern)

Surgical lung biopsy showed temporally homogeneous irregular interstitial fibrosis with preserved architecture, no honeycombing, coexisting centrilobular emphysema, and focal intraluminal macrophage accumulation-features typical of smoking-related fibrosis rather than IPF/UIP

PMC9464104.diagnosis: Vitamin B12 deficiency-associated pseudo-thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura (pseudo-TMA)
rationale_summary: Severe macrocytic anemia with pancytopenia (Hgb 5.4 g/dL, MCV 116 fL, WBC 3.5k/µL, platelets 28k/µL).
Hemolysis/TMA features (schistocytes, very high LDH 1647 IU/L, low haptoglobin) with normal coagulation studies.
ADAMTS13 activity normal (85%) and no clinical/laboratory response after 2 days of plasmapheresis, arguing against TTP.
No splenomegaly/neoplasm on CT and no PNH clone on flow cytometry, supporting an alternative cause of schistocytes/hemolysis such as B12 deficiency.

PMC4592892.diagnosis: Symptomatic left ureterolithiasis (nephrolithiasis in pregnancy) causing intermittent obstruction
rationale_summary: Recurrent acute, colicky left flank pain with nausea/vomiting
Remote history of urolithiasis
Infection less likely: afebrile, stable WBC count, urine culture no growth, no nitrites

PMC8853404.diagnosis: COVID-19-associated mononeuritis multiplex (acute bilateral common peroneal and tibial mononeuropathies)
rationale_summary: New focal lower-limb weakness after recovery from critical PCR-confirmed COVID-19 (marked left foot dorsiflexion weakness) with areflexia but otherwise normal neurologic exam, favoring peripheral nerve localization

NCS/EMG shows asymmetric, focal peripheral nerve involvement: absent left superficial peroneal sensory response with normal sural responses; peroneal and tibial motor abnormalities with absent F waves/H reflex; active denervation and reduced recruitment in peroneal-innervated muscles
Pattern is of multiple mononeuropathies at noncompressible sites rather than radiculopathy or generalized polyneuropathy/Guillain-Barré (upper extremities normal, sural sensory preserved)

PMC6389478.diagnosis: Left pyriform sinus fistula (third/fourth branchial pouch anomaly) causing recurrent cervical abscesses
rationale_summary: Recurrent left-sided cervical abscesses beginning in childhood with multiple failed excisions/imaging for a presumed second branchial cleft cyst
Episodes almost invariably follow upper respiratory tract infections, suggesting an internal aerodigestive tract communication

rationale_summary: Hypoattenuating right carotid space mass with slight homogeneous enhancement and infiltrative spread within the carotid sheath to the jugular foramen
Marked restricted diffusion with very low ADC (458×10⁻⁶ mm²/s) consistent with a hypercellular malignancy such as lymphoma

Lacks features typical of paraganglioma or schwannoma (no avid hypervascular enhancement/flow voids; not a well-circumscribed nerve sheath tumor) and no cervical lymphadenopathy despite a large infiltrative lesion

PMC7968274.diagnosis: Intraluminal meningioma of the right sigmoid sinus causing venous outflow obstruction with secondary intracranial hypertension (pseudotumor cerebri)
rationale_summary: Bilateral papilledema with mildly abnormal visual fields and markedly elevated CSF opening pressure (500 mmH2O) with normal CSF composition
No intracranial mass lesion or hydrocephalus on noncontrast CT to otherwise explain raised intracranial pressure
MRI shows a 2.5 cm right sigmoid sinus lesion that is isointense on T1/T2 with homogeneous gadolinium enhancement, typical of meningioma
CT venography demonstrates subtotal occlusion of the dominant right sigmoid sinus by a well-defined intraluminal mass, consistent with venous sinus obstruction leading to intracranial hypertension

PMC10860510.diagnosis: Idiopathic myointimal hyperplasia of mesenteric veins (IMHMV) causing ischemic colitis of the rectosigmoid/left colon
rationale_summary: 44-year-old man with subacute obstructive symptoms and sigmoid mucosal edema/stenosis on colonoscopy, with biopsy showing only mild nonspecific inflammation (not IBD/eosinophilic enteritis)

Contrast CT showed long-segment left-sided colonic wall thickening (splenic flexure to rectum) with a thin, cord-like inferior mesenteric vein and ectatic mesenteric venous branches while the inferior mesenteric artery was patent and there was no arterial/venous thrombus or atherosclerosis-pattern suggesting nonthrombotic mesenteric venous disease
Clinical deterioration to marked inflammatory response and abdominal tenderness is consistent with progression of ischemic colitis from impaired mesenteric venous outflow

PMC11101242.diagnosis: Delusional infestation (somatic-type delusional disorder; "Morgellons" presentation) involving the eyes
rationale_summary: 15-year fixed belief of persistent ocular "foreign bodies" despite repeated normal eyelid eversion/sweeping and normal orbital radiograph and MRI
Brings collected self-extracted mixed debris (plant parts, fibers, hair, thread, metal filaments, insects) consistent with the "specimen sign"
Ocular exams show only mild nonspecific surface findings (trace injection, punctate staining) without any retained foreign body, lesion, or ulcer to explain symptoms
Chronic self-directed removal rituals (sink filling/draining, mascara brush) and persistent symptoms in the absence of objective pathology

PMC2892661.diagnosis: Cervical spondylotic myelopathy with cervical radiculopathy (degenerative cervical spinal cord/nerve-root compression) mimicking ALS
rationale_summary: Progressive upper-limb atrophy, fasciculations, and weakness with very brisk reflexes initially suggested motor neuron disease

Reevaluation prompted by clear sensory findings (reduced vibration in all limbs; hyper/dysesthesia over shoulders/proximal arms) and persistent neck pain/numbness/clumsiness, which are atypical for ALS
Gait ataxia with symmetric hyperreflexia supports cervical cord involvement; proximal arm atrophy and shoulder/arm pain fit concomitant cervical root compression

PMC3638553.diagnosis: Chronic inflammatory demyelinating polyneuropathy (CIDP) with neuromuscular respiratory failure
rationale_summary: Three months of progressive symmetric proximal and distal limb weakness with hyporeflexia
Distal sensory loss (decreased pinprick) supporting a peripheral neuropathy rather than primary cardiopulmonary disease
Chronic hypercapnic respiratory insufficiency (pCO2 58) with clear lungs and no heart failure on BNP/echocardiogram, consistent with neuromuscular ventilatory failure
No improvement with diuretics or with correction of hyperglycemia, arguing against CHF or metabolic decompensation as the cause

PMC9005318.diagnosis: Acute severe aortic regurgitation (paravalvular leak) following transcatheter aortic valve replacement
rationale_summary: Developed sudden progressive dyspnea/orthopnea 3 days after TAVR
Wide pulse pressure and a new grade 3 diastolic decrescendo murmur at the left upper sternal border suggest significant aortic regurgitation
Warm extremities with pulmonary congestion/pleural effusion and markedly elevated pro-BNP indicate acute decompensated heart failure
CT excluded pulmonary embolism, dissection, and pericardial effusion, supporting a valvular cause of acute heart failure

PMC6864292.diagnosis: Idiopathic hypereosinophilic syndrome (HES) with eosinophil-associated thrombotic/necrotic skin involvement
rationale_summary: Marked persistent hypereosinophilia (21.15×10^9/L; 57.8%) with bone marrow eosinophilia (27.5%)
End-organ involvement: rapidly progressive large cutaneous necrosis with biopsy showing dermal necrosis/hemorrhage and eosinophil-rich perivascular infiltrate; pulmonary opacities with hemoptysis
Extensive evaluation for allergic, parasitic, rheumatologic, and immunologic causes was unremarkable, supporting idiopathic HES
Markedly elevated D-dimer with thrombocytopenia suggests eosinophil-related thrombotic/coagulopathic complication contributing to necrosis

PMC9449604.diagnosis: Pembrolizumab-induced immune checkpoint inhibitor-related pancreatitis (mass-forming, autoimmune pancreatitis-like)
rationale_summary: History of prolonged pembrolizumab therapy with pancreatic abnormalities appearing 3 months after discontinuation

Imaging shows focal pancreatic head enlargement with delayed enhancement, diffusion restriction, and multiple main pancreatic duct stenoses plus intrapancreatic bile duct stenosis-features compatible with inflammatory/autoimmune-like pancreatitis
No clinical/laboratory evidence of acute pancreatitis (normal amylase/lipase/CRP) and serum IgG4 is normal, arguing against classic IgG4-related autoimmune pancreatitis
Negative pancreatic juice, bile, and brushing cytology makes pancreatic/biliary malignancy less likely, supporting an ICI-related inflammatory process

PMC1971059.diagnosis: Primary skeletal muscle carcinoid tumor (well-differentiated neuroendocrine tumor) of the sartorius muscle
rationale_summary: Slowly enlarging firm intramuscular thigh mass with no other lesions on CT, MIBG, or octreotide scans, supporting a primary soft-tissue site
Histology shows nested/packeted polygonal cells separated by fine fibrovascular stroma, consistent with a neuroendocrine tumor pattern

Immunoprofile strongly positive for synaptophysin, chromogranin, neuron-specific enolase, and cytokeratin, with negative S100 and desmin, supporting neuroendocrine epithelial differentiation rather than melanoma/schwannoma or myogenic tumor
Electron microscopy demonstrates abundant neurosecretory granules, confirming neuroendocrine differentiation
Elevated 24-hour urinary 5-HIAA supports a serotonin-producing carcinoid/neuroendocrine tumor despite absent carcinoid symptoms

PMC4356045.diagnosis: Unilateral giant bullous emphysema (vanishing lung syndrome) of the left lung
rationale_summary: Chest radiograph showed marked hyperlucency of the upper left hemithorax and CT demonstrated several bullae occupying almost the entire left hemithorax with a few small cysts

Physiology showed marked gas trapping (RV 156% predicted) and large discrepancy between TLC by nitrogen washout (56% predicted) and plethysmography (88% predicted), consistent with poorly ventilated bullous spaces

PMC11440268.diagnosis: Metastatic parathyroid carcinoma with pulmonary metastases causing recurrent severe hyperparathyroidism
rationale_summary: Markedly elevated PTH (1891 pg/mL) with mildly high calcium in a patient previously treated with parathyroidectomy suggests recurrent autonomous parathyroid hormone production
Multiple bilateral well-defined lung nodules with dominant 99mTc-MIBI uptake indicate functioning parathyroid tissue within pulmonary lesions
Neck ultrasound/CT did not localize parathyroid tissue, supporting an ectopic/metastatic source rather than a cervical parathyroid remnant
Normal thyroid tests and thyroglobulin argue against hyperfunctioning/metastatic thyroid disease as the cause of MIBI-avid lung nodules

PMC6120420.diagnosis: Pembrolizumab (immune checkpoint inhibitor)-associated myocarditis causing cardiogenic shock with secondary acute kidney injury
rationale_summary: On pembrolizumab for metastatic lung adenocarcinoma
Profound hypotension (SBP 50-70 mm Hg) with elevated troponin (2.8 ng/mL)
New global systolic dysfunction (EF 55-60% to 35%) without regional wall-motion abnormalities, arguing against focal ischemia
Mild creatine phosphokinase elevation and severe AKI with FeUrea 46% and bland urinalysis compatible with intrinsic injury from shock rather than obstruction or glomerulonephritis

PMC11826251.diagnosis: Desmoid-type fibromatosis of the breast (breast fibromatosis)
rationale_summary: Slowly enlarging, firm ill-defined irregular breast mass fixed to deep tissues with no axillary lymphadenopathy
Imaging mimics carcinoma: high-density indistinct mass on mammography, heterogeneous hypoechoic solid mass with indistinct borders (BI-RADS 4C), spiculated enhancing mass on MRI
MRI kinetics show rapid initial uptake with persistent (type I) enhancement pattern, supporting a locally aggressive benign fibrous lesion rather than typical washout seen in many invasive carcinomas

PMC11526588.diagnosis: Richter transformation of chronic lymphocytic leukemia to diffuse large B-cell lymphoma
rationale_summary: Long-standing Rai stage 1 CLL now with rapid clinical deterioration, B symptoms (intermittent fevers, weight loss), and progressive bulky lymphadenopathy

PET/CT shows intensely FDG-avid nodal and extranodal disease with very high SUVs (up to ~20), including a nasopharyngeal mass causing airway compromise-features concerning for aggressive lymphoma arising from CLL rather than indolent CLL progression
Core biopsy was pursued specifically due to high suspicion for Richter transformation in this clinical context

PMC6454123.diagnosis: Incomplete Kawasaki disease (Kawasaki disease presenting with targetoid/bullous rash)
rationale_summary: Child with high fever, elevated ESR (95 mm/hr), bilateral nonexudative conjunctival injection, and strawberry tongue mucositis

Generalized targetoid/dusky macules and patches without desquamation; biopsy shows intraepidermal cleft with subtle interface change, supporting a Kawasaki-associated eruption rather than infection (negative Mycoplasma/HSV/viral panel)
Echocardiogram without coronary abnormalities can occur early in Kawasaki disease

PMC2829597.diagnosis: Reactive infectious mucocutaneous eruption (Mycoplasma pneumoniae-associated mucositis)
rationale_summary: Preceded by flu-like illness with sore throat followed by prominent painful mucosal/perineal ulceration, mild conjunctival injection, and oral involvement with only limited skin rash (face/neck).
Concurrent nonproductive cough and subsequent hypoxemic respiratory failure with minimal radiographic consolidation fits atypical pneumonia associated with mucositis.

Broad infectious/autoimmune workup (HIV PCR, ASO, complements, autoimmune screen) was negative and symptoms resolved over days with supportive care/steroids, consistent with a self-limited postinfectious mucocutaneous syndrome.

PMC9463427.diagnosis: Primary synovial sarcoma of the right axilla
rationale_summary: Right axillary mass showed epithelial-like cells in a mesenchymal background on FNA (class III), compatible with a biphasic-type soft-tissue sarcoma pattern.
TTF-1 was negative in the axillary lesion, arguing against metastatic thyroid carcinoma despite coexisting papillary thyroid carcinoma and elevated thyroglobulin.
Breast ultrasonography was normal and there were no surrounding enlarged axillary nodes, supporting a primary soft-tissue tumor rather than nodal metastasis or occult breast cancer.
Imaging showed a well-defined lobulated axillary soft-tissue mass with FDG avidity (SUVmax 5.1) and poor contrast enhancement, consistent with a malignant soft-tissue neoplasm.

PMC7374193.diagnosis: Bilateral optic nerve head hemangioblastomas (retinal capillary hemangioblastomas, von Hippel-Lindau-type lesion)
rationale_summary: Longstanding headaches with documented bilateral optic nerve head swelling and suspected optic disc masses starting in adolescence
Progressive, bilateral optic nerve head lesions extending into the papillomacular bundles with severe, progressive vision loss
Lesions treated for years with intravitreal anti-VEGF and steroid implants with limited response, consistent with exudative vascular tumors at the disc
Targeted genetic testing for von Hippel-Lindau and other phakomatoses was negative, supporting a sporadic/VHL-mosaic-type presentation rather than classic inherited disease

PMC8544693.diagnosis: Multisystem inflammatory syndrome in adults (MIS-A) following SARS-CoV-2 infection
rationale_summary: Acute febrile illness occurring shortly after RT-PCR-confirmed SARS-CoV-2 infection (after 14-day isolation, previously asymptomatic)
Shock with hypotension, tachycardia, cool extremities and poor perfusion
Prominent extrapulmonary involvement: rapidly desquamating maculopapular rash with annular plaques, severe fatigue, vomiting and diarrhea
Marked systemic inflammation with leukocytosis and markedly elevated inflammatory markers

Severe cardiac involvement: acute pulmonary edema during resuscitation and echocardiographic global LV hypokinesia with EF <30%, four-chamber dilation, valvular regurgitation, and pericardial effusion, with no respiratory disease (normal oxygenation, clear lungs)

PMC3655491.diagnosis: Recurrent Guillain-Barré syndrome (predominantly motor axonal variant/AMAN)
rationale_summary: Prior severe Guillain-Barré syndrome at age 12 with recovery, now new acute relapse after recent upper respiratory infection
Acute/subacute progression of limb pain, weakness, and bulbar symptoms with reduced negative inspiratory force
Neuro exam: motor weakness with hyporeflexia/areflexia and intact sensation
MRI: anterior (motor) nerve-root enhancement
CSF: elevated protein (albuminocytologic dissociation pattern, though with lymphocytic pleocytosis)
EMG/NCS: predominantly motor polyneuropathy with axonal>mild demyelinating changes and preserved sensory responses, consistent with motor axonal GBS variant

PMC8462061.diagnosis: Insulin autoimmune syndrome (Hirata disease) causing hyperinsulinemic hypoglycemia in the setting of evolving autoimmune type 1 diabetes
rationale_summary: Recurrent fasting hypoglycemia with inappropriately high insulin (158.35 pmol/L) and C-peptide (1.09 nmol/L) plus suppressed beta-hydroxybutyrate indicates endogenous hyperinsulinism
Sulfonylurea screen was negative, arguing against exogenous secretagogue exposure
Marked postprandial/reactive hyperglycemia alongside prolonged fasting hypoglycemia on CGM suggests insulin dysregulation consistent with insulin-binding antibodies
Insulin autoantibody (and multiple other islet autoantibodies) were positive, supporting an autoimmune mechanism rather than a structural pancreatic lesion; abdominal ultrasound was unremarkable

PMC9650838.diagnosis: Recurrent bacterial meningitis secondary to idiopathic intracranial hypertension-related cerebrospinal fluid leak
rationale_summary: History of multiple hospitalizations over 10 years for similar meningitis-like episodes with chronic headaches

CSF showed marked neutrophilic pleocytosis, very high protein, and low glucose consistent with bacterial meningitis despite initial negative cultures/mNGS; later relapse had CSF culture growing hemolytic streptococcus

MRI demonstrated multiple signs of chronically raised intracranial pressure (partially empty sella, distended optic nerve sheaths, optic papilla flattening, narrowed ventricles) supporting idiopathic intracranial hypertension as an underlying driver predisposing to recurrent infection (via intermittent CSF leak)

PMC6914944.diagnosis: Diclofenac-induced perioperative anaphylaxis (anaphylactic shock with bronchospasm/angioedema)
rationale_summary: Abrupt onset 1-2 minutes after starting IV diclofenac
Severe hypotension and tachycardia with hypoxemia and low EtCO₂ consistent with anaphylactic shock
Bronchospasm signs: bilateral rhonchi, increased peak/plateau airway pressures, copious secretions
Angioedema (lip/periorbital/facial edema) despite absence of urticaria
Improvement after stopping diclofenac and administering adrenaline, oxygen, fluids, steroids, and antihistamine

PMC4960400.diagnosis: Progressive pseudorheumatoid dysplasia (spondyloepiphyseal dysplasia tarda with progressive arthropathy)
rationale_summary: Longstanding childhood-onset large and small joint symptoms misdiagnosed as JIA/RA with early severe degenerative disease (bilateral total hip replacement at 19)
Noninflammatory evaluation: normal ESR/CRP and negative RF, anti-CCP, and ANA despite extensive joint involvement

Imaging shows skeletal dysplasia/degenerative pattern: platyspondyly with biconcave vertebrae, widened phalangeal epiphyses, and marked osteoarthritic changes with osteophytes/loose bodies rather than erosive inflammatory arthritis
Bony swelling of MCP/PIP/DIP with extensive osteoarthritis fits progressive pseudorheumatoid dysplasia

PMC7901359.diagnosis: Cerebral salt wasting syndrome (CSW) secondary to recent traumatic brain injury
rationale_summary: Severe hypotonic hyponatremia (Na 118 meq/L, serum osmolality 226 mosm/kg)
Inappropriately concentrated urine with high urine sodium (Uosm 518 mosm/kg, UNa 83 meq/L) despite hyponatremia
Clinical hypovolemia: hypotension, dry mucous membranes, reduced skin turgor, marked orthostatic hypotension with tachycardia and dizziness
Low serum uric acid (2.6 mg/dL), supportive of renal salt wasting pattern
Temporal association with recent head trauma and intracranial findings (frontal hygroma)

PMC7904273.diagnosis: Celiac disease with associated aplastic anemia (immune-mediated marrow failure)

EGD shows duodenal mucosal congestion/edema with otherwise normal ileum/colon, pointing to a proximal small-intestinal process consistent with celiac disease (tTG IgA and duodenal biopsy pending)

PMC11264972.diagnosis: Azathioprine-induced hypersensitivity syndrome with acute myocarditis causing recurrent distributive shock
rationale_summary: Systemic inflammatory reaction began shortly after starting azathioprine (fever, abdominal pain, hypotension progressing to shock and acute renal failure) with no infectious source on cultures/imaging
Developed acute erythematous pustular rash during the febrile shock illness
Re-challenge with a single azathioprine dose triggered rapid recurrence of vasodilatory shock within 12 hours, strongly implicating azathioprine
Marked troponin elevation with transient ST-segment elevations but normal coronary angiography supports drug-related myocarditis rather than obstructive MI

PMC7424302.diagnosis: Transient acute coronary syndrome from septal perforator (LAD septal branch) injury/occlusion during left bundle branch pacing lead screwing
rationale_summary: After deep septal lead advancement, patient developed sudden chest pain, diaphoresis, hypotension with anterior-lateral ST elevation (I, aVL, V1-V3) and reciprocal inferior depression
Coronary angiography showed a large septal branch at the original lead site (an anatomic substrate for direct injury/compression) with preserved LAD TIMI 3 flow
Immediate lead withdrawal/repositioning led to resolution of symptoms and ST changes within 10 minutes, consistent with transient septal branch compromise rather than fixed epicardial LAD occlusion

PMC11524424.diagnosis: Myxedema coma (severe decompensated hypothyroidism) causing pancytopenia
rationale_summary: Known congenital hypothyroidism with months of levothyroxine nonadherence and severe malnutrition/weight loss
Presentation with altered mental status/unconsciousness, hypothermia (33.1 °C), hypotension, and borderline bradycardia
Thyroid labs show profound hypothyroidism (TSH 71 μIU/mL with very low free T4 0.23 ng/dL and low free T3)
Associated hyponatremia and pancytopenia with unremarkable smear and no evidence of hemolysis or DIC
No clear alternative cause identified (normal cortisol; negative toxicology/COVID; imaging without infection or malignancy; HLH workup not supportive despite high ferritin)

PMC6169071.diagnosis: Pseudo-thrombotic microangiopathy from severe vitamin B12 (cobalamin) deficiency
rationale_summary: Microangiopathic hemolysis: schistocytes (10%), very high LDH, undetectable haptoglobin, Coombs negative, normal coagulation
Cytopenias beyond hemolysis: persistent neutropenia and later thrombocytopenia, with very low reticulocyte production index (ineffective erythropoiesis)
Neurologic symptoms (finger paresthesia) consistent with cobalamin deficiency
MCV not elevated but RDW markedly high, compatible with evolving/combined anemia rather than classic TTP alone

PMC10191487.diagnosis: Congenital hepatic fibrosis with portal hypertension causing hypersplenism
rationale_summary: Persistent hepatosplenomegaly from 6-15 months with largely normal liver enzymes (only mildly elevated AST) suggests a primarily portal hypertensive process rather than hepatocellular injury
Intermittent pancytopenia with residual thrombocytopenia fits hypersplenism from splenomegaly/portal hypertension
Ultrasound showing heterogeneous, hyperechoic liver parenchyma and elevated hepatic arterial resistive index supports underlying hepatic fibrosis/cirrhotic-appearing changes
Bone marrow biopsy showed trilineage hematopoiesis without malignancy/infiltration and viral PCRs were negative, making leukemia/viral suppression unlikely
Targeted testing for common lysosomal storage diseases was negative, supporting a non-lysosomal hepatic cause of hepatosplenomegaly

PMC11256461.diagnosis: Drug reaction with eosinophilia and systemic symptoms (DRESS) syndrome due to sulfasalazine
rationale_summary: Symptoms began ~3-4 weeks after starting sulfasalazine and progressed despite stopping it
Confluent morbilliform rash with fever, malaise, facial/pharyngeal swelling, and tender lymphadenopathy
Systemic involvement: hepatitis (ALT 224, AST 78, INR 1.4, hypoalbuminemia), serositis/effusions (pericardial, pleural), hepatosplenomegaly and ascites
Hematologic abnormalities with leukocytosis and eosinophilia (up to 11%) and markedly elevated ferritin
Infectious causes and other exposures were investigated and largely excluded; he met all seven RegiSCAR criteria for DRESS

PMC10141456.diagnosis: Eosinophilic myocarditis (acute necrotizing eosinophilic myocardial injury related to marked eosinophilia in AML)
rationale_summary: Marked hypereosinophilia (up to 33.3×10^9/L) concurrent with chest pain and large troponin rise
New severe global LV systolic dysfunction (EF 32%) shortly after presentation/chemotherapy with no prior cardiac disease
Stress CMR showed extensive subendocardial hypoperfusion in all coronary territories, mimicking multivessel ischemia
Coronary angiography showed no atherosclerotic coronary disease, arguing against triple-vessel CAD as the cause
Persistent troponin elevation and LV dysfunction despite standard heart-failure/anti-ischemic therapy support ongoing inflammatory/toxic myocardial injury rather than obstructive CAD

PMC7441664.diagnosis: Hirayama disease (juvenile muscular atrophy of the distal upper limb / monomelic amyotrophy)
rationale_summary: Young man with insidious onset distal upper-limb weakness and marked hand muscle wasting (first dorsal interosseous) with sparing of brachioradialis
Pure motor involvement (no sensory, autonomic, bowel/bladder symptoms) with lower-motor-neuron signs (atrophy, reduced tone, fasciculations, fibrillations, minipolymyoclonus)
Electrodiagnostics show motor axonal loss and chronic denervation in C7-T1-innervated muscles, predominantly affecting one upper limb

PMC9566503.diagnosis: Right ventricular (right ventricular outflow tract) myxoma
rationale_summary: Adolescent with new systolic murmur, dyspnea/chest pain, elevated NT-proBNP and signs of right-sided heart strain (RA/RV dilation, mild hepatomegaly)

Echocardiography shows a large (3.5×4.0 cm), rounded lobulated, well-circumscribed, inhomogeneous, pedunculated mass attached to the infundibular septum causing severe RV outflow obstruction (peak gradient 80 mmHg)

CT confirms a heterogeneously enhancing soft-tissue mass occupying the RVOT without pulmonary embolism, consistent with an intracardiac tumor rather than thromboembolism or infection (no fever/CRP elevation, no risk factors)

PMC5184841.diagnosis: Early repolarization (J‑wave) syndrome causing idiopathic ventricular fibrillation
rationale_summary: 12‑lead ECG showed widespread J waves with normal QT and no structural heart disease on echo/CMR
Telemetry documented frequent monomorphic PVCs degenerating into polymorphic VT and ventricular fibrillation requiring repeated defibrillation
Arrhythmia burden and J‑wave amplitude decreased with magnesium/potassium and labetalol, consistent with a J‑wave-mediated VF storm rather than long‑QT or cardiomyopathy
